races.race_name: Irish EBF Median Sires Series Maiden (IRE Incentive Race)

runners.name: Ischgl
runners.spotlight: Belied odds of 100-1 to chase home a Ballydoyle hotpot here on debut (6f, soft); however, he was beaten 6l and his dam was best on testing ground, so he might be vulnerable to one of the newcomers.
runners.comment: Fine run at massive odds here on debut, quicker ground an unknown, could be vulnerable

runners.name: High King
runners.spotlight: Calyx colt; price rose from E55,000 as a foal to 150,000gns as a yearling; closely related to 7f 2yo winner Look Around (inc Listed; RPR 105), half-brother to four winners inc Sara Lucille (French Group-placed 5f inc 2yo; 100); dam 2yo Group 3 winner; yard won this in 2023.
runners.comment: Calyx colt, dam 2yo Group 3 winner, likely looking type on paper, yard won this in 2023
runners.owner: Barry Fowler & John C Oxley & Justin Edward Casse

runners.name: Belle Of The Ball
runners.spotlight: Mohaather filly; E55,000 foal, E45,000 yearling; half-sister to winners Emiyn (1m-2m2f; RPR 97), Edelak (1m4f; 91) and Edzel (French 1m4f); dam 1m1f and 1m4f (Group 3) winner; may need further in time but yard amongst winners; market can guide.
runners.comment: Mohaather filly, dam 1m1f and 1m4f winner, yard in form but may need further in time
runners.owner: OMEGA Racing & Belle Morris & Russell B Jones Jr

runners.name: Lan De Ghaire
runners.spotlight: Home-bred Dawn Approach filly; dam 2yo C&D winner (RPR 88), out of unraced half-sister to Group-placed 1m-10.6f winner Theobald; first 2yo runner of the season for yard; market can reveal expectations.
runners.comment: Dawn Approach filly, dam a 2yo C&D winner, market can reveal expectations

runners.name: Golden Arizona
runners.spotlight: Arizona colt; first foal; unraced dam out of 1m 2yo/1m4f winning half-sister to 8.6f winner Gallope; respected source but others have more appealing profiles.
runners.comment: Arizona colt, dam unraced, respected source but others preferred

runners.name: Best Love
runners.spotlight: Kodi Bear filly; 17,000gns yearling; dam French 1m AW winner, half-sister to 6f 2yo winner Art Lover, out of French 7.5f Listed winning half-sister to 1m2f Listed winner Bocaiuva; likely best watched on debut unless the market speaks.
runners.comment: Kodi Bear filly; dam French 1m AW winner, likely best watched unless market speaks

runners.name: Star Glory
runners.spotlight: Cotai Glory filly; E35,000 yearling; closely related to 7f winner Kings Time (RPR 94), half-sister to Hungarian 1m/1m2f winner Mirash King (inc 2yo); dam 7.4f 2yo winner (86); represents in-form yard; market check advised.
runners.comment: Cotai Glory filly, dam 7.4f 2yo winner, yard in form, check the market

runners.name: High Morals
runners.spotlight: Colt by Highly Motivated; dam unraced half-sister to winners What A Question (6f 2yo), Arranmore (6f-1m3f inc 2yo) and Blue Pinatubo (6f 2yo), out of US 1m3f Grade 3 winner; appeals on paper and yard in form; worth a market check on debut.
runners.comment: Highly Motivated colt, dam related to multiple 2yo winners, yard in form, check market

runners.name: Otherworldly
runners.spotlight: Persian King colt; half-brother to winners Ghaly (1m; RPR 113) and Haasim (UAE Listed-placed 6f; 95); dam Group-placed 1m winner (100); looks yard's second string on rider arrangements.
runners.comment: Persian King colt, dam group placed 1m winner, overlooked by McMonagle
runners.owner: Justin Edward Casse & Mrs A M O'Brien

runners.name: Belicoso
runners.spotlight: Churchill colt; dam French Listed-placed 1m AW winner (RPR 93), half-sister to winners Texas Fever (US 8.5f 2yo Grade 3) and Bold Angel (US 1m stakes), out of unraced close relative of Kentucky Derby winner Fusaichi Pegasus; yard won this last year; preferred by McMonagle.
runners.comment: Churchill colt, dam Listed placed 1m AW winner, yard won this last year, go well

runners.name: Immortal Guard
runners.spotlight: Persian Force colt; E43,000 yearling and resold as a yearling for E100,000; dam 6f winner (RPR 74), half-sister to useful winners Taexali (5f 2yo) and Caliburn (7.7f-1m4f), out of 7f Listed winner; one of two for yard whose juveniles are hitting form.
runners.comment: E100,000 Persian Force colt, dam modest 6f winner, one of two for yard, interesting
runners.owner: Ramiro Restrepo & Jacob West & Giselle De Aguiar

runners.name: Duke Of Arrakis
runners.spotlight: Lucky Vega colt was 14-1 for his debut here last month (6f, soft) and finished last of seven (well behind Ischgl); could do better on quicker ground but others appeal more.
runners.comment: Lucky Vega colt, well behind Ischgl when last here on debut, others appeal more

runners.name: Ocean Club
runners.spotlight: Persian Force colt is one of two runners for yard; 14-1 on debut in the Brocklesby and never landed a blow; huge improvement required.
runners.comment: Persian Force colt, also ran in the Brocklesby on debut, huge improvement needed

races.race_name: AES Recycling First Flier Stakes (Listed Race)

runners.name: Star Prospect
runners.spotlight: Starman colt got off the mark at the first time of asking here (5f, soft) when 3.5l ahead of Blixen Force; ran through the line strongly and while he has different ground to contend with now, he is a half-brother to two fast-ground winners; should be thereabouts.
runners.comment: Won on soft here on debut, half-brother to two fast ground winners, contender

runners.name: New Yorker
runners.spotlight: No Nay Never colt sent off the 4-7 favourite on debut at Dundalk and duly delivered a 1.25l win, despite displaying signs of greenness; his speedy dam Fairyland relished fast ground, so he should be well suited by conditions; leading claims for yard that has won three of the last four renewals.
runners.comment: Justified short odds on debut at Dundalk, should act on fast ground, leading claims
runners.owner: Derrick Smith & Mrs John Magnier & Michael Tabor

runners.name: Maria Quiteria
runners.spotlight: One of three for yard; Churchill filly bolted up under a positive ride on debut at Dundalk (5f), beating a stablemate by 4.25l; the runner-up hasn't done much for the form and this is a much tougher assignment; will need to improve plenty to make an impact.
runners.comment: Form of debut AW win has taken a knock, improvement needed in this tougher race

runners.name: Velozee
runners.spotlight: Sioux Nation filly made a winning start in a fillies' maiden at Cork (5f, soft), just getting up in the dying strides after blowing the start; quicker ground to contend with here and the third from her maiden has been beaten since, but should be more streetwise and yard's juveniles are always worthy of respect.
runners.comment: Got up in dying strides after tardy start to win at Cork, quicker ground now, respected

runners.name: What A Girl Wants
runners.spotlight: Persian Force filly is one of three for yard; made a promising start when a 1.25l third behind Star Prospect here last month (5f, soft); likely to improve plenty for that initial experience and also for quicker ground; looks the yard's main hope on jockey arrangements; interesting.
runners.comment: Promise on debut behind Star Prospect, likely to improve for quicker ground, interesting

runners.name: Rebel Dance
runners.spotlight: Starspangledbanner filly; 500,000gns yearling; half-sister to winners No Speak Alexander (7f-1m inc 2yo/Group 1; RPR 111) and Heavenly Being (5f 2yo; 78); dam 5f 2yo winner (inc Listed; 95); pitched in at the deep end on debut, but yard can ready one and she's worth monitoring in the market.
runners.comment: 500,000gns Starspangledbanner filly, half-sister to Gr' 1 winner, market best guide

runners.name: Blixen Force
runners.spotlight: Persian Force colt is one of three for yard; hung right in the Brocklesby at Doncaster (5f, gd-sft) on debut when a 3.25l third of 15; no match for Star Prospect over C&D (soft) on latest; much more needed and David Egan prefers the chances of What A Girl Wants.
runners.comment: Third in Brocklesby on debut, no match for Star Prospect latest, much more needed

races.race_name: Coolmore Stud Henry Longfellow Irish EBF Tetrarch Stakes (Listed Race)

runners.name: Neolithic
runners.spotlight: Didn't show much in first two starts but made it third time lucky when winning a median sires series race at Naas (6f, good); reappeared with a decent third here behind the evergreen Big Gossey in the Gladness Stakes (7f, sft-hvy); took the scalp of Greek Mythology at Gowran (7f, soft) last time but that rival lacked a recent run; top rated but Moore prefers Causeway.
runners.comment: Took the scalp of Greek Mythology at Gowran, top rated but Moore prefers Causeway
runners.owner: Westerberg & Mrs Magnier & M Tabor & D Smith

runners.name: Causeway
runners.spotlight: Left Gowran debut effort behind to win a 7f maiden here (sft-hvy) last October; won the Madrid handicap at Naas (7f, soft) on seasonal return off 94; that form has taken some knocks but interesting that Moore prefers him to his higher-rated stablemate Neolithic; unproven on quick ground; cheekpieces go on.
runners.comment: Won 7f maiden here last season, landed Madrid handicap on return, preferred by Moore
runners.owner: Michael Tabor & Derrick Smith & Mrs John Magnier

runners.name: Andab
runners.spotlight: Consistent in stakes company on turf last season and signed off with a Listed win at Dundalk (7f) in October; beaten a head on seasonal return in Al Rayyan Mile at Doha (gd-fm); respectable third in Listed race at Newcastle latest; cheekpieces go on; in the mix.
runners.comment: Consistent Listed AW winner, effective on quick ground, cheekpieces on, in the mix

runners.name: Geryon
runners.spotlight: Won maiden here on debut (7f, good); ran with real credit in Group 2s here on his next two starts over 7f (good) and 1m (good); stayed on well for fourth on seasonal return in Leopardstown Group 3 (behind Redemption Road, ahead of Iron Lily); return to 1m a plus and he's a leading contender.
runners.comment: Course maiden winner, stayed on over 7f in Gr'3 latest, return to 1m a plus, big chance

runners.name: Redemption Road
runners.spotlight: 1-5 as a two-year-old, the win coming in a 7.5f (yielding) Tipperary maiden in August; huge step forward on latest when running on nicely for a 3.25l third in a Leopardstown Group 3 (Geryon and Iron Lily behind); step back up to 1m looks sure to suit; go well if handling this quicker ground (half-brother won on good to firm).
runners.comment: Big step forward in Leopardstown Gr'3 latest, return to 1m a plus, should go well

runners.name: Greek Mythology
runners.spotlight: Won a 7f Dundalk median auction race on debut in December; 3.25l behind Neolithic on reappearance in a Gowran rated race (7f, soft) last month; sharper for that, step up to 1m should suit and could close the gap to that rival on this quicker ground.
runners.comment: Dundalk maiden winner, 3.25l behind Neolithic latest, could close the gap on better ground

runners.name: Iron Lily
runners.spotlight: Good run on debut here (7f, sft-hvy) in October when a 2.25l second behind Causeway; off the mark in 7f Dundalk maiden later that month; far from disgraced on seasonal return in 7f Leopardstown Group 3 (gd-yld); closely matched with Geryon and Redemption Road on that run and shaped like further would suit; not out of this.
runners.comment: Second behind Causeway here on debut, closely matched with two of these, not out of this

runners.name: Take Charge Star
runners.spotlight: Placed here on debut (7f, good) in August and won a Gowran maiden (7f, gd-yld) the following month; respectable sixth in Goffs Million on final start of last season; no match for Hardy Warrior on return at Naas (1m, soft); return to quicker ground a plus but more needed up in class.
runners.comment: Gowran maiden winner last year, beaten on return at Naas, more needed up in class

races.race_name: Keadeen Hotel Supporting Newbridge Community Day Irish EBF Fillies Handicap

runners.name: Institute
runners.spotlight: Placed in two of three starts last season, over 6f at Navan (yielding) and 5f at Dundalk; stepped up to a mile on seasonal return at Leopardstown and faded in the final furlong (1.25l behind Justiciar) after racing keenly early; drop in trip a positive and Moore takes over for handicap debut; needs to settle better.
runners.comment: Didn't see out 1m after racing keenly latest, down in trip, Moore up, must settle better
runners.owner: Derrick Smith & Mrs John Magnier & Michael Tabor

runners.name: Saint Agatha
runners.spotlight: Ran a fine race over C&D (gd-yld) on debut last July when a 0.5l second behind subsequent Group 3 winner Sugar Island (now rated 104); soundly beaten in conditions race back here in August; keen and weakened late on seasonal return at Dundalk (6f); will strip fitter now and could be on a generous mark based on that debut effort.
runners.comment: Handicap debutante, could be well treated based on debut effort over C&D, interesting
runners.owner: Cayton Park Stud & Mrs John Magnier

runners.name: Anushka
runners.spotlight: Off the mark at the third attempt last October in a 7f Dundalk fillies' maiden; pitched into Group 3 company on seasonal reappearance and failed to make an impact; not discounted here with sights lowered on handicap debut.
runners.comment: Dundalk fillies' maiden winner, out of depth latest, not discounted on handicap bow

runners.name: Spinning Around
runners.spotlight: Narrowly denied on second start in a 7.5f Roscommon maiden (good) in August; no show in Goffs million over this C&D (soft) but off the mark on handicap debut at Dundalk (7f) in October; will strip fitter for comeback run back there in March when beaten 1.5l off revised mark; tongue-tie on.
runners.comment: Won on h'cap debut at Dundalk in Oct', sharper for comeback there in March, tongue tied
runners.owner: Mrs M V Magnier, Mrs O'Louglin, Mrs Byrne

runners.name: Star Of Beauty
runners.spotlight: Best effort last season a 0.5l third of 13 in a Naas maiden (6f, good) in August; two runs this season below that level and her opening mark doesn't look overly lenient; tongue-tie and cheekpieces replace the visor worn last time.
runners.comment: Below best two runs this year, opening mark doesn't look lenient, new headgear
runners.owner: David K Kelly & Mrs Pauline Condon

runners.name: Sanctijude
runners.spotlight: 0-8 but has run plenty of solid races in defeat in maidens and on her handicap debut over C&D (yld-sft) last September when beaten 0.75l off 80; 1lb lower now after two respectable maiden runs this term at Naas and Gowran (both 1m, soft); ran well both previous starts on good ground; player.
runners.comment: 0-8, has run well in defeat inc' on h'cap debut over C&D, handles good ground, player

runners.name: Slaney View
runners.spotlight: Showed ability in all three maiden starts at 5f-7f on soft, good and good to yielding ground; failed to beat a rival home stepped into Group 3 company at Leopardstown latest (7f, gd-yld); could do better now tackling a handicap for the first time.
runners.comment: Handicap debutante, ability in maidens, outclassed in Gr' 3 latest, could do better now
runners.owner: Molly & Paul Willis Racing, Mrs J S Bolger

runners.name: Lamberella
runners.spotlight: Hasn't threatened in three maiden runs at 7f-1m on yielding and good to yielding ground but did catch the eye making late gains at Limerick last time (7f, yielding); the type to do better now tackling handicaps.
runners.comment: Caught the eye making late gains latest, potential improver on handicap debut

runners.name: Justiciar
runners.spotlight: Handicap debutante one of two for yard; promise on debut over C&D (good) last June when a 1.5l third behind Composing (now rated 104); disappointed when beaten favourite over C&D (good) in August; a bit keen when probably not quite seeing out 1m at Leopardstown on return; drop in trip a plus; could go well.
runners.comment: Handicap debutante, best run on debut, potentially well treated on that, could go well

runners.name: Green Carrera
runners.spotlight: Thurles maiden winner last season (1m, gd-yld) but that form hasn't really worked out; came up short in Listed race at Pontefract (1m, soft) when last seen in October; drops in trip for her handicap debut; stable flying and ground fine; check the market.
runners.comment: 1m Thurles maiden winner, came up short in Listed company, stable in form, check market

runners.name: Stella Amorosa
runners.spotlight: Handicap debutante; definite signs of ability in two of her four maiden runs last season, including a staying-on fourth of 19 here over 6f (good); well beaten over 7f at Dundalk when last seen in October, but too soon to write off and her yard is in form.
runners.comment: H'cap debutante, promise in 6f maiden here last year, returns with yard in good form

runners.name: Prevalence
runners.spotlight: One of two for yard; modest effort on debut at Dundalk (7f) in September but much more like it second time out over the same C&D in November, winning by 0.5l; off since and now makes her turf and handicap debut with rider taking off a useful 5lb; market should reveal expectations.
runners.comment: 7f maiden winner at Dundalk in Nov', off since, h'cap and turf debut, check market

races.race_name: Coolmore Auguste Rodin Irish EBF Athasi Stakes (Group 3) (Fillies)

runners.name: Black Caviar Gold
runners.spotlight: Won a 7f maiden at Cork on soft on third start at two; promoted from 85 to 108 after showing huge improvement to win a 7f Group 3 at this venue (soft); that involved a reversal of form with the very useful Pivotal Attack; loss of a shoe probably damaged her chance on return in Leopardstown Group 3 won by True Love; leading chance if restored to form; cheekpieces fitted.
runners.comment: Loss of a shoe probably damaged her chance on return, leading chance on juvenile form

runners.name: Kensington Lane
runners.spotlight: Off the mark at the fifth attempt in fine style at Gowran, confirming the promise she showed when chasing home the very useful Geryon at the Curragh; flopped in a Group 3 race won by Black Caviar Gold on final start; finished in front of that below-par rival on reappearance.
runners.comment: Useful form at two before a poor 7f Group 3 run at this venue, fair run on seasonal debut

runners.name: Sinmara
runners.spotlight: Always in touch when a promising third behind two Aidan O'Brien-trained fillies in a 7f maiden on soft-to-heavy here on only start at two; can go on to better things after wide-margin Gowran win last month.
runners.comment: Promising debut here last October; can go on to better things after wide-margin Gowran win

runners.name: Caught U Sleeping
runners.spotlight: Overcame inexperience when making a winning debut in a 7f fillies' maiden at Naas last August; kept on for third behind the subsequent Prix Marcel Boussac winner Diamond Necklace in a C&D Listed race; ran poorly over 1m on heavy on final start; in front of below-par Black Caviar Gold on seasonal debut.
runners.comment: Winner and Listed-place at two, in front of below-par Black Caviar Gold on seasonal debut

runners.name: Drop Dead Gorgeous
runners.spotlight: Dubawi filly; ninth foal; half-sister to eight winners including Classic scorers Gleneagles, Marvellous and Joan Of Arc, as well as Group 1 juvenile winner Happily; mentioned in positive terms by trainer before a smart debut win over this trip at Naas in March; high-class prospect; should stay further but fine with this trip.
runners.comment: Half-sister to four Group 1 winners, unraced at two, smart debut winner at Naas in March
runners.owner: Michael Tabor & Derrick Smith & Mrs John Magnier

runners.name: Wild Bessie
runners.spotlight: Ran only once at two; did well to beat a Ballydoyle-trained odds-on chance at Cork a month ago; open to further progress; this looks too demanding.
runners.comment: Did well to beat a Ballydoyle-trained odds-on chance at Cork, open to further progress

runners.name: White Sand Beach
runners.spotlight: Fifth foal out of the top-class Alice Springs; consistent maiden; stable's second string; major improvement needed.
runners.comment: Fifth foal out of the top-class Alice Springs, consistent maiden, major improvement needed
runners.owner: Michael Tabor & Derrick Smith & Mrs John Magnier

runners.name: Alphecca
runners.spotlight: Progressive on easy ground last autumn; off the mark at Killarney in early October and won a valuable restricted contest over 7f at Naas just six days later; unplaced in a handicap last week on her seasonal debut; faces a tough task in this grade.
runners.comment: Picked up a good prize at two, unplaced in a handicap last week, tough task in this grade

runners.name: Killashee Warrior
runners.spotlight: Thurles maiden winner after a fine effort in a 7f Listed race at Leopardstown (1 1/2l behind third-placed Caught U Sleeping); appeared to surpass her juvenile form with Leopardstown fourth behind True Love, and ahead of four of these rivals, including joint-favourite Black Caviar Gold.
runners.comment: Appeared to surpass her juvenile form with Leopardstown effort ahead of four of these

runners.name: Mayflower
runners.spotlight: Handled soft ground when making a winning debut at Leopardstown last July; closely matched with several of these on running behind True Love last month.
runners.comment: Won her only race at two, closely matched with several of these on Leopardstown running

runners.name: Pollenca
runners.spotlight: Outran 66-1 odds when staying on for fourth over 1m at Dundalk in January; 10-1 when confirming that promise with a course 7f maiden win on heavy ground in March.
runners.comment: Confirmed promise of a Dundalk run with a course 7f maiden win on heavy ground in March

runners.name: Sky Watch
runners.spotlight: Improved with each outing last season, needs to step up on the form of Dundalk maiden win that earned an official rating of 81.
runners.comment: Improved with each outing last season, needs to step up on the form of Dundalk maiden win

races.race_name: Clem Murphy Memorial Irish EBF Mooresbridge Stakes (Group 2)

runners.name: Minnie Hauk
runners.spotlight: Last year's leading middle-distance 3yo filly; won the Cheshire Oaks, the Classics at Epsom and the Curragh and the Yorkshire Oaks, and at least matched her best effort when touched off by Daryz on soft in the Arc, well clear of the remainder; form significantly boosted before season's end; possibly one race too many when sixth in the Breeders' Cup Turf; 1m2f trip is a mild concern; won over 1m at two.
runners.comment: Dual Oaks winner, also won Yorkshire Oaks, second in Arc, 1m2f trip is a mild concern
runners.owner: Derrick Smith & Mrs John Magnier & Michael Tabor

runners.name: Shaool
runners.spotlight: Highly progressive last season, improving to the tune of 35lb between June and October; versatile in respect of both trip and ground, but best at form 1m2f on last two starts; won a 21-runner premier handicap here in October and a Listed race at Leopardstown in November; failed to make an impact on return in Naas event won by Beset.
runners.comment: Highly progressive at three, failed to make an impact on return in Naas event won by Beset

runners.name: Beset
runners.spotlight: Ended last season on a high with 1m4f Listed win at Naas in a race in which stablemate Starford flopped; maintained progression at Naas on return; beat all bar a restored-to-form Starford in a Group 3 over C&D; has developed into a dependable mare; best suited by easy ground; faces stiffest task yet.
runners.comment: Has developed into a dependable mare, best suited by easy ground, faces stiffest task yet
runners.owner: K Blake/Mrs E Palmer/M Wilson/N Luck

runners.name: Cercene
runners.spotlight: Third in the Irish 1,000 Guineas; sprang 33-1 surprise on quick ground in the Coronation Stakes at Royal Ascot; no match for the front-running Whirl on heavy ground over 1m2f in the Nassau, still a good effort as outsider of five; stayed on for fifth in the 1m Matron Stakes; rated 10lb behind Minnie Hauk.
runners.comment: Third in Irish 1,000 Guineas, 33-1 Coronation Stakes winner, rated 10lb behind Minnie Hauk

runners.name: Edward Hamilton
runners.spotlight: Has finished last in both races since Dundak maiden win; supporting role here.
runners.comment: Has finished last in both races since Dundalk maiden win, supporting role here
runners.owner: P M Brant & Mrs J Magnier & M Tabor & Derrick Smith

runners.name: Tiberius Thunder
runners.spotlight: Won at Dundalk before a creditable third behind Delacroix and Lambourn in last year's Ballysax Stakes; confirmed a good standard when fourth of 12 in the Hampton Court Stakes at Royal Ascot; ran better than finishing his position implies when eighth of nine in the Saratoga Derby; unsuited by soft ground in Naas event won by Beset.
runners.comment: Capable sort but mixed form at three, unsuited by soft ground in Naas event won by Beset
runners.owner: AMO Racing Limited & Giselle De Aguiar

runners.name: Adelaide River
runners.spotlight: Second in the Irish Derby and Grand Prix de Paris in 2023; failed to match that level in Australia until finishing a nose second to Birdman in a 1m2f Group 3 at Randwick last September; down the field in the Caulfield Cup on his final start; resumes his Irish career, now a gelding and housed with Joseph O'Brien.
runners.comment: 2023 Irish Derby second for Ballydoyle, back after a spell in Australia, best watched

races.race_name: PG Duffy & Sons Citroen Apprentice Handicap

runners.name: Methgal
runners.spotlight: Made the breakthrough in a 7f Dundalk handicap in March for this rider; runner-up on his next start and maintained the improvement when second at Leopardstown 19 days ago, beaten a short head after making most; strong chance.
runners.comment: Maintained the improvement shown on Dundalk AW when second at Leopardstown, strong chance

runners.name: Moyassr
runners.spotlight: Excellent AW form during the winter, winning over 1m in October and twice in January; 0-11 on turf; never showed at Limerick nine days ago.
runners.comment: Excellent form at Dundalk during the winter, never showed at Limerick on return to turf

runners.name: Oxford Circus
runners.spotlight: Placed at Dundalk; not a bad run on recent turf debut over 1m at Cork; blinkered now; needs to improve.
runners.comment: Placed at Dundalk, not a bad run on recent turf debut, blinkered now, needs to improve

runners.name: Carrigans Grove
runners.spotlight: Enjoyed a successful 2025, winning three times and placing on a further five occasions from nine runs; should be sharper for a recent run over 6f at this venue; better ground a plus.
runners.comment: Enjoyed a fine campaign in 2025, should be sharper for a recent run, better ground a plus

runners.name: Go Out
runners.spotlight: Maiden has done most of his racing on AW, runner-up three times; fair recent 6f run here, one place off Carrigans Grove; new headgear combination.
runners.comment: Maiden has done most of his racing on AW, runner-up three times, fair recent 6f run here
runners.owner: Mrs John Murphy & John Pearson & Karl Kirwan

runners.name: Roman Harry
runners.spotlight: Good run on AW last time; hard to win with (2-41); looks held by Methgal on Leopardstown running before more recent third behind another of today's rivals, Comfort Line.
runners.comment: Good run on AW last time, hard to win with, looks held by Methgal on Leopardstown running

runners.name: Summer Island
runners.spotlight: AW winner on stable debut off 6lb lower; needs to find extra judged on Bellewstown third last month.
runners.comment: AW winner on stable debut off 6lb lower, needs to find extra judged on Bellewstown third
runners.owner: Nigel O'Hare & Martin W White & Mark Devlin & C G Bryson

runners.name: Notforalongtime
runners.spotlight: 9-32 at Dundalk; fine effort when third of 18 at Cork last time, with Oxford Circus back in eighth; turf record (1-27) remains a negative.
runners.comment: 9-32 at Dundalk, fine effort at Cork last time, but turf record (1-27) remains a negative

runners.name: La Tulipe Noire
runners.spotlight: AW maiden winner; her only turf wins came back-to-back at Cork and Roscommon last autumn; a bit high in the ratings since then.
runners.comment: Her only turf wins came back-to-back last autumn, a bit high in the ratings since then

runners.name: Comfort Line
runners.spotlight: Heavily-raced gelding recorded a seventh AW win recently; turf record is less persuasive (3-71), though some encouragement can be taken from last month's third of 20 at Leopardstown.
runners.comment: Heavily raced gelding recorded a seventh AW win recently, turf record is less  persuasive

runners.name: Pebble Island
runners.spotlight: Modest form for the Crisfords; towards the back in two handicap runs this season.
runners.comment: Modest form for the Crisfords, towards the back in two handicap runs this season

runners.name: Cannonball Queen
runners.spotlight: Has finished last in two handicap attempts this season; impossible to make a case for her.
runners.comment: Has finished last in two handicap attempts this season, impossible to make a case for her

runners.name: Loingseoir
runners.spotlight: Seven-time winner; three course wins, overall record here is patchy; mid-division on both starts this season.
runners.comment: Mid-division on both starts this season, three course wins, overall record here is patchy
runners.owner: W Keeling & M C Grassick & Mrs M J Grassick

runners.name: Free Solo
runners.spotlight: Has been out of form since Roscommon win last August; better ground may prompt a revival; however, Methgal is a more obvious contender for the stable.
runners.comment: Has been out of form since Roscommon win last August, Methgal stablemate appeals more

runners.name: Pinar Del Rio
runners.spotlight: Won at this venue for two of his three previous trainers; made an encouraging start for this stable when fourth at Dundalk in January; 9lb lower than when second here last May; interesting with Nicola Burns booked.
runners.comment: Won at this venue for two of his previous trainers, interesting with Nicola Burns booked

runners.name: Java Wood
runners.spotlight: Modest form in turf maidens before 40/1 win on AW/handicap debut last August; same very capable rider here.
runners.comment: Modest form in turf maidens before 40/1 win on AW/handicap debut last August, same rider

races.race_name: Treacy Group Supporting Newbridge Community Day Handicap

runners.name: Platino Bianco
runners.spotlight: Took a sharp rise in the ratings for taking second in AW claimer over this trip in February; well beaten in two sprint handicaps on turf since then.
runners.comment: Took a sharp rise in the ratings for second in AW claimer, well beaten on turf since then

runners.name: I'm Spartacus
runners.spotlight: Six-time winner over this trip on AW; still seeking turf breakthrough after 21 attempts; potentially well handicapped based on his victory at Dundalk in March, achieved at the main expense of today's rivals Reposado and Masked Angel; 11lb better off with Reposado.
runners.comment: Six-time AW winner, still seeking turf breakthrough, potentially well handicapped

runners.name: This Guy
runners.spotlight: Maiden was runner-up three times in a row at Dundalk during December/January; not as good in recent months; 20-1 chance when unplaced at Naas last Monday.
runners.comment: Maiden looked on the verge of AW success during the winter, unplaced at Naas last Monday

runners.name: Masked Angel
runners.spotlight: Generally reliable at Dundalk last year until a rare bad run in December; deserved her breakthrough success in February, and gave another solid display when third behind today's rivals I'm Spartacus and Reposado at the same venue in March; lightly raced on turf, could feature.
runners.comment: Has shown a fair standard of form at Dundalk in recent months, lightly raced on turf

runners.name: Tai Tam Bay
runners.spotlight: Second over C&D on heavy three starts ago, 1-29 record is a negative, placed ten times; visor replaces the usual blinkers.
runners.comment: Second over C&D on heavy three starts ago, 1-29 record is a negative, placed ten times
runners.owner: Dan Kiely & Leonard Kinsella & Jennifer Coyle

runners.name: Send Harry
runners.spotlight: Model of consistency on turf last summer, when his form included two third placings over C&D; transferred that form to 7f at Dundalk, picking up a well-deserved win; could feature now if reappearing in peak condition.
runners.comment: Notably consistent last year, could feature here if reappearing in peak condition

runners.name: Iff In Doubt
runners.spotlight: Showed promise for 10lb claimer Enola Pollet (rides Jon Riggens here) before Billy Lee took over for a 7f Dundalk win; has since finished second over 1m at the same venue; previous 6f form is weak, but he is more mature now, and his trainer is a master with sprinters.
runners.comment: 7f AW winner and second over 1m on latest, previous 6f form is weak but more mature now

runners.name: Reposado
runners.spotlight: Remains a maiden after 14 races; positives can be taken from last month's C&D second, which gives him an edge over several members of this field; that was on soft and better ground should suit on the evidence of his Dundalk form; however, he is not well treated relative to I'm Spartacus based on a clash in March.
runners.comment: Remains a maiden after 14 races, up 4lb for recent C&D second, better ground should help
runners.owner: AMO Racing Limited & Giselle De Aguiar

runners.name: Imperial Dream
runners.spotlight: C&D winner on quick ground in August 2024; only flashes of his best form last year; best run was over 7f at Dundalk; poor run there on final outing before a break.
runners.comment: C&D winner on quick ground in August 2024, only flashes of his best form last year

runners.name: Rappell
runners.spotlight: Three turf wins, the latest coming over C&D in 2024; added a 6f AW win last September; mid-division over C&D recently; hard to see him turning the tables on runner-up Reposado.
runners.comment: Mid-division over C&D recently, hard to see him turning the tables on runner-up Reposado

runners.name: Hugo's Girl
runners.spotlight: 14-race maiden has run some solid races in defeat, including third of 15 in a 7f fillies' handicap at this venue a year ago; third over 1m at Dundalk in March was encouraging; below her best with a hood fitted (discarded now) at Leopardstown last month.
runners.comment: 14-race maiden has shown winning potential from time to time, others have stronger claims

runners.name: Four Blondes
runners.spotlight: Only one win from 19 starts; runner-up five times; stable is enjoying a good spell, and Siobhan Rutledge's 3lb claim is an asset.
runners.comment: Only one win from 19 starts, runner-up five times, stable enjoying a good spell

runners.name: Genesis
runners.spotlight: Both wins gained on AW over this trip; 0-10 on turf, out of his depth in a strong C&D race last autumn (10lb out of the handicap); one of his better turf runs was when third of 21 over 7f at this venue last May.
runners.comment: Both wins gained on AW, 0-10 on turf, out of his depth in a strong C&D race last autumn

runners.name: Blue Anthem
runners.spotlight: Three wins for George Boughey (two on AW); signed off with a 6f AW second at Wolverhampton and bought for 21,000gns last October; unplaced in two Dundalk outings and well held by Reposado on running here last time.
runners.comment: Three wins for George Boughey, well held by Reposado on running here last time

runners.name: Amerilis
runners.spotlight: Ran twice for Paddy Twomey at two; made a good start for Gillian Scott when fourth of 12 in a C&D maiden in March, running on well in the closing stages; might prefer further, but interesting as a relatively unexposed filly making her handicap debut.
runners.comment: Two runs for Paddy Twomey, made a good start for Gillian Scott here in March, interesting

runners.name: Jered Maddox
runners.spotlight: Admirably durable veteran stuck to his task well when recording his 12th AW win last month; 1-27 record on turf strikes a strong cautionary note; 0-6 at this venue in his 87-race career.
runners.comment: Veteran stuck to his task well when recording his 12th AW win last month, 1-27 on turf

runners.name: Jon Riggens
runners.spotlight: Three-time course winner over 5f/6f on varying ground; poor return to action in March over C&D on heavy; better over 7f last time for this 10lb claimer; trainer has a more obvious chance with Iff In Doubt.
runners.comment: Has done a majority of his racing and winning at this venue, good run over 7f last time

runners.name: Oppenheimer
runners.spotlight: Mid-division on handicap/seasonal debut here last month; well held by runner-up Reposado; gelded since last outing.
runners.comment: Mid-division on handicap/seasonal debut here last month, well held by runner-up Reposado

runners.name: The Love Machine
runners.spotlight: 5f nursery winner for the Mulvany stable; out of form since joining this stable; still 4lb above his winning mark.
runners.comment: 5f nursery winner for the Mulvany stable, out of form since joining this stable

runners.name: Green Icon
runners.spotlight: Won twice over 7f for Owen Burrows as a juvenile; third over 1m here last August; no sign of similar form since then, struggling this season.
runners.comment: Third over 1m here last August, no sign of similar form since then, struggling this season

races.race_name: Each The Nook Novices' Handicap Hurdle

runners.name: Path Of Stars
runners.spotlight: 0-13 over hurdles but gave good account of herself when third at Plumpton (2m4f, good) four weeks ago; effectively 2lb lower here and Benjamin Macey claims 5lb; no suprise if she goes well again.
runners.comment: 0-13 over hurdles but gave good account of herself when third at Plumpton last month

runners.name: Dream's Ka
runners.spotlight: Safely held in both handicaps, more recently when upped to 2m2f and fitted with a tongue-tie fitted at Plumpton; clearly needs to improve but top trainer is persisting with him; market reference recommended.
runners.comment: Safely held in first two handicaps (1m7f/2m2f) but top trainer is persisting with him

runners.name: Risk It All
runners.spotlight: Second in consecutive 2m handicaps (one here) in February/March but wasn't quite in same form when well-held third on latest start and is now 0-9 over hurdles; unexposed at around 2m4f but needs to find something extra.
runners.comment: In the frame three consecutive times over 2m but needs to find something extra here

runners.name: Pottersmattyeeehaa
runners.spotlight: 0-9 over hurdles; suggested his turn was near when placed twice over 2m3f during the winter but wasn't quite at same level when fourth on final two starts last season; needs a boost from the new cheekpieces.
runners.comment: Placed twice during the winter but form has dipped slightly since; cheekpieces now added

runners.name: Ephyra
runners.spotlight: Ran no race at Plumpton last month and needs to put that performance behind her but she bumped into a revitalised opponent when second there (2m4f, good to soft; third has won since) two starts ago and has claims if first-time cheekpieces enable her to replicate that form.
runners.comment: Ran no race last time but has claims if judged on Plumpton second two runs ago

runners.name: Tony Joe
runners.spotlight: Won good-ground Hexham bumper in the autumn; well beaten in three subsequent maiden hurdles (1m7f-2m4f, good to soft/soft) but had wind surgery during 69-day break and is back on good ground for this handicap debut; improvement possible.
runners.comment: May benefit from wind op and/or return to good ground on this handicap debut

runners.name: Curious Mrs Fox
runners.spotlight: Lowly rated Flat maiden; has taken pretty well to hurdling this spring, making the frame at Newcastle (2m4f, good to soft) and Market Rasen (2m, good), but does not look obviously well treated for handicap debut.
runners.comment: Lowly rated Flat maiden; has taken quite well to hurdling but others have more potential

runners.name: Can U Feel It
runners.spotlight: Unplaced all five hurdling starts but was given a lot to do before keeping on steadily for fifth of 13 on handicap debut at Stratford (2m2f, good) a fortnight ago; remains open to improvement and is one to watch closely in the betting; tongue-tie added.
runners.comment: Kept on steadily from poor position for fifth on handicap debut; can improve

races.race_name: Norfolk Wildlife Trust Centenary Mares' Handicap Chase (Queen Boudicca Mares' Chase Series) (GBB)

runners.name: Gotthereintheend
runners.spotlight: Won over hurdles in December and made all in four-runner C&D (good to soft) contest on chasing debut in March; safely held at Stratford since but remains unexposed over fences and that course form counts for a lot.
runners.comment: C&D winner on chase debut; safely held since but still enters calculations

runners.name: She Is For Me Boys
runners.spotlight: Suffered dip in form after quite impressive win on chase/handicap debut in the autumn but was back on song when close third in valuable mares' chase at Market Rasen (2m5f, good) three weeks ago; in much calmer waters here and ought to be hard to beat.
runners.comment: Close third in valuable mares' chase at Market Rasen last month; ought to be hard to beat

runners.name: Moonlit Potter
runners.spotlight: Seemed to be enjoying hersef in the lead before mid-race mishap at Hereford (3m1f, good; first-time blinkers) last month but she's now 0-9 over fences and has basically become disappointing.
runners.comment: Suffered mid-race mishap last month and is now 0-9 over fences; has become disappointing

runners.name: Annie Nail
runners.spotlight: Won this race a year ago and was also on target over hurdles here (2m7f, good) last month; again ran well when second in 3m chase (good) three weeks ago but is effectively 10lb higher from out of the weights today.
runners.comment: Runs this track well and is in good form but competes from 9lb out of the weights

runners.name: Best Women
runners.spotlight: Dual Flat and dual chase winner in France; returned from long absence with three hurdle runs for Alex Hales last season, the best her second of five behind Annie Nail here (2m7f, good) last month; 5lb better off with her on this British chase debut, and the drop back in trip ought to suit.
runners.comment: Second to Annie Nail over hurdles here last month and 5lb better off today

races.race_name: Heritage House Mares' Maiden Hurdle (GBB Race)

runners.name: Mighty Fleur
runners.spotlight: Safely held when switched to fences for handicap debut in January but was second in handicap hurdle at Haydock (3m, good) last month, having been badly hampered on the run-in; drops back in trip with cheekpieces added here; strong claims if in similar form again.
runners.comment: Good second in 3m Haydock handicap last month; strong claims if in similar form again

runners.name: Ladies Day
runners.spotlight: Runner-up in two good bumpers in the autumn, the latter a Listed contest at Huntingdon (soft); not disgraced in Grade 2 bumper at Aintree (good to soft) last month and is bred to be suited by today's longer trip; commands respect on hurdling debut.
runners.comment: Second in two good bumpers in the autumn; commands respect on hurdle debut

runners.name: Merely A Detail
runners.spotlight: Good-ground bumper winner who has made the frame on all five hurdling starts, most recently when third of 13 (just behind Lady Kara) in Stratford maiden (2m, good) a fortnight ago; her tendency to race too freely remains a nagging concern but she's a contender if her stamina holds out over this new trip.
runners.comment: Placed in 13-runner maiden at Stratford (2m) last month; contender if new trip suits

runners.name: Kernie d'Airy
runners.spotlight: Seen only twice since winning slow-ground French bumper on debut in November 2024 but was second on hurdle debut at Hereford (2m3f, good) in March and still has potential.
runners.comment: Second at Hereford (2m3f) on hurdle debut in March and can build on that effort here

runners.name: Lady Kara
runners.spotlight: Had breathing issue two starts ago but, with tongue-tie fitted, was creditable second of 13 at Stratford (2m, good) last month; will be fine over this longer trip and ought to be on the premises again.
runners.comment: Creditable second at Stratford (2m) last month and will be fine over today's longer trip

runners.name: Blue's Secret Gem
runners.spotlight: Made the frame in two bumpers towards end of last season but the form isn't anything to get too excited about and improvement is required on this hurdling debut.
runners.comment: Made the frame in both bumpers but the form isn't anything to get excited about

runners.name: Johnny's Belle
runners.spotlight: Won slow-ground Irish point on debut in January and also showed promise on her first two hurdling starts (2m3f/2m4f); finished behind Kernie d'Airy on first occasion, though, and has quite a bit to find here.
runners.comment: Irish point winner; first two hurdle runs were quite promising but she has a bit to find

races.race_name: Norfolk Community Foundation David Keith Memorial Veterans' Handicap Chase (Leg 5 Stayers' Series)

runners.name: Time To Bite
runners.spotlight: Eight-time chase winner who often makes the running and is very well suited by good or faster ground; went close off his lower hurdle mark last month; on career-high mark from just out of the weights here and usually runs over slightly shorter trips but still features prominently in calculations.
runners.comment: Eight-time chase winner; well suited by good ground; ran well over hurdles last month

runners.name: Lord Baddesley
runners.spotlight: Easily won three-runner race at Newbury (2m4f, good) in October but subsequent form last season was underwhelming; well handicapped now (even from 1lb out of the weights) and will probably be okay back at 3m but needs the new cheekpieces to perk him up.
runners.comment: Below form since small-field win in October; needs boost from new cheekpieces

runners.name: Hang In There
runners.spotlight: 9-28 over fences, the latest win gained at Chepstow (2m7f, good to firm) in October; below that form three times on good to soft ground since but didn't run badly on penultimate appearance and will be better suited by the forecast good ground here; might be the answer.
runners.comment: 9-28 over fences; needs to better recent efforts but getting back on good ground will help

runners.name: Hold Your Fort
runners.spotlight: Made all in three-runner contest at Ascot (3m, good) in March and arguably bettered that form when close/clear second at Haydock (3m1f, good to soft) last month; might be a shade vulnerable off new mark but at least we know he's in excellent nick.
runners.comment: Won an Ascot in March and last month's Haydock second was arguably a career-best effort

runners.name: Numitor
runners.spotlight: Bounced back to form with game win at Hereford (2m5f, good) last month, his ninth success over fences; still well handicapped after recent 4lb rise and will be fine over this longer trip but it's hard to predict whether he'll be in the right mood again.
runners.comment: Returned to form with game Hereford win last month but has become hard to predict
runners.owner: Paul G Jacobs And Wetumpka Racing

runners.name: Mars Harper
runners.spotlight: Left Gordon Elliott after disappointing hurdle run last autumn and was again well beaten when returning for new stable in March; given a wind op since and reverts to chasing on a workable mark but still looks risky.
runners.comment: Well beaten over hurdles on stable debut; had wind op since but still looks risky

runners.name: Kitty's Light
runners.spotlight: Won three valuable marathons in a row in 2022-23 campaign and was honourable fifth in Grand National the following season; never really got going in 2025-25 but returns from 471-day absence on a tempting mark and will be suited by the forecast good ground; market helpful.
runners.comment: Very useful stayer in his prime; returns from long absence on tempting mark
runners.owner: R J Bedford & All Stars Sports Racing

races.race_name: Chestnut Group Norfolk National Handicap Chase (GBB Race)

runners.name: Stans The Man
runners.spotlight: Won this race in 2025 (good ground) and returned to form towards end of last season, latterly when second to recent big-race winner Havaila in Sussex National at Plumpton (3m4f, good); high on the list.
runners.comment: Won this in 2025 and returned to form at end of last season; high on the list

runners.name: Wicked Thoughts
runners.spotlight: Evidently well suited by the new trip when scoring at Taunton (3m4f, good) last month, and is now 2-4 over fences; open to further improvement if not inconvenienced by this switch to a left-handed track; respected.
runners.comment: Evidenty well suited by 3m4f at Taunton last month and now 2-4 over fences; respected

runners.name: Esperti
runners.spotlight: Revitalised 9yo who won stylishly on good ground over C&D and at Ffos Las (3m) last month; contests a much stronger race today but still needs to be taken very seriously in current mood.
runners.comment: Revitalised 9yo who won stylishly on good ground over C&D and at Ffos Las (3m) last month

runners.name: Always Busy
runners.spotlight: Back in good nick for his two runs last month, latterly when close third to Wicked Thoughts at Taunton (3m4f, good); still unexposed over marathon trips and still on a workable mark; could have a say.
runners.comment: Sound effort when third to Wicked Thoughts last month; still unexposed over marathon trips

runners.name: Tommie Beau
runners.spotlight: Gained three of his 14 wins here, including 2022 edition of this race, but has regressed and become inconsistent; didn't run badly in last month's Sussex National at Plumpton (3m4f, good; first-time cheekpieces) but younger rivals have much more appealing profiles.
runners.comment: Veteran; won this race in 2022 but has regressed and become inconsistent

runners.name: Planned Paradise
runners.spotlight: Bounced back to form and won soft-ground marathons at Banger (3m5f) and Hexham (4m) in February/March; pulled up (lame) in early stages at Plumpton last time but has made the frame in the last two editions of this race (both on good ground) and can make his presence felt again.
runners.comment: Made the frame in last two editions of this race and has won two marathons this year

runners.name: Unanswered Prayers
runners.spotlight: Well suited by a stamina test on good ground and lurks on a dangerous mark but has not been firing on all cylinders since running well on last season's reappearance outing in October; safely held when fourth in recent Sussex National; needs to up his game.
runners.comment: Well suited by a good-ground stamina test but well below best since good run in October

runners.name: Enjoy d'Allen
runners.spotlight: Won three times from the front (once over C&D) last season and lost out only narrowly in refitted cheekpieces at Plumpton (3m1f, good) a fortnight ago but this veteran is nothing like the force of old, and might find this race to competitive.
runners.comment: Won three times (once over C&D) last season but this 12yo might find this too competitive

runners.name: Northern Poet
runners.spotlight: Pulled up three consecutive times after his two good-ground wins (3m/3m5f) in March 2025; back on a good mark now and was seemingly perked up by first-time visor when close fourth of six (just behind Enjoy d'Allen) at Plumpton (3m1f, good) last month but overall profile remains unconvincing.
runners.comment: 3m5f winner in 2025; seemingly perked up by new visor last month but not easy to predict

runners.name: Stolen Silver
runners.spotlight: Well beaten all five starts last season, the last three times in valuable chases for new stable since February; plummeting down the weights and will appreciate this ease in grade but needs a revival.
runners.comment: Well beaten all five starts last season; eased in grade here but needs a revival

races.race_name: Chestnut Group Fakenham Grand Prix Handicap Hurdle (GBB Race)

runners.name: Grand Conqueror
runners.spotlight: Made rapid progress when switched to handicaps during the winter, winning twice (2m4f/2m1f), and returned from two-month break with creditable second at Plumpton (2m4f, good; first-time hood) last month; probably still has more improvement to come; good chance.
runners.comment: Progressive since switched to handicaps; went close at Plumpton last time; good chance

runners.name: Afadil
runners.spotlight: Without a win since February 2024 but gave good account when sixth (promoted to fifth) at last month's Aintree festival; 2lb lower here and well worth another crack at 2m4f; considered.
runners.comment: On a long losing run but gave good account at last month's Aintree festival
runners.owner: P J Vogt And Mrs C H Vogt

runners.name: Nardaran
runners.spotlight: 3-10 over hurdles; ended last season with two good runs, the latter a close third at Wincanton (2m5f, good); swaps cheekpieces for first-time blinkers and has good 5lb claimer Freddie Keighley enlisted today; shortlisted.
runners.comment: Ended last season with two good runs and has good 5lb claimer enlisted today; shortlisted

runners.name: Chillhi
runners.spotlight: Flat and dual hurdle winner for James Owen last summer but well beaten on both starts for new stable last month (hurdle/Flat); might be aided by refitted visor and/or first-time tongue-tie here but has lots to prove.
runners.comment: Well beaten on both starts for new stable last month; visor refitted; tongue-tie is new

runners.name: Morning Mayhem
runners.spotlight: Three wins for new stable last season, the latest here (2m, good to soft) in March; might have been a shade unlucky when keeping-on third at Stratford (2m2f, good; denied clear run) last time and is unexposed over 2m4f but needs to maintain his progress to land this prize.
runners.comment: 2m course winnner in March and looked shade unlucky over 2m2f at Stratford on latest start

runners.name: Royal Way
runners.spotlight: Won comfortably off reduced mark at Ludlow (2m5f, good) in March and has also performed well on both outings since, more recently when fourth at Sandown (2m4f, good) nine days ago; ran poorly on only previous visit to Fakenham but still enters calculations.
runners.comment: Won off reduced mark in March and has also performed well on both outings since

runners.name: Secret Vino
runners.spotlight: Won four-runner C&D (good to soft) contest rather cheekily in March and is now 3-6 in handicaps; 8lb higher in a much deeper race today but it's hard to tell how much was left in the tank last time.
runners.comment: Cheekily won small-field C&D contest in March and is now 3-6 in handicaps

runners.name: Kentucky River
runners.spotlight: Fair Flat racer who got off the mark over hurdles with easy success at Huntingdon (2m4f, good) last month, his second handicap; hiked up 15lb for that but still can't be opposed with much confidence here.
runners.comment: Off the mark over hurdles with easy win at Huntingdon last month, his second handicap
runners.owner: J R B Williams & Emdells Limited

runners.name: Tyson
runners.spotlight: Ex-Irish 6yo; initially disappointing for new stable this year but was made odds-on favourite at Newcastle (2m4f, good to soft) in March and won readily; even a 9lb rise may not stop him now he's up and running for Dan Skelton.
runners.comment: Initially disappointing for Dan Skelton but went off odds-on and won readily last time

runners.name: Breezethroughlife
runners.spotlight: Won small-field C&D (good) maiden in the autumn and put two disappointing runs behind him when third to Rialannah here (2m7f, good) last month; not yet fully exposed but others appeal more all the same.
runners.comment: Back on track with 2m7f third here last month but others appeal more all the same

runners.name: Dream Diamond
runners.spotlight: Comfortably won Worcester maiden (2m, good) on last summer's stable debut but has been some way below that form on all three starts since; will be fine over today's trip but needs to get career back on track.
runners.comment: Comfortable winner on last summer's stable debut but below that form all three runs since

runners.name: Rialannah
runners.spotlight: Came good for Lucy Wadham at second attempt, winning readily here (2m7f, good) three weeks ago; should be okay back at 2m4f and recent 5lb rise does not look excessive; could have more to offer for new stable.
runners.comment: Ready 2m7f course winner last month, on just her second start for Lucy Wadham

races.race_name: Break Mares' Handicap Hurdle

runners.name: Dartmouth Rose
runners.spotlight: Made all in small-field C&D (good) contest last month and now 2-2 since cheekpieces were added; another 4lb higher today but the headgear may unlock further improvement; likely contender.
runners.comment: Made all over C&D last month and now 2-2 over C&D; likely contender

runners.name: Yanka Blue
runners.spotlight: 0-7 over hurdles; no match for clear winner when second here (2m, soft) in December and well beaten over 2m4f on last month's stable debut; might have needed that recent run and is open to improvement over this new trip but others have much more obvious claims.
runners.comment: Well beaten on last month's stable debut and now 0-7 over hurdles

runners.name: Harley's Pride
runners.spotlight: Low-mileage 6yo who displayed significant improvement when upped to 3m for handicap debut at Doncaster (good to soft) in February, winning by over 3l; now with a new stable (was with David Pipe) and could still have a lot more to offer.
runners.comment: Improved to make winning handicap debut when upped to 3m in February; now with new stable

runners.name: Masonbrook Meadow
runners.spotlight: Scored off today's mark in first-time cheekpieces at Exeter (2m, good to firm) in October and posted a good effort over 2m4f the following month but was pulled up three weeks ago, after a break, and her Flat pedigree raises doubts over the suitability of this trip.
runners.comment: 2m winner off this mark in October but pulled up last month and has stamina to prove today

runners.name: Eliza Dolittle
runners.spotlight: Looked in excellent nick when making all for clearcut success at Plumpton (2m4f, soft) in February but was beaten about 30l over that C&D on next two outings; moves back up in trip with questions to answer.
runners.comment: Good winner at Plumpton (2m4f) in February but soundly beaten there twice since

runners.name: Crackerjack Queen
runners.spotlight: 0-5 over hurdles, and absent since remote third in first-time cheekpieces here (2m, soft; behind Yanka Blue) in December; needs to take a big step forward but pedigree offers significant hope she'll be suited by this first venture beyond 2m4f.
runners.comment: Safely held in first two handicaps (2m3f/2m) but has stamina in her pedigree

races.race_name: EBF Restricted Maiden Stakes (Band D) (EBF Restricted Race Qual') (GBB/IRE Incentive Race) (Div I)

runners.name: Furturra
runners.spotlight: Finished sixth of eight at a big price at Musselburgh (good) last Sunday but he caught the eye with his late headway and was only beaten around 3l; sets the standard on that form and he should improve for the experience.
runners.comment: Made some promising late headway and was only beaten around 3l at Musselburgh; respected

runners.name: Concert Pitch
runners.spotlight: Has quite a lot of stamina in his pedigree but he made some good late headway to finish third of nine over C&D (good) on recent debut; that was a promising effort and he's a player if he can build on that.
runners.comment: Made a promising start when third at a big price over C&D 11 days ago; respected

runners.name: Givendale
runners.spotlight: 1,000gns foal, 5,500euros yearling; sixth foal; half-sister to winners Noorban (5f including 2yo; RPR 91) and Hamaleel (1m AW; 68); dam 5f 2yo winner only start (72), out of 6f 2yo winning half-sister to 1m2f Listed winner Dawnus; makes some appeal on paper and she needs checking in the market.
runners.comment: Half-sister to a useful 5f 2yo winner; needs checking in the market on debut

runners.name: Golden Buzz
runners.spotlight: March foal; fifth foal; half-sister to 6f AW winner Badosa (RPR 62); dam 5f AW 2yo winner (59), sister to Group-placed 5f 2yo/6f winner Bould Mover, out of 5f 2yo winner; has a speedy pedigree and she's one to keep an eye on in the market.
runners.comment: Has a speedy pedigree and she's one to keep an eye on in the market

runners.name: Spark In The Coal
runners.spotlight: 5,000gns yearling; fifth foal; half-sister to winners Advertised (6f/7f AW 2yo; RPR 93), Monte Linas (7f-8.5f including France; RPR 90) and Buon Viaggio (1m/1m2f including KSA; 78); dam 7f/1m winner (89), out of half-sister to 6.5f 2yo Group 3 winner Perugina; yard 11% with 2yos last year and market should be informative on debut.
runners.comment: Yard 11% with 2yos last year and market should be informative on debut

runners.name: Spear Of Artemis
runners.spotlight: 1,500gns yearling; fourth foal; closely related to French 6f 2yo winner Love Symphony; dam 7f AW winner (RPR 78), half-sister to US 1m/1m1f Grade 1 winner Raging Bull, out of French Listed-placed 1m winner; yard 0-4 with 2yos this season but they have had two seconds.
runners.comment: 1,500gns yearling; yard 0-4 with 2yos this season but they have had two seconds

runners.name: Mr De Maggs
runners.spotlight: £11,000 yearling; first foal; dam 5f winner (RPR 81), half-sister to winners Sheepscar Lad (5f including 2yo) and Retracement (1m/1m2f), out of 5f 2yo Listed winner; has speed on both sides of his pedigree and interesting to see how he figures in the market.
runners.comment: Has speed on both sides of his pedigree and interesting to see how he figures in market

runners.name: Brixworth Bandit
runners.spotlight: 1,000gns foal; February foal; brother to 6f/7f winner Mad Punter (including 2yo/Italy/Greece; RPR 68), closely related to 7f winner Mystery Monarch (87), half-brother to Polish 1m2f winner Twilight Sea; dam maiden (46) half-sister to 6f Group 2 winner Twilight Blues; yard has been firing in winners recently and he needs watching in the market on debut.
runners.comment: Brother to a 2yo winner and he needs watching in the market for in-form yard

runners.name: Flashing Star
runners.spotlight: 10,000gns yearling; first foal; dam 5f/6f winner (including 2yo; RPR 92), half-sister to Polish 6f winner Antar Bin Shaddad, out of 7f-1m2f winning close relative of NZ 1m2f Group 1 winner Turn Tide; already been gelded and market should guide on debut.
runners.comment: 10,000gns yearling; already been gelded and market should guide on debut
runners.owner: Ursa Major Racing, J Craggs & Partner

runners.name: Lairy Mary
runners.spotlight: 33-1, finished tenth of 11 in a novice over C&D (good to soft) 19 days ago; stablemate of Furturra and she could be one for later on.
runners.comment: In rear over C&D 19 days ago; stablemate of Furturra and she could be one for later on

runners.name: Brandenburg
runners.spotlight: 66-1 for the Brocklesby at Doncaster (good to soft) in March and he never got involved before finishing 12th of 15; should know more this time but he needs to leave that initial form a long way behind.
runners.comment: Finished out the back at a big price in the Brocklesby in March; others preferred

races.race_name: EBF Restricted Maiden Stakes (Band D) (EBF Restricted Race Qual') (GBB/IRE Incentive Race) (Div II)

runners.name: Lady Dublin
runners.spotlight: 12-1 and green, shaped well on her debut when fourth in novice over C&D (good to soft) 19 days ago, not enjoying a clear run over 1f out; that race has already thrown up a couple of winners so this Ardad filly is a player with better to come.
runners.comment: Promising debut fourth over C&D (form franked); she's a player with better to come.

runners.name: Ponte Carlo
runners.spotlight: 33-1 and green, never threatened when a debut third of four in novice at Musselburgh (5f, good to soft) 22 days ago; more is required.
runners.comment: Never threatened when a debut third of four at Musselburgh; more is required here

runners.name: Bella Lucia
runners.spotlight: Belardo filly; dam, multiple 1m AW winner (RPR 72), is a half-sister to Italian 5f-7.5f winner Selfiestick; she's no forlorn hope on her initial run with her stable among the winners.
runners.comment: Belardo filly; she's no forlorn hope on her initial run with stable among the winners

runners.name: Moonlight Tango
runners.spotlight: 22-1, looked very much in need of the initial experience when last of six in novice at Thirsk (5f, good) 16 days ago; in very good hands and can build on it here.
runners.comment: Very much in need of experience when last of six at Thirsk on her debut; can build on it

runners.name: Spirit Of Mary
runners.spotlight: £5,000 yearling; Ubettabelieveit filly; unraced dam out of a maiden half-sister to 5f Group 2 winner Liberty Beach; the market can guide on her debut.
runners.comment: Ubettabelieveit filly; the market can guide on her debut

runners.name: Enemy Action
runners.spotlight: 7,000euros yearling; Cotai Glory gelding; dam, 5f AW 2yo winner (RPR 80), is a half-sister to winners Duckadilly (useful 7f/1m) and Saint Brandon (7f), out of 5f/6f winning half-sister to 6f Group 2 winner Magical Memory; the market can guide.
runners.comment: Cotai Glory gelding; the market can guide on his first run

runners.name: Explainingislosing
runners.spotlight: 1,000guineas yearling; dam, 6f AW winner (RPR 63), is a sister to 6f Listed winner Raucous and a half-sister to 5f Group 3 winner Manaccan; yard is going well so this son of Showcasing needs considering despite his high draw.
runners.comment: Yard is going well so this son of Showcasing needs considering despite high draw

runners.name: Jimmy You Mushroom
runners.spotlight: 35,000euros foal, 10,000gns yearling; half-brother to winners Action Point (5f/6f Listed; RPR 100) and Desert Swirl (5f; RPR 62); dam, unraced sister to 5f Listed winner Excelette, is out of an unraced half-sister to dual 5f Group 2 winner Bolshoi; appeals on paper but the betting can prove an accurate indicator for this son of Supremacy.
runners.comment: Appeals on paper but the betting can prove an accurate indicator for this son of Supremacy

runners.name: Mr Darling
runners.spotlight: 1,800gns yearling; Lope Y Fernandez colt; dam, 5f AW winner (RPR 80), is a half-sister to prolific 5f/6f winner Twilight Madness; possibilities starting out for his in-form stable.
runners.comment: Lope Y Fernandez colt; possibilities on his debut for his in-form stable

runners.name: May Bee In Profit
runners.spotlight: 33-1 and green, debut ninth of 11 in C&D novice (good to soft) 19 days ago; can take a step forward now.
runners.comment: Very green when a debut ninth over C&D; she can take a step forward now

runners.name: Craven Force
runners.spotlight: 16-1, never-dangerous sixth of nine in C&D novice (good) on his debut 11 days ago; needs to take a step forward.
runners.comment: Never-dangerous debut sixth over C&D; needs to take a step forward
runners.owner: Craven Surface , Mrs T Burns & Partners

races.race_name: Mayday Fillies' Novice Stakes (GBB Race)

runners.name: Donna Rumma
runners.spotlight: Bettered encouraging debut second here by finishing a short-head second to a promising sort in valuable event at Musselburgh (5f, good) eight days ago; good chance.
runners.comment: Promise on both starts, notably when short-head 2nd at Musselburgh last week; good chance

runners.name: Love A Giggle
runners.spotlight: 130,000gns yearling by Kodiac; made a winning debut in a four-runner event at Southwell last month (2-1 second-favourite); has a stiffer task with a penalty but stable going well; solid chance.
runners.comment: Has a penalty after winning four-runner Southwell event last month; solid chance

runners.name: Lake Muritz
runners.spotlight: Oasis Dream filly; has changed ownership since promising second of six in a Thirsk novice on debut last month (5f, good; 50-1); improvement likely.
runners.comment: Promising second in a Thirsk novice on debut (50-1) and should improve for that run

runners.name: Silesia
runners.spotlight: Promising third, 0.75l behind Lake Muritz, when an 11-4 shot on her Thirsk debut (5f, good; ducked right at the start) last month; improvement likely.
runners.comment: Promising third when an 11-4 shot on Thirsk debut and should improve for the run

runners.name: Persian Sunset
runners.spotlight: By speedy juvenile Persian Force; third foal; half-sister to 5f 2yo winner out of 5f 2yo Listed winner; interesting newcomer but wide draw not ideal.
runners.comment: Half-sister to 5f 2yo winner out of a 5f Listed 2yo winner; interesting; not ideally drawn

runners.name: Showmethebubbles
runners.spotlight: Beaten over 6l when a 66-1 shot here on her debut; others look stronger.
runners.comment: Beaten over 6l when a 66-1 shot here on her debut; others look stronger

races.race_name: We Love A Bank Holiday Handicap

runners.name: Showtown
runners.spotlight: Placed on all four starts since his debut run, all over 6f, on reappearance finishing runner-up in a Pontefract novice (good to soft); drop to 5f could suit; interesting with headgear tried on handicap debut.
runners.comment: Placed on last four starts (6f); drop in trip could suit; interesting with headgear tried

runners.name: O Fortuna
runners.spotlight: Unexposed; shaped well when fourth at Catterick in April (5f, good) but not so well in a 5f handicap at Newcastle last time (stumbled at the start); tongue-tie now tried; poorly drawn.
runners.comment: Unexposed; good 4th on reappearance but lesser run last time (stumbled start); poor draw

runners.name: Alfa Duplicate
runners.spotlight: Won AW handicaps at Southwell in February (5f) and Newcastle in March (6f); beaten 1.5l off this mark when third in a 5f Southwell handicap last time (Artista fourth) and could go well if as effective on turf.
runners.comment: Has to prove she is as good on turf but comes here having run 3 good races on the AW

runners.name: Head For Freedom
runners.spotlight: Won a C&D claimer on his second start as a 2yo last May (good); three good runs in nurseries after that; finished in front of a couple of today's rivals when a creditable fourth at Ripon on reappearance (6f, good to soft); interesting back at 5f.
runners.comment: Won C&D claimer last May; promising rapearance run over 6f; interesting back over 5f

runners.name: Explosive Finnish
runners.spotlight: Unexposed; best efforts have been on the AW, notably when close third over 6f at Southwell last time; back down in trip but could go well from stall 2 if as effective on turf.
runners.comment: Best form on AW, including close 3rd latest (6f); could go well if as effective on turf

runners.name: Hvar
runners.spotlight: Bought for 26,000gns after winning five-runner Wolverhampton novice in October (6f; carried head awkwardly) for Ralph Beckett; well beaten at Ripon on stable debut last month (6f, good to soft) but should come on for the run; poorly drawn.
runners.comment: 26,000gns buy after AW win for Ralph Beckett; should be better for last run; poorly drawn

runners.name: Star Noir
runners.spotlight: In the frame on all five starts last season, best run when a clear second in a Redcar novice in September (5f, good); has a chance on that form but lacks a recent run.
runners.comment: In the frame all five starts in 2025, best run when 2nd in 5f novice; lacks a recent run

runners.name: Ice Cold Alex
runners.spotlight: Won a 5f Pontefract novice on debut in April 2025 (good); some fair form after that and could go well despite high draw.
runners.comment: Novice winner on debut as a 2yo; fair form in nurseries; could go well despite high draw

runners.name: Houndhill
runners.spotlight: Placed in a couple of small-field nurseries at Newcastle in December; didn't fare as well back on turf at Haydock last month (6f, good to firm) after a break but should come on for the run.
runners.comment: Placed in 2 small-field AW 6f nurseries in December; should come on for reappearance run
runners.owner: J Raybould N Skinner D Jenkins

runners.name: Bye Law
runners.spotlight: Won Ayr maiden and Redcar novice last August (6f, good/good to firm); didn't progress in nurseries but was highly tried; gelded after and made a fair reappearance; stall 13 not ideal and return to 5f is a  concern.
runners.comment: Two 6f wins as a 2yo but didn't progress in nurseries; gelded since; not ideally drawn

runners.name: Come On Over
runners.spotlight: Some fair efforts at up to 7f for Richard Fahey and Charlie Clover last season; bought for 16,000gns in October; had no luck in running on Catterick reappearance (5f, good) and is much better than he showed there; interesting from good draw.
runners.comment: Some fair efforts in 2025; sold 16,000gns; met trouble on stable debut; interesting

runners.name: Believeinmenow
runners.spotlight: Two fair runs in handicaps this season, following a promising fourth at Bath (5f, good) with a 0.5l second at Southwell (5f); interesting from stall one but may need luck in running.
runners.comment: Two decent runs in 5f handicaps this season and has an obvious chance from stall 1

runners.name: Black Missile
runners.spotlight: Three fair efforts in maiden/novices last season, notably when third at Leicester on debut (5f, good to soft); unexposed; quite interesting on handicap debut.
runners.comment: Three fair efforts last term, notably close 3rd on debut; interesting on handicap debut

runners.name: Yellow Diamonds
runners.spotlight: Won a four-runner 5f fillies' novice at Musselburgh last October (soft) for Kevin Philippart De Foy before sold for 25,000gns; unexposed; makes handicap debut but lacks a run and has a bad draw.
runners.comment: Won Musselburgh novice on soft before 25,000gns sale in October; unexposed; poorly drawn

runners.name: Artista
runners.spotlight: C&D maiden winner last May and a 5f AW nursery in August; fair form in handicaps this year (met trouble when 4l third at Bath last time); each-way chance.
runners.comment: C&D winner as 2yo; fair form in h'caps this year (4l 3rd at Bath latest); each-way chance

races.race_name: New Whistlejackets Guinness Bar Handicap

runners.name: Emerald Harmony
runners.spotlight: Bought for 18,000gns in October having won twice over 5f (good/good to firm) for Johnny Murtagh in Ireland; good AW second on stable debut in March and fair turf run last time; each-way chance.
runners.comment: 18,000gns buy in October having won twice over 5f for Johnny Murtagh; each-way chance

runners.name: Canon's House
runners.spotlight: Went up 28lb for four wins last season, over 5f/6f on good/good to soft; showed he is still competitive with a sound sixth at Musselburgh on reappearance (5f, soft) and should come on for that run; a player.
runners.comment: Four wins last season (went up 28lb); sound reappearance run at Musselburgh; player
runners.owner: M McHale, J Munroe, M Guy & D Walshe

runners.name: Hundred Caps
runners.spotlight: Won over 6f at Southwell in January and two sound efforts after that; C&D record is 121, both wins on fast ground last term; visor replaces cheekpieces; well drawn; chance.
runners.comment: 6f AW win in January; two wins and a second over C&D in 2025; chance in new headgear

runners.name: Trilby
runners.spotlight: Last win came off a 6lb higher mark over C&D in April 2025 (good); runner-up in that equivalent race 11 days ago (good) and with rain about, could go well again.
runners.comment: Last win was over C&D in April 2025; good second over C&D last time; could go well

runners.name: Ventura Express
runners.spotlight: 9yo who is on an 18-race losing run; good second over C&D last July (good to firm) and is now 3lb lower.
runners.comment: On an 18-race losing run but is 3lb lower than when second over C&D last July

runners.name: Copper Knight
runners.spotlight: Legendary 12yo who has won 18 of his 117 races; fine second at Ripon on reappearance (5f, good), rain will help and although yet to win in four runs here, wouldn't rule him out.
runners.comment: 12yo whose record is 18-117; good second on reappearance and wouldn't rule him out

runners.name: Hedge Fund
runners.spotlight: Not a prolific winner; rather disappointing for this yard in a light campaign last season, best run when third at Haydock in September (5f, good; hood on for first time); no hood this time; on a good mark and could go well.
runners.comment: 2-21; bit disappointing last term but on a good mark and no surprise were he to run well

runners.name: Master Of My Fate
runners.spotlight: Six of his eight wins, including two this year, have been on the AW; turf wins have been at Lingfield (good) and Pontefract (good to firm); beaten only 1.5l when fifth at Nottingham last week (good); each-way chance.
runners.comment: Six of 8 wins have been on AW; dual winner on turf, ran well latest and each-way chance
runners.owner: Ms Janelle Cao, Ross Harmon & Partner

races.race_name: Churchill Tyres Handicap

runners.name: Freddy Robinson
runners.spotlight: Course winner who comes here on the back of a good second over C&D 11 days ago; can go well again eased 1lb.
runners.comment: Course winner; good second over C&D 11 days ago; can go well again eased 1lb

runners.name: Cloudy Rose
runners.spotlight: A two-time winner last season; resumed from six months off with an encouraging sixth of 12 in 1m4f handicap at Pontefract last month, challenging wide; that form has been well advertised since so this C&D scorer merits serious consideration off a 1lb lower mark.
runners.comment: Encouraging reappearance sixth at Pontefract; this C&D scorer merits serious consideration

runners.name: Daring Leader
runners.spotlight: Ended 2025 with victory at Newcastle (1m2f) in September but he's yet to fire this spring, only eighth in 9.5f Wolverhampton handicap 25 days ago; needs to take a big step forward despite a slipping mark.
runners.comment: Yet to fire this spring, eighth at Wolverhampton last time; needs to take big step forward
runners.owner: D J Jones & M T Brougham

runners.name: Sea Master
runners.spotlight: Made light of a six-month absence when landing 12-runner handicap over C&D 19 days ago; up 3lb but he's not taken lightly.
runners.comment: Made light of six-month absence when scoring over C&D latest; up 3lb but not taken lightly

runners.name: The Pug
runners.spotlight: Has won once since 2023 and he beat just one home in 9.5f Wolverhampton handicap in March; not one to rely on.
runners.comment: Has won once since 2023 and he beat just one home in 9.5f Wolverhampton handicap in March

runners.name: Muhib
runners.spotlight: 0-20 but he arrives in decent nick, fifth of 12 to Sea Master over C&D 19 days ago; not out of things on 4lb better terms.
runners.comment: 0-20 but posted a solid fifth over C&D 19 days ago; not out of things

runners.name: Kameel
runners.spotlight: On a losing run but he failed to stay 1m6f when ninth in handicap at Wolverhampton four weeks ago; not ruled out back in trip off an easing mark.
runners.comment: On losing run but failed to stay 1m6f at Wolverhampton latest; not ruled out back in trip

runners.name: El Ghaawy
runners.spotlight: Low-mileage 4yo who failed to build on earlier promise when beating one home in 1m5f Chelmsford handicap in March; blinkers are reached for now.
runners.comment: Beat one home in 1m5f Chelmsford handicap in March; blinkers are reached for now
