runners.owner: Jwi Building Solutions J Kelly & Partner

runners.name: Assured
runners.spotlight: Lightly raced Nathaniel mare who failed to kick on when sent handicapping in 2025, seventh of 12 in 2m1f Bath handicap in October; since changed hands for 11,000gns and she needs to hit the ground running for her new yard after seven months off the track.
runners.comment: Failed to kick on sent handicapping in 2025; needs to hit ground running for new yard

races.race_name: Brian Dixon Memorial Handicap

runners.name: Suddenly I See
runners.spotlight: Upwardly mobile Ribchester colt who went 2-3 when comfortably making all in 1m Musselburgh handicap on his recent return; hiked up 7lb but well in the mix again.
runners.comment: Went 2-3 at Musselburgh h'cap on recent return; hiked up 7lb but well in the mix again

runners.name: North Force
runners.spotlight: Frankel colt who has shaped promisingly when placed on each of his three outings, sticking to his task well when third of 11 in 7f Catterick maiden on his return last month; capable of better now stepping into handicap company, especially over this stiffer track; big player.
runners.comment: Promising Frankel colt; good third at Catterick on return; player now going into h'caps

runners.name: The Resdev Scholar
runners.spotlight: Completed a hat-trick at Doncaster (6f) in November to end 2025 and he's continued in very good form this spring when in the frame in 7f handicaps at Southwell and Newbury; ought to be in the shake-up again.
runners.comment: Very good runs to make frame at Southwell and Newbury this spring. ought to be in shake-up
runners.owner: N Bradley 29, Resdev & South Bank Racing

runners.name: Lyrical Song
runners.spotlight: Took a big step forward after seven months off/gelded when getting off the mark in C&D handicap 19 days ago; this son of Lope Y Ferndanez can make his presence felt despite a 5lb rise.
runners.comment: Got off the mark over C&D 19 days ago; can make presence felt despite a 5lb rise

runners.name: Dunkeld Dreamer
runners.spotlight: Reliable sort who gained an all-the-way win in 1m handicap at Haydock nine days ago; has a career-high mark to overcome now but this C&D winner can't be ruled out.
runners.comment: All-the-way winner at Haydock nine days ago; this C&D winner is firmly in the picture

runners.name: Cool Molly
runners.spotlight: Thriving on the AW in 2026 and she readily garnered a fourth victory in 7f Southwell handicap 19 days ago; up another 6lb but not taken lightly on her turf debut.
runners.comment: Gained fourth AW win of 2026 at Southwell 19 days ago; not taken lightly on turf debut

runners.name: Jungle Ruler
runners.spotlight: Made a winning start for current yard in 6f Southwell novice in January and he's continued in good form, fourth of 14 in 6f Newcastle handicap last month; this return to 7f promises to suit so he's firmly in the picture.
runners.comment: Good fourth of 14 at Newcastle last month; firmly in the picture with return to 7f a plus

runners.name: Gorey Gold
runners.spotlight: Kickstarted 2026 with 7f Southwell victory and comes here on the back of a solid third of four in 8.5f Wolverhampton handicap in March; not out of things with cheekpieces added.
runners.comment: Solid third in Wolverhampton handicap in March; not out of things with cheekpieces added

runners.name: Mandarin Spirit
runners.spotlight: Ghaiyyath filly who got off the mark in 7f Thirsk maiden in July; faced a stiff task when eighth in the Rockel Stakes at Newmarket in September and she remains with potential returning for her handicap debut after three starts.
runners.comment: Won at Thirsk; stiff task in Rockel at Newmarket so remains with potential on h'cap debut

runners.name: Night Patrol
runners.spotlight: 125-1-winner of 7f Newbury novice but he backed it up with a solid third of five in 1m York handicap later in the autumn on his final run for Richard Hannon; gelded/changed hands for 48,000gns since and needs considering for his new yard.
runners.comment: Solid third at York for Richard Hannon; gelded and needs considering for his new yard

races.race_name: Support Independent Racecourses Handicap

runners.name: Copper And Five
runners.spotlight: Veteran who won this race last year and returned to form with a creditable second over C&D (Woodleigh fifth) 11 days ago; has possibilities off unchanged mark and he won't mind what the weather does.
runners.comment: Won this race last year and was runner-up off this mark over C&D last month; shortlisted
runners.owner: Mrs Sally Doyle And Mrs Ruth Carr

runners.name: Law Degree
runners.spotlight: Two soft-ground wins (7.4f/1m) in 2024, but he's drawn a blank since and has struggled on the Flat and over hurdles in his last four starts; has plenty to prove and yard also runs Evelyn's Phoenix.
runners.comment: On long losing run and he's struggled under both codes in his last four runs

runners.name: Woodleigh
runners.spotlight: Only 1lb higher than for his Redcar win (1m2f, good to soft) in October but he's finished down the field in all four subsequent runs; wasn't beaten far over C&D 11 days ago but he's an infrequent winner and others are more persuasive.
runners.comment: On workable mark but he's an infrequent winner and others are more persuasive

runners.name: Pisanello
runners.spotlight: Useful miler in his prime but his last win was almost two years ago and he's been well held at Newcastle and Doncaster in last two starts; continues to drop down the weights but others are preferred.
runners.comment: On much-reduced mark but last win was in 2024 and he's been well held in last two runs

runners.name: Penny Ghent
runners.spotlight: 25-race maiden; went close at a big price in a Redcar handicap (1m, good) in November, but she's been back in the doldrums in last two runs and was beaten 12l on her Nottingham reappearance last month; passed over.
runners.comment: 25-race maiden and she was out the back on her Nottingham reappearance last month

runners.name: Evelyn's Phoenix
runners.spotlight: Turned things around with a rallying win on his Nottingham reappearance (8.3f, good) last month and he backed that up with a good second from wide draw at Leicester (1m2f, good to firm); remains well handicapped on his best form and he's a key player on this drop back in trip.
runners.comment: Resurgent 5yo who won at Nottingham before a good second at Leicester; key player

runners.name: Dandy's Angel
runners.spotlight: Six-time course winner (all 1m2f) who goes on any ground; never involved on her C&D reappearance last month, but she should be sharper this time and is now 11lb lower than for her win here last summer; could be dangerous if she gets a good pace and some luck.
runners.comment: Six-time course winner and she could be dangerous if she gets a good pace and some luck

runners.name: Nakatomi
runners.spotlight: 18-race maiden who has been well held in five runs at 1m4f-2m this year; mark has bottomed out now and he needs a transformation on this big drop back in trip; reapplied blinkers replace a visor.
runners.comment: 18-race maiden and he needs a transformation on this big drop back in trip
runners.owner: F Reay, S McMillan & S Pedersen

runners.name: Coolree
runners.spotlight: Acts on good to firm and soft ground; overall record of 3-38 but his last two wins have come over C&D and he returned to form with a front-running third here 11 days ago; remains well handicapped off 2lb lower than for his last success and he could make another bold bid.
runners.comment: Dual C&D winner who returned to form with a good third here 11 days ago; in the mix

runners.name: Hostelry
runners.spotlight: Ten-time turf winner who is well treated on her best form last year, but she's been quiet in three runs this season and latest was over C&D (well-held sixth) last Saturday; one of two runners for yard and she needs to raise her game.
runners.comment: Ten-time turf winner but she's been quiet in three runs this season; needs to raise game

runners.name: Far Ahead
runners.spotlight: Won a Catterick novice (7f, good to firm) last May but has form figures of 90660000 in handicaps since; has tumbled down the weights but he's failed to beat a rival in his last four starts including when prominent in the market at Redcar two weeks ago; lots to prove back up in trip.
runners.comment: Has record of 90660000 in handicaps and he's finished last in his last four runs
runners.owner: Richard Fahey Ebor Racing Club Ltd

runners.name: Cloch Nua
runners.spotlight: Tapeta specialist who returned from a short break with an eye-catching third at Newcastle (1m) two weeks ago; has claims if he can back that up, but he's 0-11 on turf and has had only one run in this sphere in his last 14 starts; mixed messages.
runners.comment: Tapeta specialist who was third at Newcastle two weeks ago but he's 0-11 on turf

runners.name: Rajawail
runners.spotlight: 24-race maiden who has been tried at all manner of different trips and was never involved when dropped back to 1m at Pontefract (good; 150-1) last week; opposable.
runners.comment: 24-race maiden who was never involved when dropped back to 1m at Pontefract last week

runners.name: Wath Court
runners.spotlight: Just one placed effort from 30 starts and and he's struggled in three runs for new yard this spring; best to look elsewhere.
runners.comment: Just one placed effort from 30 starts and he's struggled for new yard this spring

runners.name: Bantz
runners.spotlight: Eventually lost his form for Gemma Tutty, having scored twice over 7f earlier in 2025; sold for 4,500gns and gelded since last run; enough to prove back up in trip on reappearance but he is on a dangerous mark.
runners.comment: Out of sorts in the autumn and has something to prove back up in trip for new yard

races.race_name: Cuthy's Race Sponsored By TTC Handicap

runners.name: Escape Plan
runners.spotlight: Prevailed narrowly in similar event at Kempton (6f, AW) with reappearance under his belt, getting off the mark at the fifth attempt; may improve further, provided he's as effective back on turf; respected.
runners.comment: May build on Kempton AW win and improve further, provided he's as good back on turf

runners.name: Madman
runners.spotlight: Record of only 1-14 for Tom Ward, the success over 6f on AW in January; sold for 7,000gns since last run; has bits of turf form but is on a tougher mark back in this sphere.
runners.comment: Record of only 1-14 for Tom Ward, the win on AW; sold for 7,000gns since last run

runners.name: Eightthreeone
runners.spotlight: Form since handicapping and upped to 6f this year comprises a neck success at Southwell (AW) and respectable third at Yarmouth; may still have further progress in him; enters calculations.
runners.comment: Form since handicapping comprises an AW success and turf third; may do better still

runners.name: Uniter
runners.spotlight: Proving an inconsistent maiden and failed to make much impact at Kempton (6f; stable debut) most recently; yard also runs Our Guy, who looks more interesting.
runners.comment: Proving an inconsistent maiden; second start for new stable

runners.name: Viking Glory
runners.spotlight: Four-race maiden for Adrian Keatley, showing form at 6f-1m on turf/AW; drops back in distance on stable/handicap debut and return from layoff; heed the market signals.
runners.comment: Drops back in trip on stable/handicap debut and return from layoff; check the betting

runners.name: Footstepinthewoods
runners.spotlight: Always behind in big-field maiden at Newbury on reappearance but may do better now handicapping with the outing under his belt; showed his autumn form at about 6f.
runners.comment: May do better now handicapping with Newbury reappearance under his belt

runners.name: Forever Glamorous
runners.spotlight: Went close at Ffos Las (6f, heavy) on handicap debut but failed to back up that effort; left John O'Shea and had gelding operation since final 2yo run; enough to prove.
runners.comment: Has failed to back up a good effort at Ffos Las; debut for new stable

runners.name: Adalida
runners.spotlight: Made the frame at Haydock (6f) last time in only her second handicap attempt; seems more exposed than most of these rivals, being 0-10, but she's a form contender and could go well.
runners.comment: Seems more exposed than most, being 0-10, but she's a form contender and could go well

runners.name: Nebbia
runners.spotlight: Best AW effort last autumn when second at Wolverhampton (5f) on penultimate start; has inconsistent form but could go well in this first turf/handicap attempt.
runners.comment: Has inconsistent form but could go well in this first turf/handicap attempt

runners.name: Medyg
runners.spotlight: Showed ability on AW in the autumn, close fourth dropped to 6f at Wolverhampton the final occasion; gelded since; may be capable of progress now handicapping.
runners.comment: Showed ability on AW in the autumn; gelded since; may show progress

runners.name: Sun Of Dolly
runners.spotlight: Midfield in 6f novice contest at Leicester (6f) on seasonal debut, never landing a blow; related to winners for his stable and improvement may materialise now handicapping.
runners.comment: Related to winners for his stable; improvement may materialise now handicapping

runners.name: Yes Waliim
runners.spotlight: Gelded prior to showing no improvement when last seen but has had another physical tweak, namely wind surgery, since and now switches to handicap level; may well take a step forward; interesting.
runners.comment: Had wind surgery since last run and now switches to handicap level; step forward possible

runners.name: Our Guy
runners.spotlight: Turf/handicap debutant who has something to find on his AW efforts (1m/7f) but gives the impression this drop to sprinting will suit; improvement looks plausible.
runners.comment: Turf/handicap debutant who gives the impression this drop to 6f will suit

races.race_name: Betwright Windsor Flat Season Opener Fillies' Novice Stakes (GBB Race)

runners.name: Beauty Box
runners.spotlight: Showed promise with fourth of eight in maiden (5f, good) at the Newmarket Craven meeting, while leaving the impression this extra furlong will suit, in keeping with her pedigree (half-sister to 6f 2yo scorer); open to progress and she's the only runner who has the benefit of experience.
runners.comment: Showed promise at the Newmarket Craven meeting; the only runner with experience

runners.name: Arabica Queen
runners.spotlight: February foal; 10,000gns foal, £28,000 yearling; from the first crop of Caturra; half-sister to three winners, notably Poet's Dawn (prolific 7.4f-1m2f including 2yo; RPR 91); yard's 2yo results thus far in 2026 don't bode well.
runners.comment: £28,000 yearling; by Caturra; yard's 2yo results thus far in 2026 don't bode well

runners.name: Bymiddaytomorrow
runners.spotlight: April foal; 90,000gns yearling; by Mehmas; sister to 6f/7f 2yo scorer The Waco Kid (including Group 3; RPR 106), out of unraced half-sister to 2,000 Guineas winner Kameko; stable's first 2yo runner of the campaign; likely type on breeding.
runners.comment: 90,000gns yearling; stable's first 2yo runner of 2026; likely type on breeding

runners.name: Chanter Le Blues
runners.spotlight: April foal; 100,000gns yearling; Blue Point half-sister to five winners, notably Great Wish (6f/7f including UAE Listed; RPR 109); dam 5f/6f scorer (including 2yo Group 2; 115); yard has made a cracking start with 2yos this season; respected debutante.
runners.comment: 100,000gns yearling; yard has made a cracking start with 2yos this term; respected

runners.name: Girl Scout
runners.spotlight: March foal; 50,000gns foal, 110,000gns yearling; from the first crop of Perfect Power; half-sister to two winners, notably Eve's Boy (7f 2yo; RPR 84); dam 1m2f scorer (68), half-sister to useful 7f 2yo/1m2f winner Basateen; in excellent hands; possibilities.
runners.comment: 110,000gns yearling; by Perfect Power; in excellent hands; possibilities
runners.owner: Teme Valley, Aura Racing & T Jacobs

runners.name: Love Is
runners.spotlight: March foal; 90,000euros yearling; Sioux Nation half-sister to four winners, notably Dark Tornado (7f; RPR 99); dam 9.4f AW scorer (69), half-sister to Royal Lodge winner/2,000 Guineas runner-up Snow Ridge; check the betting.
runners.comment: 90,000euros yearling; by Sioux Nation out of AW scorer; check the betting

runners.name: Roxelina
runners.spotlight: February foal; 170,000gns foal; by Havana Grey; sibling to two winners, notably Holguin (5f-1m including 2yo/Listed; RPR 113); dam 1m AW scorer (71); owner has a particularly good record in the 2yo department; all factors amount to strong credentials.
runners.comment: 170,000gns foal; owner has a particularly good record in the 2yo department

races.race_name: Cameron Smart Memorial Novice Stakes (GBB Race)

runners.name: Heraldry
runners.spotlight: Kingman colt; scored at Kempton kept to 1m/AW on reappearance, beating Big Eagle by a neck and building on his 2yo effort; could overcome a 7lb penalty with further progress plausible.
runners.comment: Prevailed by a neck from Big Eagle at Kempton on reappearance; open to further progress

runners.name: Decade Of Time
runners.spotlight: Belied his 40-1 odds with 2l third of 25, faring best of the newcomers, in Newbury maiden (1m, good); the form looks quite useful; leading contender provided the promise is confirmed.
runners.comment: Finished 2l third of 25, best of the newcomers, in Newbury maiden; leading contender

runners.name: Kahin
runners.spotlight: 240,000gns foal; by Kingman out of maiden (RPR 81) half-sister to 7f/1m Listed winner Doom and daughter of US 9.5f/1m2f Grade 1 winning half-sister to 1m 2yo Group 1 scorer Sulk; trainer landed this race with a newcomer last year; interesting, especially if the market speaks favourably.
runners.comment: 240,000gns foal; by Kingman and from a smart family; interesting newcomer

runners.name: Big Eagle
runners.spotlight: Belied his 50-1 odds with neck second to Heraldry at Kempton (1m, AW) on seasonal debut; now gets a 7lb pull with that rival and has possibilities provided the form is backed up.
runners.comment: Neck second to Heraldry at Kempton; now gets a 7lb pull with that rival; possibilities

runners.name: Bemersyde
runners.spotlight: Showed promise with fourth of ten, behind a winner who looks a smart prospect, in the Wood Ditton (1m, good) at the Newmarket Craven meeting; one of the main contenders.
runners.comment: Showed promise with fourth in the Wood Ditton at Newmarket; one of the main players

runners.name: Turret
runners.spotlight: 580,000euros yearling; by Kingman out of unraced sister to Group 1 winners Hydrangea (1m/1m4f), The United States (Australian 1m2f) and Hermosa (1,000 Guineas); only 5-2 at the time of his withdrawl (upset in stalls) at Kempton last month; respected debutant, assuming he's calmer.
runners.comment: 580,000euros yearling; by Kingman out of sister to Group 1 winners; respected debutant

runners.name: Far Far Out
runners.spotlight: Only a modest fifth of eight in 1m AW maiden at Newcastle, running below market expectations; however, may do better switched to turf with the outing under his belt.
runners.comment: Modest fifth in AW event but may do better switched to turf

runners.name: Windbreaker
runners.spotlight: Has something to find on his December AW efforts but is nicely bred and remains open to improvement; yard also runs interesting newcomer Kahin; heed the market signals.
runners.comment: Nicely bred and remains open to to improvement; yard also runs Kahin; market useful

runners.name: Eagles Attire
runners.spotlight: Mid-divison both outings, latest in big field at Newbury (1m) where he finished behind a couple of today's opponents; likely to be more interesting in handicaps shortly.
runners.comment: Mid-division both starts; likely to be more interesting in handicaps soon

runners.name: The Anthony Gover
runners.spotlight: Turf debutant who has something to find on his AW efforts at about 1m, namely third at Wolverhampton then midfield at Southwell; handicaps may be more suitable soon.
runners.comment: Turf debutant who has something to find on his AW efforts

runners.name: Sponsor
runners.spotlight: Seemed in greater need of the outing/experience than Decade Of Time in big-field maiden at Newbury, finishing ninth; may get closer to that rival, with improvement plausible.
runners.comment: Seemed in greater need of the outing/experience than Decade Of Time at Newbury
runners.owner: Al Shaqab Racing, Miller And Day

runners.name: Symphony's Song
runners.spotlight: Absent since fourth of five, recording a meagre RPR, in 7f AW maiden at Newcastle 14 months ago for George Boughey; best watched unless the market speaks positively.
runners.comment: Absent since recording a meagre RPR on AW 14 months ago for George Boughey

runners.name: Thomas Picton
runners.spotlight: Finished behind some of today's opponents, including stablemate Decade Of Time, at Newbury then failed to improve at Epsom; looks one for handicaps shortly.
runners.comment: Looks one for handicaps shortly

runners.name: Mostaan
runners.spotlight: Too Darn Hot colt who cost 280,000gns as a yearling; appeared to need his 2yo outing at Kempton (7f, AW) where he finished fifth of eight; open to improvement this term.
runners.comment: Appeared to need his 2yo outing; open to improvement this term

races.race_name: BetWright Bangers N' Cash Handicap

runners.name: Crazee Icon
runners.spotlight: Six-race maiden who has posted good efforts since handicapping, most recently close fifth when favourite for Newmarket contest (kept to 7f) on seasonal debut; gives the impression this new trip will suit; solid.
runners.comment: Good efforts since handicapping; gives impression this new trip will suit; solid

runners.name: Jamie Sommers
runners.spotlight: AW novice winner who did well to finish a good third at Nottingham (1m, good) returned to turf eight days ago, having come from a difficult position; strong chance off same mark, provided this pans out better.
runners.comment: Did well to finish a good third at Nottingham eight days ago; strong chance off same mark

runners.name: Tough Date
runners.spotlight: Thrice-raced gelding who brings two pieces of placed form, runner-up behind the clear pick on the figures at Bath (1m; turf debut) latest; may be capable of progress in handicaps; not dismissed.
runners.comment: Thrice-raced gelding who brings placed form; may show progress in handicaps

runners.name: Legacy Rock
runners.spotlight: Five-race maiden; showed some improvement with tongue-tie fitted at Lingfield (7f, AW) most recently, finishing 1l fourth and shaping as if this longer distance is worth exploring; may take another step forward.
runners.comment: Improved for fitting of tongue-tie last time, finishing 1l fourth at Lingfield

runners.name: Tamzan
runners.spotlight: Six-race maiden who has largely consistent form on turf/AW, most recently 2l fourth in 1m novice event at Kempton on seasonal debut; again has frame possibilities, being likely to give her running.
runners.comment: Six-race maiden who has largely consistent form; again has frame possibilities

runners.name: La Peregrina
runners.spotlight: Not especially solid on his novice form but didn't fare badly last time, finishing midfield at Southwell (1m, AW); this switch to handicap level may prompt a step forward.
runners.comment: This switch to handicap level may prompt a step forward

runners.name: Eyes Front
runners.spotlight: Maiden who has been campaigned only over 6f/7f; had a consistent spell last summer but not in the same form since; chance partly depends on the effects of the new trip.
runners.comment: Chance partly depends on the effects of the new trip

runners.name: Pepper Fizz
runners.spotlight: Failed to progress last term but this reappearance, combined with a return to Windsor, may be the time to catch her; promising fourth at this venue on debut.
runners.comment: Hasn't progressed but now returns to the scene of his encouraging debut

runners.name: Tinsel
runners.spotlight: Absent since opening his account in Class 6 handicap at Wolverhampton (9.5f, AW) just before Christmas; the only last-time scorer in this field but doesn't give the impression he's crying out for return to 1m.
runners.comment: The manner of his 9.5f AW win suggests this return to 1m is a negative
runners.owner: A Bevan, I McMillan & Glassex Holdings

runners.name: Upsomdowns
runners.spotlight: Has made the frame in three of his four AW starts, creditable second in Lingfield handicap (1m) when last seen four months ago; possibilities off same mark, provided the switch to turf doesn't backfire.
runners.comment: Has made the frame in three of his four AW starts, runner-up in handicap latest

races.race_name: BetWright Safer Gambling Handicap

runners.name: Golden Long
runners.spotlight: Largely consistent over 6f in Hong Kong; beaten favourite, albeit not disgraced, in a couple of 5f contests for new yard since returning from lengthy layoff; may be able to show progress back up in distance; remains of interest at this level.
runners.comment: Consistent over 6f in Hong Kong; beaten fav in 5f events for new yard

runners.name: Lazzar
runners.spotlight: Has broadly done well on AW since December, winning four times; needs to prove he can transfer the pick of that form back to turf; 0-6 in this sphere and is 5lb higher than last attempt.
runners.comment: Needs to prove he can transfer the pick of his AW form back to turf

runners.name: Expert Agent
runners.spotlight: Generally in good form on AW since December and regained the winning thread at Kempton (6f) most recently; goes without usual cheekpieces but that was also the case when he scored over C&D (off 2lb higher) in 2024; warrants respect.
runners.comment: AW success last time; won off 2lb higher over C&D in 2024; respected
runners.owner: J P Morris, Mrs V A Tunnicliffe Et Al

runners.name: Strike
runners.spotlight: Made an encouraging start to the campaign with second-place finish at Bath; likely to remain competitive, having posted form figures of 1122 at Windsor, including a sound effort behind Tiger Tulip last May; hasn't won since 2024 but looks a solid contender.
runners.comment: Record at Windsor reads 1122; solid claims returned to this track
runners.owner: S Emmet And Miss R Emmet

runners.name: Jenever
runners.spotlight: Still to find top form this term but he's attractively handicapped and may prove resurgent with Tom Marquand up for first time; gained last three wins off current mark or thereabouts.
runners.comment: May prove resurgent with Tom Marquand up for the first time

runners.name: Amazonian Dream
runners.spotlight: Has returned to AW this year, best efforts when placed twice; losing run is mounting up but he has loads of Windsor form, including three wins, and returns to this venue off an attractive mark; possibilities.
runners.comment: Has loads of Windsor form and he's on a handy mark; possibilities

runners.name: Bold Impact
runners.spotlight: Not solid on his handicap form; failed to beat a rival in big-field event at Newmarket (second run for new yard) most recently; drop back in grade may help but there are signs that he's regressing.
runners.comment: Not solid on his handicap form; failed to beat a rival last time

runners.name: Seraphim Angel
runners.spotlight: Has a record of 3-13 overall and 2-2 at Windsor, most recently scoring over C&D (good to firm) last July on final start of her 2025 campaign; finished last at Kempton (AW) on stable/seasonal debut but looks interesting returned to this venue with the outing under her belt.
runners.comment: 2-2 at Windsor; interesting back here on second start for new stable

runners.name: Addictive
runners.spotlight: Four-race maiden in 2024 for George Boughey, close third in Chepstow handicap (5f) the final time; sold for 6,500gns and absent since; market signals may offer further guidance.
runners.comment: Absent since 2024; debut for new stable; market instructive

runners.name: Tiger Tulip
runners.spotlight: Raced on unfavoured part of the track and eased once beaten at Goodwood two days ago on seasonal debut (effort best forgiven); can enter calculations on plenty of 2025 form, including a May success (beat Strike) over C&D.
runners.comment: Enters calculations on plenty of 2025 form, including May win over C&D

runners.name: Land Of Magic
runners.spotlight: Best form last term when winning off 1lb higher at Lingfield in May; poor reappearance record augurs badly for this seasonal debut and she has never scored above Class 5; opposed.
runners.comment: Poor reappearance record augurs badly for this seasonal debut

races.race_name: Betwright Bet The Wright Way Handicap

runners.name: Filly Foden
runners.spotlight: Pacy filly who registered back-to-back wins at Southwell and Lingfield (both 5f) earlier this year; disappointing in her next three runs but she got back on track with a front-running third at Wolverhampton (6f) 13 days ago; has already had 21 runs during her career but she has plenty of form on turf and could make a bold bid back in trip.
runners.comment: Exposed filly but she was placed at Wolverhampton latest and has claims back in trip

runners.name: Queen Sana
runners.spotlight: Showed ability when in the frame in four of her first five starts, but she didn't run very well on her handicap debut at Southwell (6f, Tapeta) last month; doesn't seem to be progressing and needs the drop to 5f to make a difference.
runners.comment: Doesn't seem to be progressing and needs the drop to 5f to make a difference

runners.name: Overbudget
runners.spotlight: 115,000euros yearling; finished runner-up in all three starts but she was no match for the winner on her Wolverhampton reappearance (5f, AW) in March; needs to step up again after that odds-on defeat but it's still early days and she's in the mix on handicap debut; tongue-tie added.
runners.comment: Runner-up in all three starts and she's in the mix with tongue-tie added on handicap debut
runners.owner: Matt Broughton & Hold My Beer Syndicate

runners.name: Wild Act
runners.spotlight: Showed some promise when third in a Lingfield novice (6f, AW) in December but he reappeared with 14l defeat at Leicester (6f, good) last month; still early days and now goes handicapping but he needs to resume his progress back in trip.
runners.comment: Mixed form in his qualifying runs and needs to resume his progress on handicap debut

runners.name: Mehmas Engine
runners.spotlight: Promising third on Southwell debut (5f, AW) in February but she's taken two backward steps since and was beaten 13l at Nottingham (6f, good) last month; still unexposed and now goes handicapping back in trip but she has something to prove; cheekpieces added.
runners.comment: Regressive form in her qualifying runs and has something to prove on handicap debut

runners.name: Blue Deveron
runners.spotlight: Unexposed gelding who has form figures of 2333 and that latest third was a close call on handicap debut at Southwell (5f, AW) 11 days ago; this is a deeper race but he rallied well last time and is respected on this switch to turf; one of two runners for James Tate.
runners.comment: Has record of 2333 and that latest third was a close call on handicap debut at Southwell

runners.name: Staniel Cay
runners.spotlight: Improved form dropped back to 5f in his second handicap when beating 16 rivals at Nottingham (good; 6-1) last month; up 7lb but that was a comfortable win and this well-related colt is open to more progress; big player.
runners.comment: Beat 16 rivals at Nottingham last month and he's open to more progress; big player

runners.name: Stock Market
runners.spotlight: Maiden who showed modest form for Aidan O'Brien and has form figures of 8980 in four handicaps for his current yard; has lots to prove and he needs a transformation back in trip.
runners.comment: Nine-race maiden who has form figures of 8980 for his current yard; lots to prove

runners.name: Coyy
runners.spotlight: Showed clear promise in his qualifying runs but he's finished in rear in three handicaps, including a heavy defeat after wind surgery at Bath (5.7f, good) last month; dropped another 3lb but needs a major revival back in trip.
runners.comment: Six-race maiden who has struggled in three handicaps and needs a big turnaround

runners.name: Truly Glamorous
runners.spotlight: Improved form when runner-up on nursery debut at Chepstow (5f, good) last August but he flopped at Bath (last of six) in his final run for John O'Shea; been gelded since and has bit to prove for new yard after 233 days off.
runners.comment: Flopped in final 2yo run and has bit to prove for new yard after 233 days off; been gelded

runners.name: Too Darn Good
runners.spotlight: 0-6 as 2yo for Charlie Hills but he ran some promising races including when fourth in a C&D novice last summer and third in a Kempton nursery (6f, AW) in October; returns for new yard after 166 days off and interesting to see how he figures in the market.
runners.comment: 0-6 but he ran some promising races as 2yo; needs watching in market on stable debut
runners.owner: K Hunter, D Leon And Hunscote Stud Ltd

races.race_name: Daily Prize Wheel At Betwright.com Handicap (Div I)

runners.name: The Ubermensch
runners.spotlight: Unexposed 4yo who started his handicap career with a close call at Wolverhampton (8.6f, AW) last month; never dangerous at Yarmouth (1m, good) five days later but he ran into some trouble and was only beaten around 3l; has shaped as though this step up in trip should suit and he's an interesting contender with Tom Marquand booked.
runners.comment: Unexposed 4yo and he could resume his progress at this new trip; interesting contender

runners.name: Beachborough Girl
runners.spotlight: Made winning stable debut at Chelmsford (1m2f, AW) in January and she's held her form with three solid placed efforts since; 2lb higher than when runner-up at Wolverhampton last time but that was a bold bid and she's still unexposed on turf; respected back in this sphere.
runners.comment: In good form on AW for new yard and she's respected back on turf

runners.name: Boubyan
runners.spotlight: Has a patchy profile but he won at Newcastle (1m2f, AW) in March and was a strong-finishing second at Pontefract (1m, good) last Wednesday; on same mark here and he's a key player on this step back up in trip.
runners.comment: Close second off this mark at Pontefract last week and he's a big player back up in trip

runners.name: Alta Rica
runners.spotlight: Went close at Lingfield (1m2f, AW) in January but she's not matched that form in three subsequent runs; ran respectably at Leicester (1m4f, good) three weeks ago but she's now 0-9 and needs to find more back in trip.
runners.comment: Went close at Lingfield (1m2f) in January but she's not matched that form since

runners.name: Masqool
runners.spotlight: 11-time AW scorer but sole turf win was three years ago; some creditable efforts on Polytrack for new yard this year but he's been out of sorts at Wolverhampton in last two runs; has some questions to answer and he looks opposable back on turf.
runners.comment: Sole turf win was in 2023 and he's been out of sorts on Tapeta recently

runners.name: Cartwheel
runners.spotlight: Kicked off her 2025 campaign in the Group 3 Nell Gwyn Stakes and didn't run too badly; has had six starts in handicaps since but she's struggled in all of them including four runs this year; mark has nosedived but she's has a lot to prove at this new trip; hood removed.
runners.comment: Has struggled in all six handicaps and has a lot to prove at this new trip

runners.name: Lost In Wonder
runners.spotlight: Mixed results in four 2yo runs for Jack Jones and she ended last season with a heavy defeat in a Nottingham nursery (8.3f); that was in the mud but she returns after 193 days off and is probably best watched on stable debut.
runners.comment: Mixed record in four 2yo runs and she's probably best watched on stable debut

runners.name: Bridge
runners.spotlight: Bounced back from a poor run and rewarded strong market support when scoring by 7l over hurdles at Fontwell (2m1f, good to soft) in March; his last Flat run was in July 2023 but he showed clear promise as a juvenile and he's still lightly raced in this sphere; one to keep an eye on.
runners.comment: Won by 7l over hurdles last time and he needs watching in market back on the Flat

runners.name: Drumstick
runners.spotlight: Runner-up off 1lb higher at Kempton (1m, AW) last July but he's been well held in last two Flat runs and was tailed off on his hurdling/stable debut in September; overall record is now 0-15 and has something to prove on his return.
runners.comment: 15-race maiden and he has something to prove after 217 days off

races.race_name: Daily Prize Wheel At Betwright.com Handicap (Div II)

runners.name: Moonlit Cloud
runners.spotlight: No win since May 2024 (1m2f, Bath) but she has fallen a long way in the weights as a result; may have needed her reappearance run at Wolverhampton (9.4f) last month but she had a patchy record last season and others are more convincing.
runners.comment: On reduced mark but she's not won since May 2024 and is hard to predict; down the list

runners.name: Seventy
runners.spotlight: Made the breakthrough at Lingfield (1m4f, AW) in February and he's backed that up with two solid placed efforts on Polytrack (1m2f/1m3f); now returns to turf but he's unexposed in this sphere and is a key player on this drop back in grade.
runners.comment: Won at Lingfield in February and placed in both subsequent runs; respected back on turf

runners.name: Fast Steps
runners.spotlight: C&D winner who is versatile ground-wise; well handicapped on best form last season but he was out of sorts in the autumn and needs a major revival after six months off.
runners.comment: C&D winner but he lost his way in the autumn and needs a major revival on return

runners.name: Revich
runners.spotlight: All seven wins have been on good or slower ground on turf; last success was in 2023 but he's well treated on old form and made some late gains when sixth of 15 at Nottingham (8.3f, good) last month; wasn't beaten far in that race and he could go well again on this step back up in trip.
runners.comment: On long losing run but he caught the eye at Nottingham (8.3f) last time; not ruled out

runners.name: Lady Of The Isles
runners.spotlight: Six-race maiden who has finished a creditable fourth in two of her handicaps including in a big field at Leicester (1m2f, good) in October; on same mark on her return and she's a player if she can find some extra spark; first-time blinkers replace a hood.
runners.comment: Reached the frame in two of her three handicaps and she's in the mix on her return

runners.name: Gone Rogue
runners.spotlight: Snapped a long losing run when scoring at Brighton (1m, good to soft) in October and he ended last season with a close call at Bath (heavy); has claims if he can pick up where he left off but he's never won beyond 1m and still has stamina to prove at this trip.
runners.comment: In good form in the autumn but he has bit to prove back at this trip on his return

runners.name: Blackwaterfoot
runners.spotlight: Low-mileage 5yo who won at Ffos Las (1m2f, good) last July but that was in a small field and he struggled in his final two runs for Paul Attwater; has something to prove after 193 days off and he needs to turn things around for new yard.
runners.comment: Low-mileage 5yo but he needs to turn things around on his stable debut

runners.name: Man From Havana
runners.spotlight: 22-1 or bigger in three novice/maiden events last season but he showed ability when fifth of 14 here (1m, good) on his second start; returns after 201 days off but this is more realistic on handicap debut and he's a possible improver upped in trip.
runners.comment: Handicap newcomer and he's a possible improver upped in trip on his return
runners.owner: P J & Mrs J P Haycock

runners.name: Zhang Fei
runners.spotlight: 8yo who has struggled back over hurdles since rejoining Fergal O'Brien in the autumn; his sole Flat win came at this track (11.4f, good to firm) but that was in 2022 and he has something to prove back in this sphere; hood reapplied.
runners.comment: Struggled back over hurdles since rejoining current yard and sole Flat win was in 2022

races.race_name: Ballygowan Maiden Hurdle

runners.name: Theflyingking
runners.spotlight: Point winner won a Wexford bumper last year; looked like the winner when fallling at the last on hurdles debut over 2m6f here last October; was then narrowly beaten by subsequent Grade 1 winner I'll Sort That in a Navan Grade 3 over 2m; will be difficult to beat if repeating that form on return.
runners.comment: Just beaten by subsequent Grade 1 winner I'll Sort That in a Navan Grade 3; hard to beat

runners.name: Astelia
runners.spotlight: Third runner for yard; Flat maiden ran some fine races in defeat over hurdles last year but was below form last two starts; rated 106 so has a bit to find, but capable of a decent showing on return.
runners.comment: Rated 106 so has a bit to find but capable of a decent showing on return

runners.name: Ehteyat
runners.spotlight: Three-time Flat winner has run well in defeat in maiden hurdles this year, including over C&D; below form in an Aintree handicap last time, but this isn't as competitive and he is one to consider.
runners.comment: Below form in an Aintree handicap last time but this isn't as competitive and considered

runners.name: Berto Ramirez
runners.spotlight: A 69-rated Flat maiden; the best of four hurdle runs when beaten 15l in a Naas Listed juvenile event; beaten a long way in the Triumph Hurdle since; can go well but others look stronger.
runners.comment: Some ability in four hurdle runs but beaten a long way in the Triumph Hurdle last time
runners.owner: D O'Mahony & D McHugh & D J Kelly & G Butler

runners.name: Unknown Fortune
runners.spotlight: Third foal; dam 2m4f hurdle winner, half-sister to winners Don't Touch It (smart bumper and 2m-2m4f hurdle/chase) and Lackaneen Leader (bumper and useful 2m-2m6f hurdle); by a useful sire but unsold for E14,000 as a 3yo; one to watch for future reference.
runners.comment: Unsold for E14,000 as a 3yo; one to watch for future reference

runners.name: Angelo Dundee
runners.spotlight: Punchestown bumper winner on debut in 2023 for Dermot McLoughlin; has had an interrupted career since, but has run respectably in four winners' bumpers in that time; worth a market check on hurdles/stable debut after another break.
runners.comment: Decent form in bumpers; worth a market check on hurdles/stable debut after another break

runners.name: O'Reilly
runners.spotlight: An 80-rated Flat winner ran fine races in his first three hurdle attempts last autumn but was poor in a hood at this track when last seen; however, has a chance if restored to best on return; first-string for a yard that had a Punchestown winner.
runners.comment: Good runs first three hurdle tries but poor here when last seen; chance if back to best

runners.name: Brave Lady
runners.spotlight: Second reserve; fourth runner for yard; fine third on debut in a Galway bumper last August but a tough task when well beaten in an Aintree Grade 2 bumper on return; others look stronger.
runners.comment: Well beaten in an Aintree Grade 2 bumper on return; others look stronger; reserve

runners.name: Oriole
runners.spotlight: Three-time turf winner on the Flat is rated 73 in that sphere; sire gets jumps winners so of some interest on hurdles debut after a break.
runners.comment: Flat winner; sire gets jumps winners so of some interest on hurdles debut after a break

runners.name: Glendaars Secret
runners.spotlight: Third foal; half-brother to Scandinavian 4.5f-6f winner Archers Ignite; unraced dam out of 6f Listed winning half-sister to prolific 5f winner Lucky Parkes and Group-placed 5f winner My Melody Parkes; by a jumps sire but speed on damline; best watched on debut.
runners.comment: By a jumps sire but speed on damline; best watched on debut

runners.name: Mollys Star
runners.spotlight: Showed up well enough in a Cork bumper on second start but well beaten in maiden hurdles either side of that; hood on; best watched.
runners.comment: Showed some ability in a Cork bumper but well beaten in maiden hurdles either side of that

runners.name: Hathaways Cottage
runners.spotlight: Five-time point winner ran well in a hunters chase here in March but was well beaten in a maiden hurdle since; others appeal more.
runners.comment: Ran well in a hunters chase here in March but was well beaten in a maiden hurdle since

runners.name: Loyal Touch
runners.spotlight: One of two runners for yard; five Flat wins when trained in UK; regressive for this yard, though running well at times; could go well on hurdles debut if jumping holds up.
runners.comment: Regressive on Flat but not running badly; could go well on hurdles debut if jumping okay

runners.name: Persian Bliss
runners.spotlight: Second runner for yard; three-time AW winner ran well enough on that surface on second start for this yard; was well below his best on sole turf run, but has the class to run well if jumping soundly.
runners.comment: Well below his best on sole turf run but has the class to run well if jumping soundly

runners.name: Cinnabar Moth
runners.spotlight: A hint of ability in three bumpers, but well beaten on hurdles debut and best watched for now.
runners.comment: A hint of ability in three bumpers but well beaten on hurdles debut and best watched

runners.name: Betty's Glen
runners.spotlight: Third reserve; beaten a long way in a bumper on debut and can only be watched for now.
runners.comment: Beaten a long way in a bumper on debut and can only be watched for now; reserve

runners.name: Small Fry
runners.spotlight: Five Flat wins in UK and ran respectably both starts for this yard last autumn after 100,000gns purchase; just modest form both hurdle runs in UK, but could do better now that he is more mature; check for market strength.
runners.comment: Useful on Flat; modest both hurdle runs in UK but could do better now is more mature

runners.name: King Of The Greys
runners.spotlight: Second runner for yard; some ability on sole point start; the best of two maiden hurdle runs was at Tramore last time; loads to find.
runners.comment: The best of two maiden hurdle runs was at Tramore last time; loads to find

runners.name: Certamen
runners.spotlight: Showed some promise in a decent Thurles bumper on debut and not bad efforts in two maiden hurdles last autumn; could run respectably if ready on return.
runners.comment: Not bad runs in two maiden hurdles last autumn; could run respectably if ready on return

runners.name: Clone Cross
runners.spotlight: A maiden in points and poor in two maiden hurdles, so look elsewhere.
runners.comment: A maiden in points and poor in two maiden hurdles so look elsewhere

runners.name: Brohatna
runners.spotlight: First reserve; beaten 75l on debut over C&D in January and is best watched for now.
runners.comment: Beaten 75l on debut over C&D in January and is best watched for now; reserve

runners.name: Sneddy Eddie
runners.spotlight: Three-time Flat winner has been beaten a long way in three maiden hurdles and best wait for handicaps; hood added
runners.comment: Beaten a long way in three maiden hurdles and best wait for handicaps; hood added

runners.name: Victoria Kesia
runners.spotlight: A 1m maiden winner on the Flat; some promise on hurdling debut but was poor next time; wait for handicaps.
runners.comment: Some promise on hurdling debut but was poor next time; wait for handicaps

races.race_name: Club Lemon Handicap Hurdle

runners.name: Bella Union
runners.spotlight: Off the mark at the age of 10 when winning (sent off 22-1) over this trip at Bellewstown; claimer negates penalty but whether he'll back up that improved effort is anyone's guess.
runners.comment: Off the mark at the age of 10 when winning over this trip at Bellewstown

runners.name: Tea It Is
runners.spotlight: Yet to win but plenty of placed form in good-ground handicaps last summer; better for last month's return and could place.
runners.comment: Yet to win but plenty of placed form in good-ground handicaps last summer

runners.name: Truckers Cruising
runners.spotlight: Transferred from Liam Burke's yard to Gordon Elliott last year and first couple of handicap hurdle starts were okay; won heavy-ground chase at Ayr in December; rated 15lb lower back over hurdles here on first start since Christmas.
runners.comment: Won heavy-ground chase at Ayr in December; rated 15lb lower back over hurdles
runners.owner: Ed Mulligan & John Finnegan & Aileen Saunders & Ann Holton

runners.name: Justicialism
runners.spotlight: Over four years since he won back-to-back handicap hurdles for Brian Hamilton; lightly raced in last couple of seasons and mark is much lower nowadays; bounced back to form on second start for Noel Kelly when second here over 2m4f; this shorter trip possibly not ideal, but obvious claims.
runners.comment: Bounced back to form on second start for Noel Kelly when second here over 2m4f

runners.name: Leitzel
runners.spotlight: Placed form in maidens and in Ballinrobe handicap for Andrew McNamara and likes good ground; after long absence, tailed off over 2m4f here on yard debut.
runners.comment: Placed form for Andrew McNamara and likes good ground; tailed off over 2m4f on yard debut

runners.name: Lakefield Flyer
runners.spotlight: Third reserve; second in this in 2023; two chase wins at Downpatrick since and likes good ground; off seven months and hasn't run over hurdles since 2024.
runners.comment: Second in this in 2023; two chase wins at Downpatrick since and likes good ground; reserve

runners.name: Stay In The Game
runners.spotlight: Some fair Flat form and fourth in a pair of maiden hurdles last year; burst blood vessel at Cheltenham in November; soft ground was against him on last month's handicap debut.
runners.comment: Fourth in a pair of maiden hurdles last year; soft ground was against him on h'cap debut

runners.name: Small Bucks
runners.spotlight: 33-1 winner of second handicap start at Cork last summer (2m1f, good); not much since, soft ground proabably against her at Leopardstown in March; tongue-tie added.
runners.comment: 33-1 winner of second handicap start at Cork last summer (2m1f, good); not much since

runners.name: Soldier Saint
runners.spotlight: Was E90,000 store and runner-up in Hereford bumper for Nicky Henderson (beaten 2l by winner who is now a 118-rated hurdler); was picked up for £18,000 by Noel Kelly and had creditable run in C&D maiden; tailed off on handicap debut in December and has changed yards again since.
runners.comment: Runner-up in bumper for Nicky Henderson and creditable run in C&D maiden; new yard

runners.name: Harley Street
runners.spotlight: Ran well off 103 at 2024 Galway festival; off the bridle from early when plugging on for third at Wexford (2m4f) in October; poor since; should enjoy the ground, but might find trip a bit sharp.
runners.comment: Nearest at the finish when placed over 2m4f at Wexford in the autumn; not much since

runners.name: Tellmemamema
runners.spotlight: Related to winners and first maiden run was respectable; one to note on handicap debut, especially if money comes.
runners.comment: Related to winners and first maiden run was respectable; one to note on handicap debut

runners.name: Gforcejenny
runners.spotlight: Okay Flat form and placed in juvenile hurdle at Listowel festival; could be on a fair mark but off seven months.
runners.comment: Okay Flat form and placed in juvenile hurdle at Listowel festival; could be on a fair mark

runners.name: Powerful Rosie
runners.spotlight: Half-brother to AW winner; 40s for handicap debut at Clonmel, not beaten far in sixth on soft terrain and better ground should suit.
runners.comment: 40s for handicap debut at Clonmel, not beaten far in sixth and better ground should suit

runners.name: Portetta
runners.spotlight: Second in a Tipperary maiden on decent ground on hurdling debut a year ago; some fair efforts after that and heavy ground was no good to her on handicap over Christmas.
runners.comment: Second in Tipperary maiden on decent ground last year; heavy didn't suit on h'cap debut

runners.name: Lejolicoeur Du Mou
runners.spotlight: Fair sixth over C&D on soft; only 7-1 for Cork handicap last time where faded out of contention but still had plenty of rivals behind him; new headgear tried.
runners.comment: Sixth over C&D and only 7-1 for Cork handicap where faded out of contention; new headgear

runners.name: Chesterbaylad
runners.spotlight: Last year's fifth in this, when hooded for the first time, was his best effort so far; also ran okay here at Christmas, so clearly likes the place.
runners.comment: Last year's fifth in this, when hooded for the first time, was his best effort so far

runners.name: Light Her Up
runners.spotlight: First reserve; fair sixth on handicap debut (2m, good to yielding); 2m4f on soft ground didn't suit her when last seen in December.
runners.comment: Fair sixth on handicap debut (2m, good to yielding); 2m4f didn't suit last time; reserve

runners.name: Michael's Jet
runners.spotlight: Second runner for yard; 50-1 for recent handicap debut at Kilbeggan, pulled up but did have an excuse as saddle slipped at the start.
runners.comment: 50-1 when pulled up on h'cap debut but had an excuse as saddle slipped at the start

runners.name: Kilt
runners.spotlight: Second reserve; useful Flat form as 3yo; fair seventh on second handicap hurdle start at Fairyhouse in December; tailed off on last month's return.
runners.comment: Useful Flat form as 3yo; fair seventh on second handicap hurdle start; reserve

runners.name: Is She Now
runners.spotlight: Modest stayer on the Flat; beaten 9l when sixth of 15 at Bellewstown last summer on only handicap start over hurdles.
runners.comment: Beaten 9l when sixth of 15 at Bellewstown last summer on only handicap start

runners.name: Grey Gardens
runners.spotlight: Placed in modest Limerick maiden (2m2f, heavy) in November; off five months and has probably been kept for a summer campaign; yard won this last year.
runners.comment: Placed in modest Limerick maiden (2m2f, heavy); probably been kept for a summer campaign

runners.name: Move It On Over
runners.spotlight: Was 100-1 but only beaten 15l on handicap debut at Ballinrobe when last seen in August.
runners.comment: Was 100-1 but only beaten 15l on handicap debut at Ballinrobe when last seen in August

runners.name: Crypto Crash
runners.spotlight: Second over 1m4f on fast ground for David Marnane; first time to get nice ground hurdling, and disappointing if he can't show a bit more.
runners.comment: Second over 1m4f on fast ground for David Marnane; first time to get nice ground hurdling

races.race_name: Pepsi Max Hurdle

runners.name: Timeless Treaty
runners.spotlight: C&D maiden winner has won Punchestown and Thurles novices since; fine efforts in defeat in two novices and a Fairyhouse handicap last three starts up to 2m7f; has won on good ground, so no fears in that respect, and looks the one to beat.
runners.comment: Fine efforts in defeat in novices and a Fairyhouse handicap last three starts; one to beat

runners.name: He Can't Dance
runners.spotlight: Second runner for yard; bumper winner got off the mark over hurdles on seventh try at Limerick last time; that was over this trip on heavy ground but has run well on a sounder surface; looks the main threat to stablemate Timeless Treaty.
runners.comment: Won over this trip on hvy ground at Limerick but has run well on a sounder surface; player

runners.name: Herja
runners.spotlight: Won a 2m4f Sedgefield maiden hurdle last September; twice well beaten over fences but mostly solid efforts in defeat over hurdles since; has a bit to find with some of these.
runners.comment: Maiden winner last year; mostly solid in defeat since; bit to find with some of these

runners.name: Kevin's Pride
runners.spotlight: Bumper winner in 2024 got off the mark over hurdles in a Cork maiden over 2m4f last October; didn't jump well on chasing debut last time but can do better back in this sphere, though has a bit to find with the Elliott pair; cheekpieces.
runners.comment: Didn't jump well chasing last time; can do better back in this sphere but a bit to find

runners.name: Lark Walk
runners.spotlight: Was beaten when falling at the last on recent point debut; can only be watched on rules bow with a hood added.
runners.comment: Beaten when falling at the last on recent point debut; can only be watched

runners.name: Love Like This
runners.spotlight: Second runner for yard; maiden is rated only 82, so can't be entertained in this company.
runners.comment: Maiden is rated only 82 so can't be entertained in this company

runners.name: Huntsgrove
runners.spotlight: Maiden hurdle winner last year, but was out of his depth in smart novice company when last seen; rated only 100 so has loads to find with some of these.
runners.comment: Rated only 100 so has loads to find with some of these

races.race_name: Club Orange Beginners Chase

runners.name: Staffordshire Knot
runners.spotlight: Quality sort reached his peak when winning a Navan Grade 2 hurdle over 2m6f in February; however, jumped badly right and was poor in the Pertemps Final last time; posted two decent chase runs in 2024 and should be difficult to beat if jumping soundly.
runners.comment: Grade 2 hurdle winner but was poor last time; two decent chase runs in 2024; hard to beat

runners.name: Scalpnagoon
runners.spotlight: Second runner for yard; a 122-rated hurdler won twice over hurdles last year before losing his form badly; ran well enough behind a subsequent Punchestown winner at Cork on second chase start; can go well.
runners.comment: A 122-rated hurdler ran well enough behind a Punchestown winner at Cork; can go well

runners.name: Cinammon Roll
runners.spotlight: One of three runners for the yard; point and dual hurdles winner was below form when last seen but is rated 126 over hurdles; can run well on chase bow if ready after a lay-off.
runners.comment: Point and dual hurdle winner was below form when last seen; can run well on chase bow

runners.name: Takenasred
runners.spotlight: Maiden over hurdles but some decent runs including here in March; however, was poor in a Fairyhouse handicap last time; a fair bit to find on chase bow.
runners.comment: Maiden over hurdles but some decent runs; poor in a Fairyhouse h'cap last time; chase bow

runners.name: Court Canyon
runners.spotlight: Point winner won a Clonmel maiden hurdle over 2m4f last October; is rated 111 over hurdles but was well beaten last time in a Fairyhouse handicap after a break; has a fair bit to find on chasing debut.
runners.comment: Point winner is rated 111 over hurdles but well beaten last time; fair bit to find

runners.name: Impero
runners.spotlight: Five hurdle wins and rated 136 in that sphere; was poor on the Flat when last in action at the 2025 Galway festival; makes chasing bow now and will have to be ready after that absence.
runners.comment: Five hurdle wins and rated 136 in that sphere; chasing bow on first run for a while

runners.name: Astley
runners.spotlight: Maiden ran a cracker to be second on chasing debut here but wasn't as good since at Kilbeggan; has a bit to find with some of these.
runners.comment: Ran a cracker on chasing debut here but not as good since; bit to find with some of these

runners.name: Sampoet
runners.spotlight: Is 1-16 in points but rated only 94 over fences and has a lot to find with some of these, so can't be fancied.
runners.comment: Is 1-16 in points but rated only 94 over fences and has a lot to find with some of these

races.race_name: Club Mixers Handicap Chase

runners.name: Hardy Diamond
runners.spotlight: Mare likes good ground and has won over hurdles and fences at Kilbeggan; chase win was off 107 last summer and stuck to her guns when second over C&D in lower-grade contest last time.
runners.comment: Likes good ground and second over C&D in lower-grade contest last month

runners.name: Attaboyarchie
runners.spotlight: Long-standing maiden but not had that many goes over fences and has shown more lately; second over C&D last month; owner usually rides, but professional takes over today.
runners.comment: Second for owner-rider over C&D last time; now has the benefit of professional handling

runners.name: Ad Caelum
runners.spotlight: Four wins over hurdles and some of her early chase runs last autumn were promising; won hurdle over this trip at Ballinrobe and likes good ground, so better expected this time.
runners.comment: Recent form not great, but trip and ground will suit and a better performance quite likely

runners.name: Tullyveery Lad
runners.spotlight: Record of 3-11 over fences and will enjoy the ground; has a recent comeback run under his belt too, and was second over just a bit shorter than this trip at Wexford last year.
runners.comment: 3-11 over fences and will enjoy the ground; has a recent comeback run under his belt too
runners.owner: Pat Tennyson & N P Morrissey & Raymond Micheal Cody

runners.name: Digby
runners.spotlight: He's a different horse on good ground, so forget his recent form figures on soft; won twice this time last year and excellent second over this trip in valuable handicap at Uttoxeter; revival expected here with conditions to suit and yard had welcome high-profile winner on Saturday.
runners.comment: Better signs from the yard's runners lately and trip and gound will be perfect for him

runners.name: Secrecies Of Stone
runners.spotlight: Won over extended 2m5f last year and was leading when exiting two out on his next start; hasn't been finishing out his races lately and not sure this longer trip will help matters.
runners.comment: Won over extended 2m5f last year; one completion from four starts since; tries new trip

runners.name: Bodhisattva
runners.spotlight: Stays all day but just 1-24 over fences and that win was nearly three years ago; maybe recent banks' run will have perked him up, but others preferred.
runners.comment: Stays all day but just 1-24 over fences ; maybe recent banks' run will have perked him up

runners.name: Barrow Ranger
runners.spotlight: Won a point shortly before making handicap chase debut at Thurles in February, where he showed up well for a long way; third in 3m1f hurdle at Plumpton last month and he'll like the ground.
runners.comment: Point winner was third in 3m1f hurdle at Plumpton last month and he'll like the ground

runners.name: Goody Four Shoes
runners.spotlight: Some fair efforts hurdling and ran okay in both beginners' chases; 9-1 for handicap bow over C&D last month, disputing lead when fell 4 out.
runners.comment: Ran okay in both beginners' chases; 9-1 for h'cap bow over C&D, fell 4 out when in front

races.race_name: C&C White Maiden Hunters Chase

runners.name: Focus Point
runners.spotlight: Seven point wins; has run well at times in hunter chases, including when runner-up over 2m5f here in March; however, was poor at Cork since and beaten in a point nine days ago; needs best.
runners.comment: Seven point wins; has run well at times in hunter chases but not at Cork last time

runners.name: Catalani
runners.spotlight: Three point wins, the latest coming last November; was beaten when fell on hunter chase debut at Thurles; better effort when 17l behind a decent type in a Tramore hunters' last time; can run well.
runners.comment: Three point wins; beaten 17l by a decent type in a Tramore hunters last time; run well

runners.name: Yewtree Hill
runners.spotlight: Has posted four point wins and was a good second last time in a Tattersalls open; pulled-up on last hunter chase start, so has to rebound from that.
runners.comment: Four point wins and a good 2nd last time; pulled-up on last hunter chase start

runners.name: Fortune Lad
runners.spotlight: Won his third point by a long way last time at Loughanmore, albeit against just two rivals; comes from a respected yard that does well in these events and could well be up to winning.
runners.comment: Won his third point by a long way last time; comes from a respected yard; of interest

runners.name: Colvin
runners.spotlight: Is 1-8 in points and was poor last time; maiden over hurdles but his best run came at this track last year; others preferred.
runners.comment: Is 1-8 in points and poor last time; maiden over hurdles; others preferred

runners.name: That's It Paddy
runners.spotlight: Was fourth on point debut but was pulled-up last time; this looks a big ask for one with his lack of experience.
runners.comment: Fourth on point debut but pulled-up last time; this looks a bit ask

runners.name: Chateau Elan
runners.spotlight: Yet to win a point in nine starts; showed a hint of ability under rules; has a long absence to overcome; unlikely.
runners.comment: Yet to win a point; a hint of ability under rules; long absence to overcome

races.race_name: Energise (Pro/Am) INH Flat Race

runners.name: Todo Bien
runners.spotlight: Derby Sale graduate made E175,000 then; half-brother to Flat and jumps winners; 5-4 fav for Naas debut in February, beaten less than 6l when third of five on soft-to-heavy; the pair in front of him were first and third in Listed bumper next time; very different ground here, but sets a good standard.
runners.comment: Made E175,000 at Derby Sale; 5-4 fav when third to pair of useful types at Naas

runners.name: Hot Rock Harry
runners.spotlight: Top yard but cost only E2,000 as a foal; half-brother to Listed-placed 7f/8.6f AW winner Muddy Mooy (RPR 104); dam unraced sister to 1m4f/1m6f Listed winner Hasanka, out of 1m2f Listed winner; no surprise if he contends.
runners.comment: Top yard but only made E2,000 as a foal; half-brother to Listed-placed 7f/8.6f AW winner

runners.name: Weaponized
runners.spotlight: Second foal of bumper-winning sister to 2m5f/3m1f chase winner In Great Form, half-sister to 2m4f/2m5f chase winner Moodofthemoment; homebred filly has hood on for debut.
runners.comment: Second foal of bumper-winning sister to 2m5f/3m1f chase winner; has hood on for debut.

runners.name: Eillis
runners.spotlight: Made only E1,500 as a foal; third foal; half-sister to first-time-out bumper winner So Proud and Cyrano (Polish 6f 2yo); dam unraced close relative of 12.5f-2m2f Flat and 2m/2m4f hurdle winner Dinard Rose, out of 7f 2yo/9.6f winning half-sister to dam of Melbourne Cup winner Fiorente; local yard's bumper runners always respected, particularly here.
runners.comment: Half-sister to first-time-out bumper winner and yard's bumper runners always respected,

runners.name: Coolnagrattan
runners.spotlight: Third foal; dam 9.5f/10.4f winner (RPR 75), half-sister to 1m Listed winner Bravo Zolo, out of 6f 2yo/7f winner; heaedgear combination suggests he's a keen-going sort.
runners.comment: Poets Word gelding; dam 9.5f/10.4f winner (RPR 75), half-sister to 1m Listed winner

runners.name: Alfred Wincham
runners.spotlight: Well-bred son of Dubawi picked up for 4,000gns from Godolphin; brother to 7f 2yo winner on AW; half-brother to three other winners, notably Group 2 Hardwicke winner Fanny Logan; trainer and jockey have good record when they combine in bumpers.
runners.comment: Well-bred sort picked up for 4,000gns from Godolphin; half-brother to Group 2 winner

runners.name: Beacon Bypass
runners.spotlight: Gelding by Old Persian; E16,000 F, E8,000 3yo; first foal; dam unraced half-sister to winners Ronald Pump (smart 2m4f-3m hurdle/chase) and Yeavering Belle (bumper and 2m1f-2m7f hurdle); well beaten in a point last month.
runners.comment: Gelding by Old Persian; dam unraced half-sister to Ronald Pump; well beaten in a point

runners.name: Charles Kickham
runners.spotlight: Second foal out of bumper/2m5f hurdle winner, who herself is out of unraced half-sister to 2m hurdle winner.
runners.comment: Second foal out of bumper/2m5f hurdle winner, herself out of half-sister to hurdle winner

runners.name: Drumshanbo
runners.spotlight: Sold for E22,000 as a foal; half-brother to five winners inc Indy Five (2m3f-3m2f hurdle/chase), Oscer Romero (2m4f-3m1f hurdle/chase) and Hurricane Georgie (useful 2m2f-3m1f hurdle/chase); dam unraced half-sister to useful 2m1f-2m4f jumps winner Father Matt.
runners.comment: Sold for E22,000 as a foal; half-brother to some useful stayers over jumps

runners.name: Four Pillars
runners.spotlight: Led out unsold for E9,500 as 3yo; fourth foal; unraced dam out of point winning half-sister to 2m4f hurdle winner Best All Stars; second runner for Ross Crawford.
runners.comment: Son of Pillar Coral; unraced dam out of point-winning half-sister to 2m4f hurdle winner

runners.name: Noble Trix
runners.spotlight: Sold for E29,000 as a foal, E28,000 3yo; third foal; half-brother Flying Trix won this first-time-out last year and has since won over hurdles in France; dam won bumper only start, closely related to 2m/2m1f hurdle winner Ocras Mor, half-sister to high-class staying chaser Aces Four.
runners.comment: Half-brother to bumper/2m3f hurdle winner Flying Trix and dam won a bumper too

runners.name: Rossnowlagh
runners.spotlight: Was sold for E52,000 as a foal and quite a nice page; first foal; dam unplaced 1m1f-1m6f (RPR 51), closely related to winners Nearly Caught (1m7f Group 2) and Katie's Reign (7f 2yo), half-sister to winners Pale Mimosa (2m Group 2) and Suailce (useful 1m4f/2m); second runner for Keith Watson.
runners.comment: Was sold for E52,000 as a foal and quite a nice page; family of Pale Mimosa (2m Group 2)

runners.name: Born To Caledonie
runners.spotlight: Only 9-1 for Leopardstown debut when beaten 17l on soft-to-heavy; winner followed up at Punchestown a few days ago and this filly might enjoy the better ground here.
runners.comment: Only 9-1 for Leopardstown debut when beaten 17l on soft-to-heavy; faster ground may help

races.race_name: Lee Shaughnessy 60th Birthday Amateur Jockeys' Handicap

runners.name: Ciotog
runners.spotlight: Four C&D wins on good or slower (handles good to firm, not at best in one run on firm); not at his best this year but that is reflected in his much-reduced mark; risky but not impossible.
runners.comment: Four-time C&D winner; struggling this year but he's dropped a long way in the weights

runners.name: Alazwar
runners.spotlight: 5lb lower than for his Newbury win last summer; second of 11 at Lingfield (1m, AW) on his penultimate start but ran poorly at Wolverhampton last week; revival not out of the question.
runners.comment: On a dangerous mark and he ran okay on penultimate start; less good last week

runners.name: Romanovich
runners.spotlight: Back to winning ways in a 7f handicap at Wolverhampton in March; not so good back at Wolverhampton last month but 1m on fast ground is no problem and he is still on a fair mark.
runners.comment: AW win (7f) in March; not at best last time but conditions will be fine and on a fair mark
runners.owner: Mrs S M Maine & Roger Gould

runners.name: Scarfo
runners.spotlight: Beaten 25 times since his 7f AW win in July 2024; well short of his best in three runs this year and he looks vulnerable again.
runners.comment: On a losing run of 25 and he is yet to shine in 2026; opposable

runners.name: King Of The Dance
runners.spotlight: On a fair mark and the trip, track and ground should all be fine; arrives on the back of some fair AW runs; each-way claims again.
runners.comment: Conditions should be fine and he has been running okay on AW this spring; each-way claims

runners.name: Amathus
runners.spotlight: Seven wins, all on good to firm over 6f and 7f; record over 1m is less compelling and he was never in the hunt over C&D on last month's reappearance; minor money perhaps his best hope.
runners.comment: Multiple winner over 6f/7f; record over 1m is less compelling; others are stronger

runners.name: Man Is King
runners.spotlight: Landed a gamble in a 1m2f classified event at Chelmsford on his stable debut in February; in and out since and likely a few of these will have his measure.
runners.comment: 1m2f AW win on stable debut in February (well backed); mixed record since; others stronger

runners.name: Wrist Art
runners.spotlight: Winless since October 2023; placed twice on AW in January but it has been a struggle more recently; others are safer.
runners.comment: Without a win since 2023; placed twice on AW in January but he's struggled since

runners.name: Pay Attention
runners.spotlight: The winner of one of his 15 starts, namely an 8.3f handicap at Nottingham last June (tongue tied first time); 2lb lower today after a quiet end to 2025; goes without any accessories this time but he ran well on his reappearance last spring and Henry Callan is a positive booking.
runners.comment: Well treated on 2025 best; no accessories today but booking of Henry Callan signals intent

runners.name: Never Dream
runners.spotlight: He made a winning handicap debut in June 2024 (7f, good); mixed record since and his Brighton second last September was the only real bright spot in 2025; chance if returning at that level but it is not assured.
runners.comment: Inconsistent since 7f win in June 2024; absent 231 days; chance if returning at his best

runners.name: Saturnalia
runners.spotlight: Well handicapped on last season's best forrm (AW) but he ended the year with a whimper and returns from a 135-day break with an inexperienced amateur booked.
runners.comment: Chance on last year's best but he ended 2025 quietly; amateur takes her first rules ride
runners.owner: Ms H N Pinniger And Partner

races.race_name: Blast Event Hire EBF Restricted Maiden Stakes (Band D) (EBF Restricted Race Qualifier) (GBB Race)

runners.name: Jolly Good Fellows
runners.spotlight: She has finished third in two 5f events, improving her RPR at Pontefract 13 days ago (well backed); should give it another good go.
runners.comment: Third at Wolverhampton and Pontefract last month; should give it another good go

runners.name: Top Cote
runners.spotlight: 33-1 for his Leicester debut (5f, good to firm) nine days ago, a race in which he was green and out the back 2f out before finishing with a flourish to take second; open to improvement and he's a leading contender.
runners.comment: Penny dropped late at Leicester 9 days ago & he finished with a flourish for 2nd; chance

runners.name: Dee's Star
runners.spotlight: Showed speed before weakening into sixth in a C&D fillies' novice 17 days ago; open to improvement.
runners.comment: Weakened into sixth over C&D 17 days ago; can do better but others appeal more

runners.name: Ron's Angel
runners.spotlight: March foal by Harry Angel; 4,000gns yearling; fourth foal; dam unraced half-sister to winners Repeater (smart 7f-1m4f including 2yo) and All Out (Group-placed 5f/6f 2yo); one of two runners for John Butler.
runners.comment: 4,000gns yearling; unraced dam from a good family; one of two runners for the stable

runners.name: Sayidah Ardad
runners.spotlight: March foal by Ardad; £4,000 yearling; first foal of a French 6f winner, herself a half-sister to winners Bay Royale (1m2f) and Denim And Diamonds (12.3f); stable off the mark with 2yos this year; betting instructive.
runners.comment: £4,000 yearling; dam a 6f winner in France; stable off mark with 2yos; newcomer of note

runners.name: Binfluencer
runners.spotlight: April foal by Tasleet; fifth foal; half-brother to 5f/5.5f winner Rogue Enforcer (including 2yo/France/Italian Listed; RPR 95); dam 5f AW winner (59), half-sister to 7f Group 2 winner Finjaan and prolific 5f winner Sandfrankskipsgo; worth a market check.
runners.comment: Half-brother to sprint winner Rogue Enforcer out of a 5f AW winner; check betting

runners.name: Postremo
runners.spotlight: February foal by Soldier's Call; first foal of unraced half-sister to winners Moonstone Magic (7f Group 3) and Van Velde (6f); likely best watched on debut.
runners.comment: Only minor appeal on paper and stable have few winning 2yo newcomers these days

runners.name: Terry O
runners.spotlight: February foal by Aclaim; half-brother to winners Party Tiger (6f-1m including 2yo/Greece; RPR 76), Bold Decision (5.7f; 61) and Hourless (9.4f AW, 58; 2m hurdle); dam 1m AW winner (65); stable not known for 2yo success.
runners.comment: Half-brother to three winners out of a 1m AW winner; stable not associated with 2yos

runners.name: Tree Wizard
runners.spotlight: February foal by Tasleet; 11,000gns yearling; third foal; half-brother to 5f/6f AW winner Sarafina Mshairi (RPR 69); dam unraced, out of Group-placed 5f winner; stable's 2yos 4-6 this year so he has to be feared despite the widest stall.
runners.comment: 11,000gns half-brother to a winning sprinter; yard 4-6 with 2yos this year; interesting

runners.name: Zelen
runners.spotlight: March foal by Ubettabelieveit; 10,000gns yearling; fourth foal; half-brother to 7f winner Finn Ironside (RPR 74); dam 1m 2yo winner (88); stable's first two 2yo runners of the year have been placed.
runners.comment: 10,000gns half-brother to 7f winner Finn Ironside; yard's two 2yo runners have been placed

runners.name: Doha Rd
runners.spotlight: Never a threat after a slow start in a C&D event 17 days ago; can leave that behind her in time but others have achieved more.
runners.comment: Ninth of 13 over C&D on last month's debut, making some late headway; can do better

runners.name: Tricky Jenny
runners.spotlight: 5-1 third favourite for her debut at Musselburgh last month (5f, soft), a race where she showed speed before weakening quickly inside the final furlong; should last longer today.
runners.comment: Weakened quickly inside the final furlong on debut (5f, soft); should last longer today

runners.name: Devon Angel
runners.spotlight: Down the field in two C&D runs last month; should find nurseries more suitable in time.
runners.comment: Down the field in two C&D runs last month; should find nurseries more suitable in time

races.race_name: Blackmore Building Contractors Handicap

runners.name: Angel Shared
runners.spotlight: Prominent racer; C&D winner who completed a Newmarket hat-trick last summer (6f, good to firm); it wasn't a vintage Listed event in which she kept on well for fifth here four weeks ago but her mark has been left unchanged and another good run should be forthcoming.
runners.comment: Tough C&D winner; good fifth in Listed race here last month; in the mix with a repeat

runners.name: Moulin Booj
runners.spotlight: C&D winner; stayed on well to land a 5f handicap at Wolverhampton in March and two defeats since have come over 6f; latest Lingfield effort was much better than the distance beaten suggests; 5f on quick ground suits ideally and more patient tactics in a well-run race could pay dividends.
runners.comment: Conditions to suit and much better than latest run suggests; still figures on a good mark

runners.name: Marty Hopkirk
runners.spotlight: Lightly raced 4yo; good chance on last summer's Doncaster second but that hasn't yet proved a springboard to better things; not weighted to reverse latest Southwell placings with Fidelius.
runners.comment: May be sharper for last month's reappearance but he'll need more if he's to gain a 2nd win

runners.name: Ruby's Profit
runners.spotlight: Speedy filly; back to her last winning mark and this will be more suitable than the C&D Listed race she contested four weeks ago; hooded for the first time today.
runners.comment: Back to last winning mark; this more suitable than Listed race latest; hooded first time

runners.name: Fidelius
