runners.spotlight: 6-17 on AW and better than ever this spring, following his Lingfield win in March with a narrow defeat at Southwell; 0-8 on turf but has run well on fast ground in the past; perhaps a shade high in the weights now.
runners.comment: Better than ever on AW in recent times; 0-8 on turf and has a career-high mark to overcome

runners.name: Sturlasson
runners.spotlight: In good form in the second half of 2025, winning three 5f handicaps (Tapeta twice and good to soft); last month's return to action at Newmarket should have sharpened him up but ground this lively will be a first.
runners.comment: Ended 2025 in top form; solid return to action last month; ground a query

runners.name: Regal Envoy
runners.spotlight: Prominent racer; did best of the group to race on the stands' side when third in a 5f handicap at Newmarket three weeks ago; well handicapped on last summer's best and he has a good record at Bath; shortlisted.
runners.comment: Good course record and looked to retain a good chunk of ability on his return; shortlisted

runners.name: Desert Cop
runners.spotlight: His only win in two years came in Bahrain last March; not beaten far at Nottingham on his recent British return but it took him a long time to pick up; today's stiffer track will suit and this should be run at a good pace so he's not discounted.
runners.comment: Late headway at Nottingham eight days ago; stiffer track will help; not discounted

races.race_name: Pins And Putts Handicap

runners.name: Toolatetonegotiate
runners.spotlight: She has made steady progress throughout her career, winning three times on AW, and she was unlucky not to register a first turf success when a close third over C&D last week; saw no daylight at a crucial stage on that occasion and the form has already been franked; good chance.
runners.comment: Unlucky in running when third over C&D last week; form already franked; solid contender

runners.name: Over Spiced
runners.spotlight: Three 5f wins for Grace Harris last season; she made a promising return to action at Nottingham last month and backed it up with a good second at Salisbury yesterday; should go well again if turned out quickly.
runners.comment: Three 5f wins last year; 2 promising runs this year, including 2nd at Salisbury on Sunday

runners.name: Diomed Spirit
runners.spotlight: Big chance on his AW form but he has finished out of the placings in all nine turf runs; BHA mark is 15lb lower on turf but he's likely to remain vulnerable.
runners.comment: Big chance on AW form but 15lb lower on turf and with good reason on available evidence

runners.name: Aces Wild
runners.spotlight: 5f win at Chester (good) last summer came off a 4lb lower mark; encouraging return to action here (5.7f, good) 17 days ago and should go well if building on that first run for 199 days.
runners.comment: 4lb higher than for his last win; promising reappearance 17 days ago; should go well

runners.name: Tees Aggregates
runners.spotlight: Can front-run, as when running away with a Beverley handicap last summer off 6lb lower (5f, good to firm); promising reappearance at Southwell last month but he failed to build on it at Beverley 11 days ago; could bounce back.
runners.comment: Shaped well on AW on return but took a backward step on turf 11 days ago; can bounce back
runners.owner: Ingleby Bloodstock Ltd & The Ivy League

runners.name: Smooth Silesie
runners.spotlight: C&D winner; had a good time of things in 2025, winning four times; she has finished last in her three AW runs this year and has only dropped 3lb; may need some more help from the handicapper.
runners.comment: C&D winner; had a fine year in 2025 but yet to beat a rival this year; return to turf a +

races.race_name: Carers Centre Charity Handicap (Bath Summer Sprint Series Qualifier)

runners.name: Takincareofbizness
runners.spotlight: More encouragement since handicapping after a gelding operation, fourth at Nottingham (behind Magnificent Mel) last month and then a neck second of six at Yarmouth six days ago (both 5f on good ground); today's extra yardage should suit and he should be in the thick of it if handling the faster ground.
runners.comment: Two good h'cap runs this year (5f, good); longer trip a plus; involved if handling ground

runners.name: Rogue Rebellion
runners.spotlight: Won a 5f AW handicap for James Owen in January; only sixth of nine over 5f here for new stable four weeks ago; he needs to resume his progress to win.
runners.comment: 5f AW win for J Owen in January; midfield on last month's stable debut; more is needed

runners.name: Sands Of Seve
runners.spotlight: Everything clicked when running away with a 5f handicap at Wolverhampton in March (visored first time); nothing before or since to suggest he is the answer today.
runners.comment: Won well on AW in March (visored first time) but nothing before or since to shout about

runners.name: Baileys Ontherocks
runners.spotlight: Failed to progress for James Tate and her two runs for Tony Carroll need bettering; down in the weights but carries risk.
runners.comment: Down in the weights but she isn't progressing and others have more pressing claims

runners.name: Magnificent Mel
runners.spotlight: 0-10 but she has been placed three times since joining this yard, including when second of 17 at Nottingham 16 days ago (5f, good); up 3lb but still makes appeal.
runners.comment: 0-10 but latest Nottingham second came in a big field; solid claims despite a 3lb rise

runners.name: Miss Ayala
runners.spotlight: 7f AW win as a 2yo; struggled since and she looks vulnerable dropped in trip on her return to turf and after three months off.
runners.comment: 7f AW win as a 2yo; little to celebrate since and she looks vulnerable again

runners.name: Miss Magic Dragon
runners.spotlight: No immediate improvement for the switch to handicaps at Pontefract four weeks ago (6f, good to soft) but it was a run that promised better at some point; faster ground may help and she's one to consider.
runners.comment: Some promise on her handicap/seasonal debut last month; faster ground can help; unexposed

runners.name: Cape Toronada
runners.spotlight: Modest form in three 2yo runs and she was a well-beaten 40-1 shot at Wolverhampton on her handicap debut six weeks ago (8.6f); now reverts to sprinting.
runners.comment: Well-beaten 40-1 shot on handicap debut six weeks ago (8.6f); now drops back in trip

runners.name: Ken Brulee
runners.spotlight: Ended 2025 with a creditable, running-on fifth at Chelmsford (5f) in December; more needed to break his duck.
runners.comment: Ran well at Chelmsford on final 2yo run (5f); more needed to break his duck

runners.name: Cougar Force
runners.spotlight: Poor form in four runs last year (5f-8.6f); bred to do much better at some point and the betting should be revealing now handicapping after six months off.
runners.comment: Could leave 2yo form well behind her now handicapping at a low level

races.race_name: Lee's 60th - Still Horsing Around Handicap (Bath Summer Stayers' Series Qualifier)

runners.name: Mrembo
runners.spotlight: Five-time winner over 1m2f-11.7f on turf; beaten under 2l after a break at Kempton and might have gone close with more luck.
runners.comment: Beaten under 2l after a break at Kempton and might have gone close with more luck

runners.name: Beaune
runners.spotlight: 0-9 on turf but returns to this surface after posting form figures of 12211 over 1m4f on the AW; his mark has suffered as a result but he still appeals more than most.
runners.comment: 0-9 on turf but returns to this surface after posting figures of 12211 over 1m4f on the AW

runners.name: Buck Barrow
runners.spotlight: Ffos Las winner over 1m2f (good to soft) last summer; just one effort of any real note since.
runners.comment: Ffos Las winner over 1m2f last summer; just one effort of any real note since

runners.name: Knightmare
runners.spotlight: Still a maiden and started out as a sprinter, but seemed to see out this trip fine here last week when third; clear chance.
runners.comment: Started out as a sprinter, but seemed to see out this trip fine here last week when third

runners.name: Virtual Hug
runners.spotlight: Multiple AW winner who has won over hurdles but is 0-18 on turf on the Flat; won at Wolverhampton in March (1m4f) but he's not easy to predict.
runners.comment: Won at Wolverhampton in March (1m4f) but he's not easy to predict
runners.owner: Mike Harris Racing & Nikki Evans Racing

runners.name: Maywedance
runners.spotlight: Record of 1-19 and she's on her fourth trainer; recent AW efforts were uninspiring.
runners.comment: Record of 1-19 and she's on her fourth trainer; recent AW efforts were uninspiring

runners.name: Bobby Dassler
runners.spotlight: Often goes well here but not in his last two races (1m/1m2f) and he's 2-41 overall.
runners.comment: Often goes well here but not in his last two races (1m/1m2f) and he's 2-41 overall

runners.name: Two Plus Two
runners.spotlight: Better than ever returned to turf last May when winning on fast ground at Salisbury and here; on a realistic mark for this belated return to action.
runners.comment: Better than ever returned to turf last May, winning on fast ground at Salisbury and here

runners.name: Bohemian Breeze
runners.spotlight: Backed up a good run on the AW with a fourth here last week when just behind Knightmare; a tongue-tie returns along with new blinkers; thereabouts.
runners.comment: Backed up a good run on the AW with a fourth here last week when just behind Knightmare

races.race_name: Droneart Show May 8th Handicap

runners.name: Mali Star
runners.spotlight: Improved on her 2yo form when finishing third over C&D (firm) on last week's seasonal return; had to wait for a run on that occasion before finishing with a flourish; open to further progress.
runners.comment: Good third over C&D last week; open to improvement and he's a leading contender

runners.name: Lady Of Clover
runners.spotlight: Ran her best race to date when keeping on for third at Yarmouth (1m, good to firm) two weeks ago; could still have a bigger effort in her and the form of the stable is encouraging.
runners.comment: Best effort so far when third at Yarmouth two weeks ago; each-way shout for in-form yard

runners.name: Shes Got The Blues
runners.spotlight: 12-race maiden but she was placed in her two runs last month, both on Tapeta (1m and 7f); more to prove on turf but further progress can't be discounted.
runners.comment: Placed twice on AW last month; more to prove on turf but could still have progress in her

runners.name: Alice's Influence
runners.spotlight: Off the mark at the fifth attempt when dead-heating for a 1m handicap at Wetherby (good to firm; visored first time) eight days ago; a 6lb penalty asks more of her but she is on the up.
runners.comment: Dead-heated at Wetherby (1m, good to firm) eight days ago; 6lb penalty asks more of her

runners.name: Grand Echo
runners.spotlight: Finished second at Kempton (7f) in February but he hasn't backed it up in two runs since; more to prove on turf and his stamina isn't assured either; cheekpieces discarded and a tongue-tie is added.
runners.comment: The odd good AW run but more to prove on turf and now steps up in trip

runners.name: Pam's Sonnet
runners.spotlight: Huge prices and down the field in three AW runs in February/March (6f-1m); more realistic task today but not obviously well handicapped.
runners.comment: Her first realistic assignment today but not obviously well treated

runners.name: Sail On Sailor
runners.spotlight: Placed in his two AW runs for Simon Dow this spring (trained by Brett Johnson last year), including when stepped up to 1m at Lingfield last time; has just his second turf run, and first on fast ground, but he'll be in the shake up if reproducing recent efforts.
runners.comment: Placed in two AW handicaps for new yard this year; fast turf a query

runners.name: Resalah
runners.spotlight: Exposed low-grade maiden; stamina still not conclusively proven and others bring stronger claims.
runners.comment: Exposed low-grade maiden who still has to prove her stamina for 1m; easy enough to swerve

runners.name: Henfield
runners.spotlight: Down the field in three maidens for Daniel Steele last season (5f-1m); not easily recommended on stable/handicap debutt.
runners.comment: Poor form in three runs for D Steele last year (5f-1m); opposable on stable/handicap debut

runners.name: Marveling
runners.spotlight: She hasn't beaten many home in three maiden/novice runs over 7f; potential improver now up in trip for her handicap debut; betting to guide.
runners.comment: Not beaten many home in three 7f runs but could progress now handicapping over 1m
runners.owner: John Troy And Mrs Rebecca Philipps

races.race_name: Shooting Star EBF Novice Stakes (GBB Race)

runners.name: From Me To You
runners.spotlight: Seventh in two starts over 5f on turf last month, though his latest Leicester performance was much better than his Newbury debut; may step forward again, but will probably need to; stable also runs Shadow King.
runners.comment: Seventh in two starts on turf last month; may step forward again, but will need to

runners.name: Shadow King
runners.spotlight: 11-2 when last of five on last month's Newmarket debut (5f, good), but is likely to improve and the form of that race has since been well advertised by the first two; looks the stable's first string and respected.
runners.comment: Last of five on last month's Newmarket debut, but the form has since been well advertised
runners.owner: Exors Of The Late Sheikh Mohammed Obaid

runners.name: Bin Waary
runners.spotlight: February foal by Saxon Warrior; 48,000 foal; 62,000gns yearling; half-brother to winners Rapid Force (6f AW 2yo; RPR 80) and We Sea You (US 1m/8.5f turf); dam Group-placed 5f winner (101), half-sister to 6f Listed (including 2yo) winner Katla, out of half-sister to Wootton Bassett; the outside stall may not be ideal for a 2yo newcomer.
runners.comment: 62,000gns half-brother to two winners; outside stall may not be ideal for a newcomer

runners.name: Martha Brown
runners.spotlight: March foal by Aclaim; half-sister to Slovakian 8.7f/1m2f winner Prufry; dam placed 6f/7f 2yo (RPR 66), half-sister to Group-placed 5f 2yo winner Ahlan Emarati, out of Listed-placed 6f/7f winner; apprentice-ridden on debut and others appeal more on breeding.
runners.comment: Apprentice-ridden on debut and others appeal more on breeding

runners.name: Meennaa
runners.spotlight: March foal by No Nay Never; 225,000gns yearling; first foal; dam Group-placed 5f 2yo/6.5f winner (RPR 101), sister to French 5f/6f winner Kentish Waltz, out of 6f 2yo winner, is granddaughter of 6f 2yo Listed winner; enough to like on breeding and stable has plenty of winning 2yo newcomers; respected, especially if market positive.
runners.comment: Enough to like on breeding and stable has plenty of winning 2yo newcomers; respected

runners.name: Blue Skies Above
runners.spotlight: April foal by Pinatubo; 55,000gns yearling; half-brother to winners Loves Me Likearock (5f-6.5f including 2yo/AW/Australia; RPR 93), Midnightattheoasis (5f/6f AW; 76) and Life After Love (5f AW; 65); dam 5f winner (including 2yo; 96), sister to smart 5f/6f winner Double Up; stable has its share of 2yo winners first time and should handle the surface on breeding; watch market.
runners.comment: Stable has its share of 2yo winners first time and should handle the surface on breeding

runners.name: Green Sovereign
runners.spotlight: March foal by Lope Y Fernandez; £40,000 yearling; half-brother to 5f AW 2yo winner Aegina (RPR 91); dam 6f winner (77), half-sister to 7f Group 3 winner Twilight Spinner, out of Listed-placed 5f/6f winner; stable doesn't have many 2yo winners first time, so may be one for another day.
runners.comment: Stable doesn't have many 2yo winners first time, so may be one for another day

runners.name: Perigord Noir
runners.spotlight: March foal by Invincible Army; 45,000gns yearling; brother to 1m AW 2yo winner Flagon Dry (RPR 70), half-brother to 6f 2yo winner Dragon Leader (including Listed; 100); dam 5.7f-1m winner (including 2yo/US; 92), closely related to 5f winner Exclusively, out of 6f 2yo Listed winner; would be a rare winning 2yo newcomer for the stable.
runners.comment: Would be a rare winning 2yo newcomer for the stable

runners.name: Drum Major
runners.spotlight: 40-1 when last of nine on his Newbury debut (5f, good) 17 days ago; half-brother to an AW winner, but needs to take a big step forward.
runners.comment: Last of nine on his Newbury debut 17 days ago; needs to take a big step forward

races.race_name: McCaffertys Bars Maiden Stakes (GBB Race) (Div I)

runners.name: Noble Vow
runners.spotlight: The most exposed of these after five defeats but he brings the best form and he pulled too hard on his comeback at Bath; better expected.
runners.comment: He brings the best form and he pulled too hard on his comeback at Bath; better expected

runners.name: Thunder Call
runners.spotlight: Well bred; never sighted on Newmarket debut (7f, good) but showed more when runner-up on heavy at Doncaster (6f); returns in a winnable maiden with his yard going well.
runners.comment: Doncaster second; returns in a winnable maiden with his yard going well

runners.name: Speculative
runners.spotlight: 72,000gns yearling by Wootton Bassett; ran okay in two 7f races at Newbury last summer, latterly always prominent before fading late on; can win races and perhaps over 6f.
runners.comment: 72,000gns yearling by Wootton Bassett; ran okay in two 7f races at Newbury last summer

runners.name: Tyrant Gg
runners.spotlight: 145,000gns breeze-up 2yo; third foal out of a 5f AW winner; debut has been a while coming and would need to see confidence behind him.
runners.comment: 145,000gns breeze-up 2yo; third foal out of a 5f AW winner

runners.name: Bundok
runners.spotlight: Trailed home last in a 7f good-ground maiden at Newbury last month; went off 50-1.
runners.comment: Trailed home last in a 7f good-ground maiden at Newbury last month; went off 50-1

runners.name: That's Random
runners.spotlight: Just minor promise in two AW starts 137 days apart, the latest over C&D; perhaps one for handicaps.
runners.comment: Just minor promise in two AW starts 137 days apart, the latest over C&D

runners.name: Nzuri
runners.spotlight: Stable won this last year but this filly has looked limited in her first two races (6f).
runners.comment: Stable won this last year but this filly has looked limited in her first two races (6f)

runners.name: Ruiz
runners.spotlight: A tailed-off last in both runs here last autumn, over 7f.
runners.comment: A tailed-off last in both runs here last autumn, over 7f

runners.name: Whitesnake
runners.spotlight: 22-1 and beaten just over 11l in a 6f soft-ground novice at Windsor in September; a 4yo filly who has to concede weight all round.
runners.comment: 22-1 and beaten just over 11l in a 6f soft-ground novice at Windsor in September

runners.name: Imagine That
runners.spotlight: Never better than midfield when a 125-1 chance here in September (6f); drawn wide and one to watch for now.
runners.comment: Never better than midfield when a 125-1 chance here in September (6f); drawn wide

runners.name: Distillation
runners.spotlight: RPRs in the 40s last season and has since been gelded; can only be watched.
runners.comment: RPRs in the 40s last season and has since been gelded; can only be watched

races.race_name: McCaffertys Bars Maiden Stakes (GBB Race) (Div II)

runners.name: Tie Fighter
runners.spotlight: Only beaten a short head in a Leicester novice (5f, good to soft) in October, but his other five performances as a 2yo leave something to be desired; opposable from the outside stall after 195 days off.
runners.comment: One piece of worthwhile form in six starts as a 2yo; opposable from the outside stall

runners.name: Gris De Chine
runners.spotlight: Showed some ability in two starts at Wolverhampton (6f, Tapeta) in March; frame possibilities again if showing the same level of form on first start for new trainer.
runners.comment: Showed some ability in two starts at Wolverhampton in March; frame possibilities again

runners.name: Sargent Dennis
runners.spotlight: Stepped up from his Chelmsford debut when second of nine at Wolverhampton (6f, Tapeta) 17 days ago; wouldn't need to step forward much more to be a major player.
runners.comment: Stepped up from his debut when runner-up at Wolverhampton 17 days ago; major player

runners.name: Kiani King
runners.spotlight: Fourth in two starts at Wolverhampton (6f, Tapeta), finishing just under 2l behind Sargent Dennis on the second occasion having stumbled soon after the start; frame material once again.
runners.comment: Fourth in two starts at Wolverhampton; frame material once again
runners.owner: P C J Dalby & R D Schuster

runners.name: Marengo Storm
runners.spotlight: £100,000 yearling; sent off 10-1 and showed ability when fifth of 12 on his debut over C&D four weeks ago; the form has been boosted by the subsequent wins of the second and third and a big performance is anticipated.
runners.comment: Fifth on his debut over C&D four weeks ago and the second and third have won since

runners.name: Elmfield
runners.spotlight: 50,000gns yearling; half-sister to 6f winner Rhythmic Acclaim (RPR 79); dam 1m winner (78), half-sister to Group-placed 6f 2yo winner Musical Art, out of 7f winning half-sister to 1,000 Guineas winner Finsceal Beo; the only newcomer in the field and the market should be informative.
runners.comment: The only newcomer in the field and the market should be informative

runners.name: Squashy Berry
runners.spotlight: 150-1 when a well-beaten last of 13 on his Leicester debut (6f, good) three weeks ago.
runners.comment: Well-beaten last of 13 on his Leicester debut three weeks ago

runners.name: Trinculo
runners.spotlight: Sent off 50-1 and beaten a very long way on his debut in the Woodcote at Epsom (6f, good to soft) 11 months ago; gelded since and probably best watched on return.
runners.comment: Beaten a very long way on his debut in the Woodcote at Epsom 11 months ago; best watched

runners.name: Wayward Queen
runners.spotlight: Never better than midfield on her Doncaster debut (6f, good to firm) ten days ago, but is out of a Group 3 winner (RPR 100) so surprising if she isn't capable of better.
runners.comment: Never better than midfield on Doncaster debut ten days ago, but probably capable of better

runners.name: Lesley Buckley
runners.spotlight: Astronomical prices and well beaten in three starts on Polytrack late last year; makes no appeal on stable debut (same owner) after 137 days off.
runners.comment: Well beaten in three starts on Polytrack late last year; makes no appeal on stable debut

races.race_name: White Lodge Fillies' Handicap

runners.name: Hamaleel
runners.spotlight: Front-runner who made all on her return from four months off over 1m here in January, but didn't get home under similar tactics over the same trip last time; return to 7f may be no bad thing; respected.
runners.comment: Front-runner who won over 1m here in January; return to 7f may be no bad thing

runners.name: Espanita
runners.spotlight: Did well in her first two starts earlier in the year, including an all-the-way success in a Wolverhampton novice (7f, Tapeta); disappointing when sent off odds-on for last month's handicap debut at Southwell (7f, Tapeta) which leaves her with a bit to prove.
runners.comment: Did well in first two starts earlier in the year but disappointed on handicap debut

runners.name: Sahana
runners.spotlight: Recorded victories at Yarmouth (1m, good to firm) and Lingfield (7f, Polytrack) last year; off six months, albeit she defied a similar absence on last season's reappearance; minus the hood for the first time in her career, though, so not quite sure what to expect.
runners.comment: Off six months, albeit she defied a similar absence last season; no hood today

runners.name: Mayfair Market
runners.spotlight: Showed some ability in four starts last year without necessarily progressing; needs to find a bit more with a tongue-tie applied back from seven months off.
runners.comment: Showed some ability in four starts last year without progressing; tongue-tie on

runners.name: Jowalla
runners.spotlight: Placed in her first three starts last summer including when runner-up in a Wolverhampton maiden (7f, Tapeta); may not have handled Epsom on nursery debut (7f, good; first-time cheekpieces) and worth a market check back from nine months off.
runners.comment: Placed in first three starts last summer and may not have handled Epsom on nursery debut

runners.name: Court Of Stars
runners.spotlight: Showed some ability in four starts last year, the latest when fourth of 13 behind a subsequent winner in a C&D novice in October; market informative on handicap debut after seven months off.
runners.comment: Showed some ability in four starts last year; market informative after seven months off

runners.name: Feisty Minx
runners.spotlight: Not much to get excited about in three starts over 7f on Polytrack during the winter, albeit she was well backed on the second occasion; well-bred, though, and another market move for her on handicap debut would be interesting.
runners.comment: Well held in three starts on Polytrack during the winter, but worth a market check
runners.owner: Mrs F Cotton, Mr & Mrs P Conway

races.race_name: Brooke Handicap (London Mile Series Qualifier)

runners.name: Valentine Boy
runners.spotlight: Won over C&D in November and probably put up an even better performance when beaten half a length into third of nine in a 0-80 back here four weeks ago (runner-up has won since); races off the same mark and remains unexposed for a 4yo; high on list.
runners.comment: C&D winner in November and latest third back here has been franked; high on list

runners.name: The Dark Baron
runners.spotlight: Most consistent since switched to the AW in October (1332321) and ran well back on turf when beaten a length into third of 11 at Bath (1m, good) last month; gets on well with Laura Coughlan; respected.
runners.comment: Most consistent since switched to the AW in October; gets on well with Laura Coughlan

runners.name: Influential
runners.spotlight: Placed once in four starts and that was when a well-held second of three in a Newcastle novice (1m, Tapeta) in October; surprising if he isn't capable of better, though, given his pedigree and 240,000gns price tag.
runners.comment: Placed once in four starts but surprising if he isn't capable of better given his pedigree

runners.name: Dannick
runners.spotlight: In the frame in all four starts on the AW, but yet to win after 12 attempts; returns from 191 days off having undergone wind surgery and he may be best watched despite having run well fresh in the past.
runners.comment: 0-12 and back from 191 days off; best watched despite having run well fresh in the past

runners.name: Cephalus
runners.spotlight: All 11 wins on the AW, including one over C&D, though his latest success for the Moores was in a Newcastle seller (1m, Tapeta) in October; out of the frame in all four starts for this yard since then and may be best watched despite his declining mark.
runners.comment: Out of the frame in all four starts for this yard and may be best watched

runners.name: Man Of Desert
runners.spotlight: Eight-race maiden who has shown ability on a couple of occasions; his last three efforts leave plenty to be desired though, including on his Yarmouth reappearance (7f, good to firm) 13 days ago; look elsewhere.
runners.comment: 0-8; last three efforts leave plenty to be desired; look elsewhere

runners.name: Bubbles Wonky
runners.spotlight: 2-17 on turf for Tim Easterby, but unplaced in three starts on Polytrack (1m/1m2f) since joining this yard; down again in grade, but a longer trip back on grass may be what he needs.
runners.comment: Down again in grade, but a longer trip back on grass may be what he needs

runners.name: Grey Soul
runners.spotlight: Ended his time with Roger Varian with success at Newcastle (1m, Tapeta; first-time blinkers, now removed) in October; changed hands for 22,000gns soon afterwards; market useful on stable debut after 195 days off.
runners.comment: Off the mark at Newcastle in October; market useful on stable debut after 195 days off

races.race_name: Injured Jockeys Fund Handicap (London Middle Distance Series Qualifier)

runners.name: Dust Cover
runners.spotlight: Record on the AW since November reads 1211 including when defying a wide draw over C&D in February; blew out completely at Musselburgh (12.5f, good) last month, but given his record on turf (85000) perhaps that performance isn't so hard to forgive; interesting back here.
runners.comment: Record on AW since November reads 1211 including a win over C&D; interesting back here

runners.name: Dancing Tiger
runners.spotlight: Dual turf winner, the latest on his Doncaster reappearance (1m2f, good to soft) in March; up 4lb and a player if as effective on Polytrack.
runners.comment: Won on Doncaster reappearance in March; up 4lb and a player if as effective on Polytrack

runners.name: Must Believe
runners.spotlight: Both wins for Ismail Mohammed came on the AW last autumn, including one over C&D; makes his stable debut after three months off having been picked up for 25,000gns in February; usual visor now missing; market useful.
runners.comment: C&D winner; makes his stable debut after three months off; market useful

runners.name: Masterinthewoods
runners.spotlight: Haydock winner (10.5f, good) last August and ended the campaign with a fine third of 14 over this C&D; off 222 days since, but he ran well on his reappearance last year; shortlisted.
runners.comment: Off 222 days since finishing third over C&D, but he ran well on his reappearance last year

runners.name: Tortured Soul
runners.spotlight: 10-32 on the AW and as tough as old boots, going down by less than a length in his last three outings including when beaten a short head over C&D in March; likely to be bang there once again.
runners.comment: 10-32 on the AW and as tough as old boots; likely to be bang there once again

runners.name: Star Time
runners.spotlight: 2-6 on the Flat (including a dead heat); may need a stiffer test than this now, but market useful nonetheless after 433 days off.
runners.comment: May need a stiffer test than this now, but market useful nonetheless after 433 days off

runners.name: Caph Star
runners.spotlight: Has only raced four times since winning over 1m here in August 2022, but he proved he still possessed plenty of ability when winning at Nottingham (1m2f, good) 16 days ago; up another furlong in trip and 5lb higher, but still worthy of consideration.
runners.comment: Course winner who is 5lb higher than when winning at Nottingham last time; considered

runners.name: Generous Support
runners.spotlight: 0-6 over middle-distances on turf for Jim Bolger last year but made the frame on five occasions; changed hands for 32,000gns in October and makes her stable/AW debut after 200 days off; market useful.
runners.comment: 0-6; makes her stable/AW debut after 200 days off; market useful

runners.name: Big Bear Hug
runners.spotlight: Made successful stable debut/reappearance at Goodwood (1m4f, good to soft) in June last year, but now 9lb lower for a reason; others stronger.
runners.comment: Made a successful stable debut/reappearance at Goodwood last June, but has regressed since

runners.name: Somkiyr
runners.spotlight: 1-7 on the Flat in France, but poor over hurdles since joining this yard; look elsewhere.
runners.comment: 1-7 on the Flat in France, but poor over hurdles since joining this yard; look elsewhere

races.race_name: Barrie Wells Trust Box4Kids Fillies' Handicap (GBBplus Race)

runners.name: Club Class
runners.spotlight: Most consistent last year (3232312) including a win over C&D in September; sent off odds-on when fourth of seven behind two of these on her reappearance over 1m3f here last month, but the return to this trip should help; high on list.
runners.comment: Behind two of these on last month's reappearance here; return to 1m4f should help

runners.name: Dojin
runners.spotlight: Made it 2-4 when defying a year's absence over 1m3f here last month with Caramay back in third and Club Class in fourth; 4lb higher, but still unexposed and no problem with the return to this trip; respected.
runners.comment: 2-4 and had two of these behind her when winning here last month; respected

runners.name: Caramay
runners.spotlight: Tough mare who has been in good form on the AW since December (13341333), the wins coming at Southwell (1m4f, Tapeta) and here (1m3f); return to this trip should suit and unlikely to be far away.
runners.comment: Tough mare who has been in good form since December (13341333); unlikely to be far away

runners.name: Typical Woman
runners.spotlight: Would have needed her Pontefract reappearance after eight months off, but she has never won off a mark this high and tends to come to life later in the year.
runners.comment: Has never won off a mark this high and tends to come to life later in the year

runners.name: Chilli Queen
runners.spotlight: C&D winner who failed by a short head to complete a four-timer at Doncaster (1m4f, good to soft) last September; has run well fresh in the past, but whether she can do so after eight months off remains to be seen; market may help.
runners.comment: Has run well fresh, but whether she can do so after eight months off remains to be seen

runners.name: Opera Wave
runners.spotlight: Her form figures suggest she improved once handicapping last year, but her Southwell win (1m3f, Tapeta) came in a slowly run race and she was beaten over 8l into second of three at Newmarket (1m2f, good) on her final outing; more needed back from six months off.
runners.comment: Form figures suggest she improved once handicapping last year, but she may be flattered

runners.name: Ma Belle Artiste
runners.spotlight: Dual turf winner for Joseph O'Brien but her form on the Flat and over hurdles for this yard has been patchy; plenty to prove back from 17 months off.
runners.comment: Form for this yard has been patchy; plenty to prove back from 17 months off

races.race_name: Wimbledon Greyhound Welfare Handicap (London Stayers' Series Qualifier)

runners.name: Crackergee
runners.spotlight: Beaten a neck on his reappearance over 1m4f here this time last year, but didn't build on it and is now 0-10; needs the longer trip and first-time cheekpieces to make a difference.
runners.comment: 0-10; needs the longer trip and first-time cheekpieces to make a difference

runners.name: Karthon
runners.spotlight: 0-9 for Andrew Balding, but runner-up in three of his last four starts; up in trip for this stable debut after seven months off and holds each-way claims if ready to go; no headgear today.
runners.comment: 0-9; up in trip for stable debut after seven months off; each-way claims if ready to go

runners.name: Rogue Empire
runners.spotlight: Front-runner who is 0-6, but twice finished third on Polytrack (including over C&D) last September; more needed back from seven months off, but he may enjoy the run of the race.
runners.comment: 0-6; more needed back from seven months off, but he may enjoy the run of the race

runners.name: Haaland
runners.spotlight: Made the most of a drop in class when regaining winning form at Southwell (16.5f, Tapeta) last month (third has won since); 4lb higher and back up in grade, but he still holds more compelling claims than a few.
runners.comment: Back up in grade after his Southwell win but still holds more compelling claims than a few

runners.name: Now The Eagle
runners.spotlight: 0-6 in France and modest in his first three starts for this yard, but managed to get off the mark in a slowly run contest over C&D (40-1) four weeks ago; form far from solid and more will be needed up 4lb.
runners.comment: Off the mark in a slowly run contest over C&D four weeks ago; form far from solid

runners.name: Port Of Entry
runners.spotlight: Out of the frame in six starts, including on stable debut following a mammoth absence over C&D four weeks ago (behind Now The Eagle); look elsewhere.
runners.comment: Out of the frame in six starts, including on stable debut over C&D four weeks ago

runners.name: Capone
runners.spotlight: Veteran who is 1-29 on the Flat and has completely lost his way, including when last of nine (behind two of these) over C&D four weeks ago; opposable from 1lb wrong.
runners.comment: 1-29 on the Flat and has completely lost his way; opposable from 1lb wrong
runners.owner: Maximum Racing And D Ffrench Davis

runners.name: Gooloogong
runners.spotlight: Not seen on the Flat since finishing first and third over C&D around this time last year; comes into this fit from hurdling, though, and should make his presence felt.
runners.comment: First and third in two starts over C&D this time last year; should make his presence felt

races.race_name: Moore & Tibbits Family Fun Day Mares' Maiden Hurdle (GBB Race)

runners.name: Crystal Days
runners.spotlight: Good-ground bumper winner; has missed the frame on only one occasion over hurdles, when highly tried at Kelso on handicap debut following wind surgery; better judged on latest Hereford second (2m, good) when back in maiden company; respected at this level against her own sex.
runners.comment: Pulled up on handicap debut; latest Hereford maiden second gives her claims at this level
runners.owner: Mrs S Lee And E Lee

runners.name: Great Dance
runners.spotlight: Bumper winner (good to soft) in October and not disgraced in a Huntingdon Listed contest in December when close behind Babs; encouraging hurdle debut when a close third at Taunton (2m, good) and again ran well when fourth in a 2m5f Haydock Listed contest (first-time tongue-tie) last month; strong claims eased in class and trip.
runners.comment: Form of her fourth in a Haydock Listed contest last time entitles her to leading claims.

runners.name: Elated
runners.spotlight: Showed ability in two bumper runs and, although facing a stiff task, she confirmed that initial promise when a 14.5l third of nine at Chepstow (2m, good to soft) on last month's hurdle debut; this is a little easier and she's likely to play a part.
runners.comment: Made an encouraging hurdle debut at Chepstow considering the stiff task; this is easier.

runners.name: Crystal Jet
runners.spotlight: Progressed from last year's promising juvenile bumper form when opening her account at Hereford (2m, good; first-time cheekpieces) last month, staying on strongly to lead close home under Isabelle Ryder; appeals as the type to do well over hurdles and she commands major respect.
runners.comment: Bumper winner last month under Isabelle Ryder and commands major respect on hurdle debut.

runners.name: Risk De Cerisy
runners.spotlight: Runner-up in an Irish point in January and shaped better than the bare result when fourth at Plumpton (2m4.5f, good), making much of the running before fading late; drop back in trip looks a positive and she remains open to improvement for top stable.
runners.comment: Drop back in trip looks a positive and could have more to offer for her leading stable.

runners.name: Babs
runners.spotlight: Good-ground bumper winner in October who may not have been suited by soft ground in two subsequent runs in Listed company, although her Huntingdon seventh (Great Dance just behind) was perfectly respectable; return to good going should suit and she's a likely type to make an impact over hurdles.
runners.comment: Good-ground bumper winner; a likely type to make an impact over hurdles at some point.

runners.name: Cushendall
runners.spotlight: Comfortable winner of small-field bumper at Kelso (good) last May for Stuart Crawford but tailed off on hurdle/stable debut at Fontwell (2m1.5f, soft; hung left throughout; first-time hood) in January; that leaves a question but it's still early days and return to good ground may help.
runners.comment: Good-ground bumper winner; tailed off on stable/hurdle debut since; still early days.

runners.name: Martha's Cottage
runners.spotlight: Well held in two bumpers, including in an eyeshield at Uttoxeter in March; needs the switch to hurdles to spark major improvement; one of two for the stable.
runners.comment: Well held in bumpers and needs to take a big step forward now switched to hurdles.

runners.name: Mrs Cravatte
runners.spotlight: Modest pointer; made only a minor rules debut when down the field in a recent Ludlow bumper; others make more obvious appeal on hurdle debut.
runners.comment: Modest point/bumper form and others make more obvious appeal on hurdle debut.

runners.name: Maxiani
runners.spotlight: Pedigree strong on stamina, winning siblings including Cyclops (2m1f-3m6f hurdle/chase), Innisfree Lad (2m5f-3m1f hurdle/chase) and Credo (2m1f-3m2f hurdle/chase); well held in a point bumper in March and likely to need more time.
runners.comment: Well held on sole start in a point bumper in March and likely to need more time.

runners.name: G Whizz
runners.spotlight: Some ability in points and was a fair fourth in an Exeter bumper (2m, good; first-time tongue-tie) in March; switches to hurdles now and should have more to offer in time, but improvement needed to trouble a couple of these.
runners.comment: Switches to hurdles after showing some bumper ability; needs improvement to figure.

runners.name: Look This Way
runners.spotlight: 40-1 and well held on Huntingdon bumper debut (made early running) in March; hurdle debut for this nicely bred filly but she may be seen in a better light over longer trips in due course.
runners.comment: Well held on bumper debut and likely to do better over longer trips in time.

runners.name: Dinkys Rose
runners.spotlight: Unseated latest and had only shown modest form in a bumper and on hurdle debut previously;  needs a major step forward.
runners.comment: Needs a major step forward on what she has achieved so far

runners.name: No Risk Today
runners.spotlight: Irish point winner; had wind surgery prior to an encouraging bumper/stable debut at Taunton last November but well held on hurdle debut there in January (in a tongue-tie, retained) and now has something to prove.
runners.comment: Failed to build on an encouraging hurdle debut and now has something to prove.

races.race_name: Moore & Tibbits Solicitors Handicap Chase (GBB Race)

runners.name: Irish Chorus
runners.spotlight: 3-5 over fences; eased late on when beating Politacus here and comfortably defied a 6lb rise at Newton Abbot (2m, good), albeit at the expense of just one other finisher; up another 5lb but sure to go well.
runners.comment: Beat one other finisher last time but sure to go well again in current form

runners.name: Tommy Cullen
runners.spotlight: Only 1-9 over fences but runner-up four times; seems best at Doncaster but on a fair mark and he's able when fresh; Cobden booked.
runners.comment: Seems best at Doncaster but on a fair mark and he's able when fresh

runners.name: Politacus
runners.spotlight: No match for Irish Chorus here in March but she's got a handy pull in the weights with that rival and she lost a shoe when beaten 12l at Stratford; not discounted.
runners.comment: Lost a shoe last time; previous second to Irish Chorus gives her a live shout

runners.name: Neigh Botha
runners.spotlight: Won at Southwell (2m, good) in October but looked set to be second when left in the lead at Bangor; however, only 2lb higher now and he reportedly finished lame in November on his final run.
runners.comment: Running well prior to finishing lame in November on his final run

runners.name: Yes Indeed
runners.spotlight: Won twice last spring at about this trip but essentially underwhelming since, albeit in better races than this; the drop in class should be welcomed.
runners.comment: Current wellbeing something of an issue but the drop in class should be welcomed

runners.name: Lacrima
runners.spotlight: Quiet stable debut over hurdles last month but this gelding won four times over fences in France and he's only a 5yo; not entirely ruled out.
runners.comment: Quiet stable debut over hurdles last month but he won over fences in France

runners.name: In This World
runners.spotlight: Won twice for Dan Skelton but opposable on what he's shown in last four races, all handicaps divided between hurdles and fences.
runners.comment: Won twice for Dan Skelton but opposable on what he's shown in last four races

runners.name: Uncle Phil
runners.spotlight: Left Willie Mullins in the doldrums and hasn't got a lot going for current yard; others preferred.
runners.comment: Left Willie Mullins in the doldrums and hasn't got a lot going for current yard

races.race_name: Hazelton Mountford Insurance Brokers Handicap Hurdle

runners.name: Reforme
runners.spotlight: Was running quite well over fences until last time when pulled up; has a higher hurdle mark despite being a maiden in this discipline but if back on song he should be competitive in this bad a race.
runners.comment: If back on song after last time he should be competitive in this bad a race

runners.name: Spitalfield
runners.spotlight: Only 1-24 and that win was nearly two years ago; he was in no form earlier this year and plenty depends on whether cheekpieces can trigger something; he's dropped to a good mark.
runners.comment: No form earlier this year and plenty depends on whether cheekpieces can trigger something

runners.name: Cinderello
runners.spotlight: 40-1 when making a successful handicap debut last summer and probably better again when beaten 2l at Plumpton the final time (3m1f, good); off since September which is a slight concern, but this race has no depth.
runners.comment: Has the form to feature but off since September which is a slight concern

runners.name: Half Pint Daisy
runners.spotlight: Nothing to show for four runs in handicaps and she's 5lb out of the weights here; what could help is the step up in trip from 2m4f.
runners.comment: Nothing to show for four runs in handicaps and she's 5lb out of the weights here

runners.name: Georges Street
runners.spotlight: No worthwhile form in points and the same story so far under rules; cheekpieces are added for this handicap debut and he's raised in distance.
runners.comment: Poor so far but cheekpieces are added for this handicap debut and he's raised in distance

runners.name: Lady Harriett
runners.spotlight: Won over 2m4f in late 2024; a couple of thirds in the meantime but pulled up the last twice when slow ground was no excuse.
runners.comment: Sole win in 2024; pulled up the last twice when slow ground was no excuse

runners.name: Jet Joe
runners.spotlight: No worthwhile form, hitting a new low when tailed off in a seller latest (tongue tied); handicap debut.
runners.comment: No worthwhile form, hitting a new low when tailed off in a seller latest (tongue tied)

runners.name: Sir Rock
runners.spotlight: A three-time winner over fences and yet has shown nothing in his last three chase starts; has won over hurdles but returns from a break with much to prove.
runners.comment: A three-time winner over fences and yet has shown nothing in his last three chase starts

races.race_name: M&T Solicitors In Warwick Handicap Chase (GBB Race)

runners.name: Imperial Bede
runners.spotlight: Cheekpieces fitted in last three races; won at Catterick and back to form when a clear second at Haydock (2m4f, good); back up 3lb but still well handicapped on old exploits.
runners.comment: Won at Catterick and back to form when a clear second at Haydock (2m4f, good)

runners.name: Full Force Gale
runners.spotlight: Lightly raced; first run in this headgear combination when runner-up over C&D and late mistake didn't help; up 2lb but should run well.
runners.comment: First run in this headgear combination when runner-up over C&D

runners.name: Theonlywayiswessex
runners.spotlight: Below par when last seen in August but ran pretty well in his previous two races, including here; has plenty in his favour, other than a tough enough mark.
runners.comment: Has plenty in his favour, other than a tough enough mark

runners.name: Dj Pete
runners.spotlight: Has won twice since February but he's very hit and miss, and might be better over shorter.
runners.comment: Has won twice since February but he's very hit and miss, and might be better over shorter

runners.name: Another Folly
runners.spotlight: 2m6f winner at Stratford last October; another good run to be fourth at Ludlow in January (2m4f) but bit below that level since, last time sent to France.
runners.comment: Has the form to feature but a bit below his best the last twice, latterly in France

runners.name: Harel Du Marais
runners.spotlight: Landed his fourth win over fences at Stratford in March (2m1f, soft) but his Huntingdon third was remote and some inconsistency has crept in.
runners.comment: His Huntingdon third was remote and some inconsistency has crept in

runners.name: Salley Gardens
runners.spotlight: Ex-Olly Murphy; yet to win for current yard and now back from another lengthy break; others preferred.
runners.comment: Ex-Olly Murphy; yet to win for current yard and now back from another lengthy break

runners.name: Start Again
runners.spotlight: Exeter maiden hurdle winner in October; only made his chase/handicap debut on Saturday and was pulled up at Uttoxeter (16-1).
runners.comment: Only made his chase/handicap debut on Saturday and was pulled up at Uttoxeter (16-1)

runners.name: Diligent Militia
runners.spotlight: Point winner; didn't hang about over hurdles and was running well when clouting two out on handicap/chase debut at Chepstow, where he was made favourite in this new headgear combination; has to be of interest.
runners.comment: Running well when clouting two out on handicap/chase debut at Chepstow

races.race_name: Moore & Tibbits Handicap Hurdle

runners.name: Wild Goose
runners.spotlight: The winner of two handicaps, the latest at Hereford (2m, good) very decisively despite being headstrong initially; Aamilah Aswat rides again off a 7lb higher mark; respected.
runners.comment: Comfortable Hereford winner despite being headstrong initially; raised 7lb

runners.name: Nap Hand
runners.spotlight: Flat/hurdle winner; back from a break with pleasing runs under both codes before struggling in the Sussex Champion Hurdle; this is easier than that assignment.
runners.comment: Capable gelding and this is easier than the Sussex Champion Hurdle last time

runners.name: Jaminska
runners.spotlight: Big player on her form during the first half of last season but neither run this year was anywhere near that level; reservations for now.
runners.comment: Neither run this year was anywhere near best level; reservations for now

runners.name: Wondering Why
runners.spotlight: Comfortably beat a big field in a Lingfield maiden (2m, soft) and following run here was too bad to be true; consistent prior to that and should be up to winning races off this sort of mark.
runners.comment: Bumper/maiden winner who should be up to winning races off this sort of mark

runners.name: Gentleman Joe
runners.spotlight: Flat/hurdle winner in Ireland; first run over jumps since 2023 when an encouraging fourth at Newbury and can be forgiven his no-show in the Sussex Champion Hurdle; possibilities.
runners.comment: Encouraging fourth at Newbury and forgiven his no-show in the Sussex Champion Hurdle

runners.name: Seraphic
runners.spotlight: Won twice on the Flat this year and was competitive over hurdles during the opening half of last year; belatedly tries a hood this afternoon; considered with so little weight to carry.
runners.comment: Won twice on the Flat this year and should be competitive back hurdling; hood tried

runners.name: Continuance
runners.spotlight: Three-time winner over hurdles and nothing wrong with his most recent efforts in handicaps at top tracks; every chance provided he's straight enough after a lengthy break.
runners.comment: Consistent and every chance provided he's straight enough after a lengthy break

runners.name: Turn And Finish
runners.spotlight: Became disappointing over hurdles for Harry Derham last year and was down the field when returning in 1m4f AW handicap for new stable in March; opposable.
runners.comment: Became disappointing for Harry Derham; AW run on yard debut was forgettable

races.race_name: Racing To School Celebrating 25 Years Mares' Handicap Hurdle

runners.name: Hello Cello
runners.spotlight: Kept on well to win at Fontwell (2m3f, good to soft) in October but failed to take to fences in next two starts and finished well held reverting to hurdles back at Fontwell (2m1f) last month over what may have been an inadequate test; needs to bounce back, but only 2lb higher than for her win and the step back up in trip will suit.
runners.comment: Needs to bounce back but she's only 2lb higher than for her win; steps back up in trip.
runners.owner: Harry Fry Racing Syndicate Hello Cello

runners.name: Innisfree Pearl
runners.spotlight: Well held on first two starts over hurdles late last year but, after a respectable fourth back on the Flat (1m6f) in January, has progressed since switched to handicaps and upped in trip over jumps; on a workable mark and this longer trip could unlock further improvement.
runners.comment: Progressed since switched to handicaps and may improve again for this longer trip.

runners.name: Jessie Jump Jet
runners.spotlight: Failed to progress for previous stable and, having shaped with promise over C&D for new stable in January, is taking a similar path, most recently finishing a 17l third in first-time cheekpieces last month; goes up again in trip but needs to turn things around.
runners.comment: Hasn't kicked on from a promising start for the stable; needs to turn things around.

runners.name: Mistral Blue
runners.spotlight: Hereford (2m3f, good) winner under Fern O'Brien in April last year and virtually matched that effort on her comeback run at Huntingdon (2m4f, good) in November; best forgiven her follow-up effort back there in December as the soft ground went against her; wouldn't want any rain but returns on a competitive mark and not ruled out.
runners.comment: Wouldn't want any rain but returns from a break on a competitive mark and not ruled out.

runners.name: Flying Pimpernel
runners.spotlight: Placed in two of her three bumpers last year but she's yet to transfer that promise in three runs over hurdles (1m7f-2m3f), all on good; however, there could be more to come now switched to handicaps and stepped up in trip, off what looks a fair opening mark if she can rediscover some spark.
runners.comment: Yet to transfer bumper promise to hurdles but she now goes up in trip for handicap debut.

runners.name: Jlow
runners.spotlight: Yet to get her head in front after 12 attempts and recent efforts don't suggest she's about to make that breakthrough here, despite a 2lb easing of his mark.
runners.comment: 0-12 and recent efforts don't suggest she's about to make a breakthrough win here.

runners.name: Mc'flo
runners.spotlight: Has yet to show any notable form over hurdles, including in two starts in handicaps; others have more obvious claims.
runners.comment: Has yet to show any notable form over hurdles, including on both handicap starts.

runners.name: Eskimo Sam
runners.spotlight: Modest hurdle form for Tom Lacey in 2024-25; likely needed the run after a long absence when making handicap/stable debut in March; offered more, with that run behind her, at Newton Abbot (2m1f, good) last month but pedigree raises doubts about this longer trip.
runners.comment: Offered more latest but pedigree raises doubts over this longer trip.

runners.name: Abbey Law
runners.spotlight: 2m maiden hurdle winner last year but she struggled badly last season and was recently pulled up in cheekpieces; too much to prove at present.
runners.comment: 2m maiden hurdle winner but she struggled last season and has too much to prove.

runners.name: Luna Run
runners.spotlight: 3m hurdle winner last April in first-time cheekpieces, that her only win in 16 attempts, though often placed; given wind surgery after finishing a distant third at Plumpton (3m1f, good to soft) in October on final start for Evan Williams; back in trip following a break and worth considering for stable going well.
runners.comment: 1-16 but she's undergone wind surgery since last seen and interesting on stable debut.

runners.name: Crystal Box
runners.spotlight: Has finished well held on both completed starts over hurdles (2m-2m3f); needs this longer trip to ignite some improvement on handicap debut.
runners.comment: Shown little as yet and needs this longer trip to spark improvement on handicap debut.

runners.name: Shesonnet
runners.spotlight: Has shown ability in two bumpers and three maiden/novice hurdles, with her best effort when runner-up to a smart one at Uttoxeter (2m, soft) in November; however, she was pulled up in December when last seen and her opening mark doesn't look overly generous.
runners.comment: Needs to bounce back from pulling up latest and opening mark doesn't look generous.

runners.name: Burgeon
runners.spotlight: Has shown no notable form in three runs over hurdles and on sole start on the Flat; hopes pinned on the longer trip now switched to handicaps.
runners.comment: Yet to offer much over hurdles; attempts a longer trip.

races.race_name: Prix de Guiche (Group 3) (Colts & Geldings) (Turf)

runners.name: Hawk Mountain
runners.spotlight: Built on promising debut by winning next three starts, which included a Group 2 and the Group 1 Futurity Trophy Stakes (1m, heavy) at Doncaster; no penalty to carry at this much lower level means he sets a good standard, but bit surprising to see cheekpieces go on; holds a multitude of top level entries in Britain, Ireland and France.
runners.comment: Sets a high standard on 2yo form but surprising cheekpieces are added for return
runners.owner: Derrick Smith & Mrs John Magnier & Michael Tabor

runners.name: Campacite
runners.spotlight: Has the most experience of these and more than capable of smart form, as he showed in two French Group 1s at the end of his 2yo campaign; was a bit below his best in the Prix de Fontainebleau on his return (1m, good) but entitled to be sharper for that; trainer has won this race a few times; entered in the Poulains plus Prix du Jockey Club.
runners.comment: Showed good form as a 2yo and shaped okay on recent return
runners.owner: Guy Pariente Holding, G Augustin-Normand & Ecurie Friedrider

runners.name: Lord Clover
runners.spotlight: Made a big impression on debut, winning by 8l at this venue on the AW before finishing runner-up on soft ground here over a mile behind another talented rival; a furlong further will be no issue on pedigree, dam was third in the 1m2f Prix de l'Opera, and faster surface could see more improvement on first venture into Group company; entered in the St James's Palace Stakes and both the Poulains plus Prix du Jockey Club.
runners.comment: Bred to be a Group horse and shown plenty of promise so far; interesting

runners.name: Mr Lope Cen
runners.spotlight: Half-brother to both Ramatuelle and Tito Mo Cen; made a winning debut on the AW here over a mile in March before finishing second at Longchamp in the middle of last month on good to soft; this demands more but he is entered in the Poulains plus Prix du Jockey Club.
runners.comment: Well-bred colt, who has run well both times; first start in Group company

runners.name: Parade Bay
runners.spotlight: Only been seen once but showed the potential to be smart at Saint-Cloud last November (7.5f, heavy), full value for the 4l success, looking better the further he went; interesting Andre Fabre, who won this 12 months ago with Cualificar, puts him straight into a Group 3 for his return, and step up in trip unlikely to be an issue; entered in the Prix du Jockey Club and Grand Prix de Paris.
runners.comment: Looked a smart prospect when winning only run as 2yo; could be anything

races.race_name: Prix des Lilas (Listed Race) (Fillies) (Turf)
CARBONETZERO LIMITED HANDICAP STAKES 0-65
AES Recycling First Flier Stakes (Listed Race)

runners.name: Star Prospect
runners.spotlight: Starman colt got off the mark at the first time of asking here (5f, soft) when 3.5l ahead of Blixen Force; ran through the line strongly and while he has different ground to contend with now, he is a half-brother to two fast-ground winners; should be thereabouts.
runners.comment: Won on soft here on debut, half-brother to two fast ground winners, contender

runners.name: New Yorker
runners.spotlight: No Nay Never colt sent off the 4-7 favourite on debut at Dundalk and duly delivered a 1.25l win, despite displaying signs of greenness; his speedy dam Fairyland relished fast ground, so he should be well suited by conditions; leading claims for yard that has won three of the last four renewals.
runners.comment: Justified short odds on debut at Dundalk, should act on fast ground, leading claims
runners.owner: Derrick Smith & Mrs John Magnier & Michael Tabor

runners.name: Maria Quiteria
runners.spotlight: One of three for yard; Churchill filly bolted up under a positive ride on debut at Dundalk (5f), beating a stablemate by 4.25l; the runner-up hasn't done much for the form and this is a much tougher assignment; will need to improve plenty to make an impact.
runners.comment: Form of debut AW win has taken a knock, improvement needed in this tougher race

runners.name: Velozee
runners.spotlight: Sioux Nation filly made a winning start in a fillies' maiden at Cork (5f, soft), just getting up in the dying strides after blowing the start; quicker ground to contend with here and the third from her maiden has been beaten since, but should be more streetwise and yard's juveniles are always worthy of respect.
runners.comment: Got up in dying strides after tardy start to win at Cork, quicker ground now, respected

runners.name: What A Girl Wants
runners.spotlight: Persian Force filly is one of three for yard; made a promising start when a 1.25l third behind Star Prospect here last month (5f, soft); likely to improve plenty for that initial experience and also for quicker ground; looks the yard's main hope on jockey arrangements; interesting.
runners.comment: Promise on debut behind Star Prospect, likely to improve for quicker ground, interesting

runners.name: Rebel Dance
runners.spotlight: Starspangledbanner filly; 500,000gns yearling; half-sister to winners No Speak Alexander (7f-1m inc 2yo/Group 1; RPR 111) and Heavenly Being (5f 2yo; 78); dam 5f 2yo winner (inc Listed; 95); pitched in at the deep end on debut, but yard can ready one and she's worth monitoring in the market.
runners.comment: 500,000gns Starspangledbanner filly, half-sister to Gr' 1 winner, market best guide

runners.name: Blixen Force
runners.spotlight: Persian Force colt is one of three for yard; hung right in the Brocklesby at Doncaster (5f, gd-sft) on debut when a 3.25l third of 15; no match for Star Prospect over C&D (soft) on latest; much more needed and David Egan prefers the chances of What A Girl Wants.
runners.comment: Third in Brocklesby on debut, no match for Star Prospect latest, much more needed

races.race_name: Coolmore Stud Henry Longfellow Irish EBF Tetrarch Stakes (Listed Race)

runners.name: Neolithic
runners.spotlight: Didn't show much in first two starts but made it third time lucky when winning a median sires series race at Naas (6f, good); reappeared with a decent third here behind the evergreen Big Gossey in the Gladness Stakes (7f, sft-hvy); took the scalp of Greek Mythology at Gowran (7f, soft) last time but that rival lacked a recent run; top rated but Moore prefers Causeway.
runners.comment: Took the scalp of Greek Mythology at Gowran, top rated but Moore prefers Causeway
runners.owner: Westerberg & Mrs Magnier & M Tabor & D Smith

runners.name: Causeway
runners.spotlight: Left Gowran debut effort behind to win a 7f maiden here (sft-hvy) last October; won the Madrid handicap at Naas (7f, soft) on seasonal return off 94; that form has taken some knocks but interesting that Moore prefers him to his higher-rated stablemate Neolithic; unproven on quick ground; cheekpieces go on.
runners.comment: Won 7f maiden here last season, landed Madrid handicap on return, preferred by Moore
runners.owner: Michael Tabor & Derrick Smith & Mrs John Magnier

runners.name: Andab
runners.spotlight: Consistent in stakes company on turf last season and signed off with a Listed win at Dundalk (7f) in October; beaten a head on seasonal return in Al Rayyan Mile at Doha (gd-fm); respectable third in Listed race at Newcastle latest; cheekpieces go on; in the mix.
runners.comment: Consistent Listed AW winner, effective on quick ground, cheekpieces on, in the mix

runners.name: Geryon
runners.spotlight: Won maiden here on debut (7f, good); ran with real credit in Group 2s here on his next two starts over 7f (good) and 1m (good); stayed on well for fourth on seasonal return in Leopardstown Group 3 (behind Redemption Road, ahead of Iron Lily); return to 1m a plus and he's a leading contender.
runners.comment: Course maiden winner, stayed on over 7f in Gr'3 latest, return to 1m a plus, big chance

runners.name: Redemption Road
runners.spotlight: 1-5 as a two-year-old, the win coming in a 7.5f (yielding) Tipperary maiden in August; huge step forward on latest when running on nicely for a 3.25l third in a Leopardstown Group 3 (Geryon and Iron Lily behind); step back up to 1m looks sure to suit; go well if handling this quicker ground (half-brother won on good to firm).
runners.comment: Big step forward in Leopardstown Gr'3 latest, return to 1m a plus, should go well

runners.name: Greek Mythology
runners.spotlight: Won a 7f Dundalk median auction race on debut in December; 3.25l behind Neolithic on reappearance in a Gowran rated race (7f, soft) last month; sharper for that, step up to 1m should suit and could close the gap to that rival on this quicker ground.
runners.comment: Dundalk maiden winner, 3.25l behind Neolithic latest, could close the gap on better ground

runners.name: Iron Lily
runners.spotlight: Good run on debut here (7f, sft-hvy) in October when a 2.25l second behind Causeway; off the mark in 7f Dundalk maiden later that month; far from disgraced on seasonal return in 7f Leopardstown Group 3 (gd-yld); closely matched with Geryon and Redemption Road on that run and shaped like further would suit; not out of this.
runners.comment: Second behind Causeway here on debut, closely matched with two of these, not out of this

runners.name: Take Charge Star
runners.spotlight: Placed here on debut (7f, good) in August and won a Gowran maiden (7f, gd-yld) the following month; respectable sixth in Goffs Million on final start of last season; no match for Hardy Warrior on return at Naas (1m, soft); return to quicker ground a plus but more needed up in class.
runners.comment: Gowran maiden winner last year, beaten on return at Naas, more needed up in class

races.race_name: Coolmore Auguste Rodin Irish EBF Athasi Stakes (Group 3) (Fillies)

runners.name: Black Caviar Gold
runners.spotlight: Won a 7f maiden at Cork on soft on third start at two; promoted from 85 to 108 after showing huge improvement to win a 7f Group 3 at this venue (soft); that involved a reversal of form with the very useful Pivotal Attack; loss of a shoe probably damaged her chance on return in Leopardstown Group 3 won by True Love; leading chance if restored to form; cheekpieces fitted.
runners.comment: Loss of a shoe probably damaged her chance on return, leading chance on juvenile form

runners.name: Kensington Lane
runners.spotlight: Off the mark at the fifth attempt in fine style at Gowran, confirming the promise she showed when chasing home the very useful Geryon at the Curragh; flopped in a Group 3 race won by Black Caviar Gold on final start; finished in front of that below-par rival on reappearance.
runners.comment: Useful form at two before a poor 7f Group 3 run at this venue, fair run on seasonal debut

runners.name: Sinmara
runners.spotlight: Always in touch when a promising third behind two Aidan O'Brien-trained fillies in a 7f maiden on soft-to-heavy here on only start at two; can go on to better things after wide-margin Gowran win last month.
runners.comment: Promising debut here last October; can go on to better things after wide-margin Gowran win

runners.name: Caught U Sleeping
runners.spotlight: Overcame inexperience when making a winning debut in a 7f fillies' maiden at Naas last August; kept on for third behind the subsequent Prix Marcel Boussac winner Diamond Necklace in a C&D Listed race; ran poorly over 1m on heavy on final start; in front of below-par Black Caviar Gold on seasonal debut.
runners.comment: Winner and Listed-place at two, in front of below-par Black Caviar Gold on seasonal debut

runners.name: Drop Dead Gorgeous
runners.spotlight: Dubawi filly; ninth foal; half-sister to eight winners including Classic scorers Gleneagles, Marvellous and Joan Of Arc, as well as Group 1 juvenile winner Happily; mentioned in positive terms by trainer before a smart debut win over this trip at Naas in March; high-class prospect; should stay further but fine with this trip.
runners.comment: Half-sister to four Group 1 winners, unraced at two, smart debut winner at Naas in March
runners.owner: Michael Tabor & Derrick Smith & Mrs John Magnier

runners.name: Wild Bessie
runners.spotlight: Ran only once at two; did well to beat a Ballydoyle-trained odds-on chance at Cork a month ago; open to further progress; this looks too demanding.
runners.comment: Did well to beat a Ballydoyle-trained odds-on chance at Cork, open to further progress

runners.name: White Sand Beach
runners.spotlight: Fifth foal out of the top-class Alice Springs; consistent maiden; stable's second string; major improvement needed.
runners.comment: Fifth foal out of the top-class Alice Springs, consistent maiden, major improvement needed
runners.owner: Michael Tabor & Derrick Smith & Mrs John Magnier

runners.name: Alphecca
runners.spotlight: Progressive on easy ground last autumn; off the mark at Killarney in early October and won a valuable restricted contest over 7f at Naas just six days later; unplaced in a handicap last week on her seasonal debut; faces a tough task in this grade.
runners.comment: Picked up a good prize at two, unplaced in a handicap last week, tough task in this grade

runners.name: Killashee Warrior
runners.spotlight: Thurles maiden winner after a fine effort in a 7f Listed race at Leopardstown (1 1/2l behind third-placed Caught U Sleeping); appeared to surpass her juvenile form with Leopardstown fourth behind True Love, and ahead of four of these rivals, including joint-favourite Black Caviar Gold.
runners.comment: Appeared to surpass her juvenile form with Leopardstown effort ahead of four of these

runners.name: Mayflower
runners.spotlight: Handled soft ground when making a winning debut at Leopardstown last July; closely matched with several of these on running behind True Love last month.
runners.comment: Won her only race at two, closely matched with several of these on Leopardstown running

runners.name: Pollenca
runners.spotlight: Outran 66-1 odds when staying on for fourth over 1m at Dundalk in January; 10-1 when confirming that promise with a course 7f maiden win on heavy ground in March.
runners.comment: Confirmed promise of a Dundalk run with a course 7f maiden win on heavy ground in March

runners.name: Sky Watch
runners.spotlight: Improved with each outing last season, needs to step up on the form of Dundalk maiden win that earned an official rating of 81.
runners.comment: Improved with each outing last season, needs to step up on the form of Dundalk maiden win

races.race_name: Prix de Guiche (Group 3) (Colts & Geldings) (Turf)

runners.name: Hawk Mountain
runners.spotlight: Built on promising debut by winning next three starts, which included a Group 2 and the Group 1 Futurity Trophy Stakes (1m, heavy) at Doncaster; no penalty to carry at this much lower level means he sets a good standard, but bit surprising to see cheekpieces go on; holds a multitude of top level entries in Britain, Ireland and France.
runners.comment: Sets a high standard on 2yo form but surprising cheekpieces are added for return
runners.owner: Derrick Smith & Mrs John Magnier & Michael Tabor

runners.name: Campacite
runners.spotlight: Has the most experience of these and more than capable of smart form, as he showed in two French Group 1s at the end of his 2yo campaign; was a bit below his best in the Prix de Fontainebleau on his return (1m, good) but entitled to be sharper for that; trainer has won this race a few times; entered in the Poulains plus Prix du Jockey Club.
runners.comment: Showed good form as a 2yo and shaped okay on recent return
runners.owner: Guy Pariente Holding, G Augustin-Normand & Ecurie Friedrider

runners.name: Lord Clover
runners.spotlight: Made a big impression on debut, winning by 8l at this venue on the AW before finishing runner-up on soft ground here over a mile behind another talented rival; a furlong further will be no issue on pedigree, dam was third in the 1m2f Prix de l'Opera, and faster surface could see more improvement on first venture into Group company; entered in the St James's Palace Stakes and both the Poulains plus Prix du Jockey Club.
runners.comment: Bred to be a Group horse and shown plenty of promise so far; interesting

runners.name: Mr Lope Cen
runners.spotlight: Half-brother to both Ramatuelle and Tito Mo Cen; made a winning debut on the AW here over a mile in March before finishing second at Longchamp in the middle of last month on good to soft; this demands more but he is entered in the Poulains plus Prix du Jockey Club.
runners.comment: Well-bred colt, who has run well both times; first start in Group company

runners.name: Parade Bay
runners.spotlight: Only been seen once but showed the potential to be smart at Saint-Cloud last November (7.5f, heavy), full value for the 4l success, looking better the further he went; interesting Andre Fabre, who won this 12 months ago with Cualificar, puts him straight into a Group 3 for his return, and step up in trip unlikely to be an issue; entered in the Prix du Jockey Club and Grand Prix de Paris.
runners.comment: Looked a smart prospect when winning only run as 2yo; could be anything

races.race_name: Clem Murphy Memorial Irish EBF Mooresbridge Stakes (Group 2)

runners.name: Minnie Hauk
runners.spotlight: Last year's leading middle-distance 3yo filly; won the Cheshire Oaks, the Classics at Epsom and the Curragh and the Yorkshire Oaks, and at least matched her best effort when touched off by Daryz on soft in the Arc, well clear of the remainder; form significantly boosted before season's end; possibly one race too many when sixth in the Breeders' Cup Turf; 1m2f trip is a mild concern; won over 1m at two.
runners.comment: Dual Oaks winner, also won Yorkshire Oaks, second in Arc, 1m2f trip is a mild concern
runners.owner: Derrick Smith & Mrs John Magnier & Michael Tabor

runners.name: Shaool
runners.spotlight: Highly progressive last season, improving to the tune of 35lb between June and October; versatile in respect of both trip and ground, but best at form 1m2f on last two starts; won a 21-runner premier handicap here in October and a Listed race at Leopardstown in November; failed to make an impact on return in Naas event won by Beset.
runners.comment: Highly progressive at three, failed to make an impact on return in Naas event won by Beset

runners.name: Beset
runners.spotlight: Ended last season on a high with 1m4f Listed win at Naas in a race in which stablemate Starford flopped; maintained progression at Naas on return; beat all bar a restored-to-form Starford in a Group 3 over C&D; has developed into a dependable mare; best suited by easy ground; faces stiffest task yet.
runners.comment: Has developed into a dependable mare, best suited by easy ground, faces stiffest task yet
runners.owner: K Blake/Mrs E Palmer/M Wilson/N Luck

runners.name: Cercene
runners.spotlight: Third in the Irish 1,000 Guineas; sprang 33-1 surprise on quick ground in the Coronation Stakes at Royal Ascot; no match for the front-running Whirl on heavy ground over 1m2f in the Nassau, still a good effort as outsider of five; stayed on for fifth in the 1m Matron Stakes; rated 10lb behind Minnie Hauk.
runners.comment: Third in Irish 1,000 Guineas, 33-1 Coronation Stakes winner, rated 10lb behind Minnie Hauk

runners.name: Edward Hamilton
runners.spotlight: Has finished last in both races since Dundak maiden win; supporting role here.
runners.comment: Has finished last in both races since Dundalk maiden win, supporting role here
runners.owner: P M Brant & Mrs J Magnier & M Tabor & Derrick Smith

runners.name: Tiberius Thunder
runners.spotlight: Won at Dundalk before a creditable third behind Delacroix and Lambourn in last year's Ballysax Stakes; confirmed a good standard when fourth of 12 in the Hampton Court Stakes at Royal Ascot; ran better than finishing his position implies when eighth of nine in the Saratoga Derby; unsuited by soft ground in Naas event won by Beset.
runners.comment: Capable sort but mixed form at three, unsuited by soft ground in Naas event won by Beset
runners.owner: AMO Racing Limited & Giselle De Aguiar

runners.name: Adelaide River
runners.spotlight: Second in the Irish Derby and Grand Prix de Paris in 2023; failed to match that level in Australia until finishing a nose second to Birdman in a 1m2f Group 3 at Randwick last September; down the field in the Caulfield Cup on his final start; resumes his Irish career, now a gelding and housed with Joseph O'Brien.
runners.comment: 2023 Irish Derby second for Ballydoyle, back after a spell in Australia, best watched

races.race_name: Prix des Lilas (Listed Race) (Fillies) (Turf)
